declarations.description:
A single selectable action for [esp-action-menu](/components/action-menu).

Use the optional `icon` slot for a leading visual cue. Link-style
action items are intentionally not part of this API yet; use the
parent menu's `select` event and handle navigation in application
code until the href behavior is reviewed.

```html
<esp-action-menu>
  <esp-action-menu-item value="edit" icon="edit">
    Edit
  </esp-action-menu-item>
  <esp-action-menu-item value="settings" icon="cog">
    Settings
  </esp-action-menu-item>
  <esp-action-menu-item value="delete" icon="trash" disabled>
    Delete
  </esp-action-menu-item>
</esp-action-menu>
```
declarations.name: EspalierActionMenuItem

members.description:
Optional icon name from the configured Espalier SVG sprite.
Slotted `icon` content remains supported and overrides this
generated icon.

members.name: value

members.name: disabled
members.description: Prevents pointer and keyboard activation for this item.

members.name: iconPosition

Where the optional icon slot is positioned within the item.

Use `"right"` for Taproot-style action rows where the icon sits
in a differentiated trailing cell with a dotted divider.

members.name: inheritIconPosition
members.description: Applies the parent menu's icon position unless this item has its own override.

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

declarations.description:
A contextual overflow menu that combines a trigger button,
popover positioning, menu semantics, and roving focus for
row and card actions.

The default trigger is an icon-only [esp-button](/components/button)
showing an ellipsis. Use the `trigger` slot when a view needs a
different affordance, such as the square dropdown action used in
Taproot grids.

```html
<esp-action-menu id="demo-actions">
  <esp-action-menu-item value="edit" icon="edit">
    Edit
  </esp-action-menu-item>
  <esp-action-menu-item value="settings" icon="cog">
    Settings
  </esp-action-menu-item>
  <esp-action-menu-item value="pin" icon="pin">
    Pin
  </esp-action-menu-item>
  <esp-action-menu-item value="delete" icon="trash">
    Delete
  </esp-action-menu-item>
</esp-action-menu>
<script>
  findById("demo-actions").addEventListener("select", (event) => {
    showToast({
      message: `Selected ${event.detail.value}`,
      icon: "info-i",
      duration: 2
    });
  });
</script>
```

```html
<esp-action-menu placement="bottom-end">
  <esp-button slot="trigger" icon-only icon="select" aria-label="Row actions"></esp-button>
  <esp-action-menu-item value="edit">Edit</esp-action-menu-item>
  <esp-action-menu-item value="delete">Delete</esp-action-menu-item>
</esp-action-menu>
```

Use `icon-position="right"` for Taproot-style action menus with
trailing icons in a differentiated cell:

```html
<esp-action-menu icon-position="right">
  <esp-action-menu-item value="edit" icon="edit">
    Edit
  </esp-action-menu-item>
  <esp-action-menu-item value="delete" icon="trash">
    Delete
  </esp-action-menu-item>
</esp-action-menu>
```

Optional custom trigger element.

members.name: placement
members.description:
Preferred popover placement. Values map to the underlying
`esp-popover` side and alignment settings.

members.name: disabled
members.description: Prevents the trigger from opening the menu.

members.name: iconPosition
members.description:
Controls where item icons appear inside the menu.

`"left"` keeps icons before labels. `"right"` moves icons into
a trailing cell with a differentiated background and dotted divider.

members.name: openMenu
members.description: Opens the action menu popover when the trigger is enabled.

members.name: closeMenu
members.description:
Closes the action menu popover.

Set `restoreFocus` to return keyboard focus to the trigger.

declarations.description:
Inline profile summary that composes `esp-avatar` with a display name and optional secondary text. ```html
``` `esp-profile-chip` renders inline so it can sit inside buttons, menus, and navigation rows without adding its own interactive behavior. The composed avatar inherits `--esp-avatar-border-radius`, so set that property on `esp-profile-chip` to use the same avatar shape hook as a standalone `esp-avatar`.

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

declarations.description:
Used to display breadcrumb navigation. By default, the breadcrumbs
will be separated by right-arrows, with the final item having a
down-arrow after it.
[esp-breadcrumb](/components/breadcrumbs/breadcrumb) instances
should be placed in the default slot to build out the navigation.

```html
<esp-breadcrumbs>
  <esp-breadcrumb label="One" url="#"></esp-breadcrumb>
  <esp-breadcrumb label="Two" url="#"></esp-breadcrumb>
  <esp-breadcrumb label="Three" url="#"></esp-breadcrumb>
</esp-breadcrumbs>
```

Custom separators can be specified in the `<esp-breadcrumb>`:

```html
<esp-breadcrumbs>
  <esp-breadcrumb label="One" url="#"
    separator="<svg><use href='/assets/icons.svg#rocket' /></svg>">
  </esp-breadcrumb>
  <esp-breadcrumb label="Two" url="#"
    separator="<svg><use href='/assets/icons.svg#rocket' /></svg>">
  </esp-breadcrumb>
  <esp-breadcrumb label="Three" url="#"
    separator="<svg><use href='/assets/icons.svg#flower' /></svg>">
  </esp-breadcrumb>
</esp-breadcrumbs>
```

It defaults to the semantic heading color so navigation structure stays visible in light and dark themes. ```html <style> esp-breadcrumbs#custom-crumb { --esp-breadcrumb-separator-color: red; } </style> <esp-breadcrumbs id="custom-crumb"> <esp-breadcrumb label="One" url="#" separator="<svg><use href='/assets/icons.svg#rocket' /></svg>"> </esp-breadcrumb> <esp-breadcrumb label="Two" url="#" separator="<svg><use href='/assets/icons.svg#rocket' /></svg>"> </esp-breadcrumb> <esp-breadcrumb label="Three" url="#" separator="<svg><use href='/assets/icons.svg#flower' /></svg>"> </esp-breadcrumb> </esp-breadcrumbs> ```

declarations.description:
Groups related buttons into a visually connected segmented control.

Child `esp-button` elements lose their individual border-radius and
merge into a continuous strip; the group container provides the outer
rounded corners and border.

```html
<esp-button-group label="Actions">
  <esp-button icon-only icon="save" aria-label="Save"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="edit" aria-label="Edit"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="trash" aria-label="Delete"></esp-button>
</esp-button-group>
```

Groups can also contain labelled buttons:

```html
<esp-button-group label="Navigation">
  <esp-button label="Back" icon="arrow-big-left" collapsed></esp-button>
  <esp-button label="Forward" icon="arrow-big-right" collapsed></esp-button>
</esp-button-group>
```

Buttons can be wrapped in `esp-tooltip` or `esp-popover`:

```html
<esp-button-group label="With tooltips">
  <esp-tooltip text="Save changes">
    <esp-button icon-only icon="save" aria-label="Save"></esp-button>
  </esp-tooltip>
  <esp-tooltip text="Edit item">
    <esp-button icon-only icon="edit" aria-label="Edit"></esp-button>
  </esp-tooltip>
  <esp-tooltip text="Delete item">
    <esp-button icon-only icon="trash" aria-label="Delete"></esp-button>
  </esp-tooltip>
</esp-button-group>
```

When the group has a fixed width and contains more buttons than
can fit, horizontal scrolling kicks in:

```html
<esp-button-group label="Many actions" style="width: 360px;">
  <esp-button icon-only icon="save" aria-label="Save"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="edit" aria-label="Edit"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="upload" aria-label="Upload"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="pin" aria-label="Pin"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="cog" aria-label="Settings"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="user" aria-label="User"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="photo" aria-label="Photo"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="rocket" aria-label="Rocket"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="code" aria-label="Code"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="palette" aria-label="Palette"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="wand" aria-label="Wand"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="tags" aria-label="Tags"></esp-button>
</esp-button-group>
```

Add the `toolbar` attribute to opt into the WAI-ARIA toolbar
pattern. This changes how the group sits in the tab order: instead of
every button being its own tab stop, the whole group becomes a *single*
tab stop, and the arrow keys (plus `Home`/`End`) move focus between the
buttons inside it. Tabbing into the group lands on one button; the next
Tab leaves the group entirely. Use it when a group bundles many actions
(for example an editor formatting bar) so keyboard users do not have to
Tab through every button.

The `esp-input` elements below sit before and after the group so the
single-tab-stop behavior is easy to see: Tab from the first input lands
on one toolbar button, the arrow keys move between the buttons, and the
next Tab jumps straight to the second input.

```html
<esp-input value="Before"></esp-input>
<esp-button-group toolbar label="Document actions">
  <esp-button icon-only icon="save" aria-label="Save"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="edit" aria-label="Edit"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="upload" aria-label="Upload"></esp-button>
  <esp-button icon-only icon="trash" aria-label="Delete"></esp-button>
</esp-button-group>
<esp-input value="After"></esp-input>
```

When a button is wrapped in an `esp-tooltip`, the group sets
`trigger-tabindex="-1"` on that tooltip automatically so its wrapper span
does not reintroduce a tab stop the roving manager is removing:

```html
<esp-input value="Before"></esp-input>
<esp-button-group toolbar label="Document actions">
  <esp-tooltip text="Save">
    <esp-button icon-only icon="save" aria-label="Save"></esp-button>
  </esp-tooltip>
  <esp-tooltip text="Edit">
    <esp-button icon-only icon="edit" aria-label="Edit"></esp-button>
  </esp-tooltip>
  <esp-tooltip text="Upload">
    <esp-button icon-only icon="upload" aria-label="Upload"></esp-button>
  </esp-tooltip>
</esp-button-group>
<esp-input value="After"></esp-input>
```
declarations.name: EspalierButtonGroup

cssProperties.description: The border style between grouped buttons. Defaults to `1px dotted var(--esp-color-border)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-button-group-divider

slots.description: One or more `esp-button`, `esp-tooltip`, or `esp-popover` elements containing an `esp-button`.

members.name: label
members.description:
Accessible label for the group. Screen readers announce this
so users understand the purpose of the grouped buttons.

members.name: toolbar
members.description:
Opt into the WAI-ARIA toolbar pattern. When set, the group renders
with `role="toolbar"`, becomes a single tab stop (roving tabindex),
and the arrow keys, `Home`, and `End` move focus between the
enabled buttons. When unset, every button remains an independent
tab stop and behavior is unchanged.

members.name: activeButton
members.description: The button that currently holds the group's single tab stop.

members.name: managedButtons
members.description:
Buttons whose `tabindex` this group currently manages. Tracked so that a
button leaving the group (removed or moved elsewhere) has its roving
`tabindex` cleared instead of being stranded as untabbable.

members.name: managedTooltips
members.description:
`esp-tooltip` children whose `trigger-tabindex` this group currently
manages. In toolbar mode the group forces each tooltip wrapper out of the
tab order so it does not reintroduce a tab stop the roving manager is
removing. Tracked so a tooltip leaving the group (or toolbar mode turning
off) gets its `trigger-tabindex` cleared and its auto-detection restored.

members.name: stateObserver
members.description:
Watches slotted buttons for `disabled`/`loading` changes so the roving
tab stop can move off a button that becomes unusable (and onto one that
becomes usable). Always attached, but its callback (`applyRovingTabindex`)
is a no-op outside toolbar mode, so it stays inert until `toolbar` is set.

members.name: isEnabled
members.description:
A button is usable as a tab stop only when it is neither disabled nor
loading. `esp-button` disables its inner control while loading, so a
loading button must be skipped just like a disabled one.

members.name: getButtons
members.description:
The `esp-button` elements in the group, unwrapping any that are
nested in an `esp-tooltip` or used as an `esp-popover` target.
Mirrors the discovery logic in `handleSlotChange`.

members.name: getTooltips
members.description:
The direct `esp-tooltip` children of the group. These are the wrappers
whose `trigger-tabindex` the group manages in toolbar mode so their span
does not reintroduce a tab stop the roving manager is removing.

members.name: applyTabindexValues
members.description:
Push the roving `tabindex` (0 on the active button, -1 on the rest) and
clear any button that has left the group since the last pass so it does
not keep a stale `-1`/`0` and become untabbable elsewhere.

members.name: syncTooltipOptOut
members.description:
Force each tooltip-wrapped button's wrapper span out of the tab order by
setting `trigger-tabindex="-1"`, and clear any tooltip that has left the
group since the last pass so it does not keep a stale opt-out (its
auto-detection takes over again once it is no longer managed here).

members.name: applyRovingTabindex
members.description:
Establish or refresh the roving tabindex. Keeps the current active
button when it is still present and usable, otherwise falls back to the
first usable button. When no button is usable, every button is removed
from the tab order (-1) so disabled anchors do not stay focusable.
No-op when not in toolbar mode.

members.name: clearRovingTabindex
members.description:
Tear down the roving tabindex, restoring each button's natural
tabbability. Used when `toolbar` is toggled off so dynamic usage returns
to the default behavior.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description:
Accessible label for the group. Screen readers announce this
so users understand the purpose of the grouped buttons.

attributes.name: toolbar
attributes.description:
Opt into the WAI-ARIA toolbar pattern. When set, the group renders
with `role="toolbar"`, becomes a single tab stop (roving tabindex),
and the arrow keys, `Home`, and `End` move focus between the
enabled buttons. When unset, every button remains an independent
tab stop and behavior is unchanged.

slots.description: Optional custom SVG icon. Slotted icons override the generated SVG from the `icon` attribute. ```html <esp-button label="Profile" icon="user-circle"></esp-button> ```

members.name: writeHostTabindex
members.description: Write the host `tabindex` without re-triggering the forwarding logic.

members.name: removeAttribute
members.description:
Clear forwarding when the host `tabindex` is removed. Forwarding a
non-negative value strips the host attribute (so the inner control is the
sole tab stop), which means a later `removeAttribute("tabindex")` changes
nothing on the host and never reaches `attributeChangedCallback`. Catching
the removal here restores the inner control's natural tabbability even when
the host attribute was already absent. Removals we perform ourselves while
neutralizing are skipped via the guard.

members.name: focus
members.description:
Focus the button. Forwards focus (and any `FocusOptions`) to the inner
`<button>` (or the `<a>` when `href` is set).

members.name: buttonType
members.description:
Controls the [button type](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/button#type).
The available options are: `button`, `submit`, or `reset`.
The default value is `button`. To use a link instead of a
button, set the `href` attribute.

```html
<esp-button label="Default"></esp-button>
<esp-button button-type="submit" label="Submit"></esp-button>
<esp-button button-type="reset" label="Reset"></esp-button>
<esp-button href="#" label="Link"></esp-button>
```

members.name: collapsed
members.description:
By default, buttons take up the entire width of their parent
container. Use the `collapsed` attribute to shrink them to the
size of their content:

```html
<esp-button label="Full Width"></esp-button>
<esp-button collapsed label="Collapsed"></esp-button>
```

members.name: disabled
href
members.description:
If href is set, the button will be rendered as an anchor link
pointing to the href value instead of a button.

```html
<esp-button href="#" label="Link button"></esp-button>
```

members.name: iconOnly
members.description:
If true, the button only displays the icon.

```html
<esp-button icon-only icon="cog" aria-label="Settings"></esp-button>
```

members.name: incognito
members.description:
Renders the button with a borderless, transparent treatment for
quiet icon or inline actions.

```html
<esp-button incognito icon-only icon="edit" aria-label="Edit"></esp-button>
```

members.name: icon
members.description:
Optional icon name from the configured Espalier SVG sprite.
Slotted SVG content remains supported and overrides this value.

```html
<esp-button label="Settings" icon="cog"></esp-button>
```

members.name: label
members.description:
The label of the button.

```html
<esp-button label="Do something"></esp-button>
```

members.name: loading
target
members.description:
If the button is a link, this specifies the target browser window.

```html
<esp-button href="#" target="_blank" label="External link"></esp-button>
```

members.name: rel
members.description:
If the button is a link, this specifies the relationship
between the current page and the linked URL. Common values
include `"noopener noreferrer"` for external links opened
in a new tab.

```html
<esp-button href="#" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" label="External"></esp-button>
```

members.name: formNoValidate
members.description:
When true and `button-type` is `"submit"`, the enclosing
form is submitted without running constraint validation.

```html
<esp-button button-type="submit" formnovalidate label="Save Draft"></esp-button>
```

exists as an attribute for styling purposes.

parameters.name: selector

members.description:
Traverse up the DOM tree to find the closest element that

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

events.name: clicked
events.description: Emitted when the button is clicked. ```html <esp-button label="Click me!"></esp-button> <script> const theButton = findByTagName("esp-button")[0]; theButton.addEventListener("clicked", () => { showToast({ message: `Hi! You clicked the button. This message will self-destruct in 4 seconds...`, icon: "info-i", duration: 4 }); }); </script> ```

declarations.description:
Groups multiple [esp-checkbox](/components/checkbox)
components and manages their collective state. Works with
[esp-form-item](/components/form-item) for label and
error message integration.

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-form-item label="Select toppings">
    <esp-checkbox-group>
      <esp-checkbox value="cheese">Cheese</esp-checkbox>
      <esp-checkbox value="pepperoni">Pepperoni</esp-checkbox>
      <esp-checkbox value="mushrooms">Mushrooms</esp-checkbox>
    </esp-checkbox-group>
  </esp-form-item>
</esp-box>
```

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-form-item label="Disabled group">
    <esp-checkbox-group disabled>
      <esp-checkbox value="a">Option A</esp-checkbox>
      <esp-checkbox value="b" checked>Option B</esp-checkbox>
      <esp-checkbox value="c">Option C</esp-checkbox>
    </esp-checkbox-group>
  </esp-form-item>
</esp-box>
```
declarations.name: EspalierCheckboxGroup

slots.description: Place `esp-checkbox` elements in the default slot.

members.name: formCtrl
members.default: new FormFieldController({ host: this, internals: this.internals, getFormValue: () => { const v = this.values; return v.length > 0 ? v.join(",") : null; }, getValidity: () => { if (this.required && this.values.length === 0) { return { flags: { valueMissing: true }, message: this.requiredMessage || "Please select at least one option.", }; } return null; }, onReset: () => { for (const cb of this.getCheckboxes()) { cb.checked = false; } }, onRestore: (state: string) => { const selected = state.split(","); for (const cb of this.getCheckboxes()) { cb.checked = selected.includes(cb.value); } }, onDisabled: (isDisabled: boolean) => { this.disabled = isDisabled; }, })

declarations.description:
A checkbox control for toggling boolean values. Uses Tabler
SVG icons for checked, unchecked, and indeterminate states.
Works standalone or inside an
[esp-checkbox-group](/components/checkbox/group) for multi-select.

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-form-item label="Terms and conditions">
    <esp-checkbox value="agree">
      I agree to the terms
    </esp-checkbox>
  </esp-form-item>
</esp-box>
```

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-checkbox checked value="newsletter">
    Subscribe to newsletter
  </esp-checkbox>
  <esp-checkbox disabled value="disabled-opt">
    Disabled option
  </esp-checkbox>
  <esp-checkbox indeterminate value="partial">
    Indeterminate state
  </esp-checkbox>
</esp-box>
```

pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

declarations.description:
Used to choose a seed color for the Espalier color scheme.
Espalier uses the [OKLCH](/guides/color/oklch) color notation.
By default this picker lets you choose hue and chroma while
lightness stays at 0.7 (the design-system default) so variant
derivation stays consistent. Set `mode="hcl"` when an editor
needs to tune hue, chroma, and lightness directly.
declarations.name: EspalierColorPicker

members.name: mode
members.description:
Which controls to show. `hc` preserves the original hue/chroma UI; `hcl` also renders a lightness slider. ```html

Email brand color

 ```

members.name: chroma
members.description: The selected chroma (0-25)

members.name: hue
members.description:
The selected hue (0-360) ```html

A color picker with purple selected

 ```

members.name: lightness
members.description:
The selected OKLCH lightness as a percentage (0-100).

The emitted `seedColor` converts this value to OKLCH's 0-1
lightness scale.

type.text: CustomEvent<{ seedColor: string, hue: number, chroma: number, lightness: number }>

events.description: Fired when the user changes the color. The event detail contains the seed color OKLCH string, plus raw hue, chroma, and lightness values. ```html <esp-box> <esp-color-picker></esp-color-picker> </esp-box> <script> const espRoot = document.getElementsByTagName("esp-root")[0]; const stored = localStorage.getItem("esp-seed-color") ?? "oklch(0.7 0.125 216)"; const colorPicker = findByTagName("esp-color-picker")[0]; // Parse stored seed-color to set sliders const parts = stored.match(/oklch\(([\d.]+)\s+([\d.]+)\s+([\d.]+)\)/); if (parts) { colorPicker.hue = +parts[3]; colorPicker.chroma = Math.round(+parts[2] * 100); } colorPicker.addEventListener("value-changed", (event) => { const { seedColor } = event.detail; localStorage.setItem("esp-seed-color", seedColor); espRoot.setAttribute("seed-color", seedColor); }); </script> ```

declarations.description:
A date / datetime / time / range picker built on the browser-native [Temporal API](https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/docs/) and the native Popover API. It participates in native `
` elements via `ElementInternals`, so its value is submitted with the form just like a built-in ``. Wrap the picker in an [esp-form-item](/components/form-item) for a label, validation messages, and consistent spacing with other Espalier form controls. ```html ``` The picker supports four modes. Use the `mode` attribute to switch between a single date, a date-and-time, a time-only, or a date range: ```html ``` For date range selection the value uses ISO 8601 interval notation (`start/end`). Click once to set the start, then click again to set the end. If the second click is before the first, the dates are automatically swapped: ```html ``` Constrain the selectable range with `min` and `max`. Dates outside the range are greyed out and cannot be selected. The picker also reports `rangeUnderflow` / `rangeOverflow` through the Constraint Validation API: ```html ``` Because the picker is form-associated, its value is included when the form is submitted and it responds to form resets: ```html
``` The Temporal API must be available at runtime. If your target browsers do not yet ship it natively, load a polyfill such as temporal-polyfill in your document <head> before using this component.
declarations.name: EspalierDatePicker

members.name: formCtrl
members.default: new FormFieldController({ host: this, internals: this.internals, getFormValue: () => this._value || null, getValidity: () => this._getValidityResult(), getValidationAnchor: (): HTMLElement => this.inputRef.value ?? this, onReset: () => { this._rangeAnchor = null; this._rangePreview = null; this._value = ""; this.requestUpdate("value", ""); this._syncFromValue(); }, onRestore: (state: string) => { this.value = state; }, onDisabled: (isDisabled: boolean) => { this.disabled = isDisabled; }, })

members.name: formItemDescription
members.default: new FormFieldDescriptionController({ host: this, getTarget: () => this.inputRef.value, })

members.name: popoverCtrl
members.default: new PopoverController({ host: this, closeStrategy: "source-identity", isOpen: () => this.open, onShouldClose: () => this._closePopover(), onPositionUpdate: () => this._positionPopover(), shouldContinueTracking: () => { const r = this.getBoundingClientRect(); const vpH = window.innerHeight || document.documentElement.clientHeight; return !(r.bottom < 0 || r.top > vpH); }, getInsideElements: () => [this.popoverRef.value ?? null], onOutsideClick: () => this._closePopover(), })

members.name: mode
members.description:
The picker mode.

| Value      | Behavior                              |
|------------|----------------------------------------|
| `date`     | Pick a single date                     |
| `datetime` | Pick a date and time                   |
| `time`     | Pick a time only                       |
| `range`    | Pick a start and end date              |

```html
<esp-form-item label="Start time">
  <esp-date-picker mode="time"></esp-date-picker>
</esp-form-item>

<esp-form-item label="Trip dates">
  <esp-date-picker mode="range"></esp-date-picker>
</esp-form-item>
```

members.name: value
members.description:
ISO 8601 value string. The format depends on the current `mode`:

| Mode       | Format                                  |
|------------|-----------------------------------------|
| `date`     | `YYYY-MM-DD`                            |
| `datetime` | `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss`                   |
| `time`     | `HH:mm` or `HH:mm:ss`                   |
| `range`    | `YYYY-MM-DD/YYYY-MM-DD` (ISO 8601)      |

```html
<esp-date-picker value="2025-09-01"></esp-date-picker>
<esp-date-picker mode="datetime" value="2025-09-01T14:30:00"></esp-date-picker>
<esp-date-picker mode="range" value="2025-07-01/2025-07-14"></esp-date-picker>
```

members.name: placeholder
members.description:
Placeholder text shown when no value is set.

```html
<esp-date-picker placeholder="Pick a date…"></esp-date-picker>
```

members.name: min
members.description:
The minimum selectable date as an ISO 8601 date string. Dates
before this value are greyed out and cannot be selected. When the
current value is earlier than `min`, the picker reports a
`rangeUnderflow` validity error.

```html
<esp-date-picker id="min-example"></esp-date-picker>
<script>
  const picker = document.getElementById("min-example");
  picker.min = Temporal.Now.plainDateISO().toString();
</script>
```

members.name: max
members.description:
The maximum selectable date as an ISO 8601 date string. Dates
after this value are greyed out and cannot be selected. When the
current value is later than `max`, the picker reports a
`rangeOverflow` validity error.

```html
<esp-date-picker id="max-example"></esp-date-picker>
<script>
  const picker = document.getElementById("max-example");
  picker.max = Temporal.Now.plainDateISO().add({ weeks: 2 }).toString();
</script>
```

members.name: disabled
members.description:
Disables the picker. When `true`, the trigger button cannot be
clicked and the popover will not open. The picker also sets
`aria-disabled` on its internals.

```html
<esp-date-picker disabled></esp-date-picker>
```

members.name: name
members.description:
The name used when the picker participates in a `<form>`.
The selected value is submitted under this name.

```html
<form>
  <esp-date-picker name="departure" value="2025-08-15"></esp-date-picker>
</form>
```

members.name: required
members.description:
Makes the picker required for form validation. When `true` and
no value is set, the picker reports a `valueMissing` validity
error through the Constraint Validation API.

```html
<esp-form-item label="Check-in date">
  <esp-date-picker name="checkin" required></esp-date-picker>
</esp-form-item>
```

members.name: requiredMessage
members.description:
A custom message to display when the picker is required but no
date is selected. Defaults to `"Please select a date."` when
not set.

```html
<esp-date-picker required required-message="A departure date is required."></esp-date-picker>
```

members.name: viewDate
members.description: The month currently visible in the calendar grid.

members.name: focusedDate
members.description: The date that has keyboard focus inside the grid.

members.name: open
members.description: Whether the popup calendar is open.

members.name: parsed
members.description: Cached parsed representation of `value`.

members.name: _hour
members.description: Time-picker state: selected hour (1–12).

members.name: _minute
members.description: Time-picker state: selected minute (0–59).

members.name: _period
members.description: Time-picker state: AM / PM.

members.name: _rangeAnchor
members.description:
The range start date held during the two-click selection flow.
Separate from `parsed` so we can track the intermediate
"start-only" state that isn't a valid range value.

members.name: _rangePreview
members.description:
The date the user is currently hovering over while in
the `start` phase. Drives the preview highlight in the grid.

members.name: _rangePhase
members.description: Current range selection phase.

members.name: _getGrid
members.description:
Return a memoized calendar grid. Recalculates only when the
visible month, value, anchor, or preview changes.

members.name: _valueOnOpen
members.description:
Snapshot of the value when the popover was opened, so Cancel
can revert to it.

members.name: _touchGesture
members.description:
Which gesture is currently active:
- `"none"` — no gesture in progress
- `"pending"` — touch started, waiting to determine intent
- `"swipe"` — horizontal swipe to change month
- `"drag"` — long-press drag to select range end date

members.name: _swipeAnimating
members.description: Whether a swipe slide animation is in progress.

members.name: _longPressTimer
members.description: Long-press timer ID.

members.name: _edgeScrollTimer
members.description: Auto-scroll timer for edge month navigation during drag.

members.name: LONG_PRESS_MS
members.description: Delay (ms) before a held touch becomes a long-press drag.

members.name: EDGE_SCROLL_MS
members.description: Delay (ms) before edge-dwell navigates to the adjacent month.

members.name: _dateAtPoint
members.description:
Find the day cell under a touch point via `elementsFromPoint`,
walking into shadow roots. Returns the `PlainDate` from the
`data-date` attribute, or `null`.

members.name: _edgeSide
members.description:
Check if a touch point is near the left or right edge of the
calendar. Returns `"left"`, `"right"`, or `null`. The touchable
boundary is one day-cell width from each edge.

members.name: _handleEdgeScroll
members.description:
During a drag, if the finger is near the left or right edge
of the calendar, auto-navigate to the adjacent month after
one second so the user can extend the range.

members.name: _resetTouchAction
members.description: Remove the `touch-action: none` override so the browser can scroll normally again.

members.name: focus
members.description: Focus the date picker's text input.

members.name: validate
members.description: Re-run constraint validation and dispatch `validity-changed`.

members.name: checkValidity
members.description: Check whether the current state is valid (delegates to ElementInternals).

members.name: formResetCallback
members.description: Called by the browser when the owning `<form>` is reset.

members.name: formStateRestoreCallback
members.description: Called by the browser to restore form state (bfcache, etc.).

members.name: formDisabledCallback
members.description: Called by the browser when a parent `<fieldset>` is enabled or disabled.

members.name: _getValidityResult
members.description:
Return the current validity state for the controller.
Returns `null` when valid, or `{ flags, message }` when invalid.

members.name: _getValidationDates
members.description:
Extract the PlainDate value(s) that should be checked against
min/max.

members.name: _moveFocus
members.description: Move focusedDate by a number of days; adjust viewDate if needed.

members.name: _selectRangeEndpoint
members.description:
Range selection state machine.

| Current phase | Action                                          |
|---------------|-------------------------------------------------|
| `empty`       | Set anchor → `start`.                           |
| `start`       | Commit range (auto-flip if needed) → `complete`. |
| `complete`    | Re-start: set anchor → `start`.                 |

members.name: _showTimePicker
members.description: Whether the current mode should show a time picker.

members.name: _showCalendar
members.description: Whether the current mode should show a calendar grid.

members.name: _commitTime
members.description: Convert 12h + period into 24h and commit the time value.

members.name: _shrinkToFitViewport
members.description:
If the popover is wider than the viewport, iteratively reduce
its font-size until it fits. Each step scales down by 0.5px,
stopping at a minimum of 8px to keep the calendar legible.

members.name: _shrinkTimePickerToFit
members.description:
If the time picker row is wider than the popover (driven by the
calendar grid's natural width), reduce the time picker's font-size
until everything fits on one line without wrapping.

members.name: _scrollToContainPopover
members.description:
If the popover overflows the viewport, scroll the page so the
trigger field (and therefore the popover anchored to it) is
fully visible.

members.name: _handleGridKeyDown
members.description:
Full keyboard handler for the calendar grid following the
WAI-ARIA Grid / Date Picker pattern.

| Key               | Action                                    |
|-------------------|-------------------------------------------|
| `ArrowLeft`       | Previous day                              |
| `ArrowRight`      | Next day                                  |
| `ArrowUp`         | Same weekday, previous week               |
| `ArrowDown`       | Same weekday, next week                   |
| `Home`            | First day of the current week (Monday)    |
| `End`             | Last day of the current week (Sunday)     |
| `PageUp`          | Same day, previous month (Shift: prev yr) |
| `PageDown`        | Same day, next month (Shift: next yr)     |
| `Enter` / `Space` | Select focusedDate                        |
| `Escape`          | Close popup, return focus to trigger       |

members.name: _onDayCellHover
members.description:
Called on `pointerenter` for each day cell when in range mode
during the `start` phase.

  picker.min = Temporal.Now.plainDateISO().toString();
</script>
```

attributes.name: max
attributes.description:
The maximum selectable date as an ISO 8601 date string. Dates
after this value are greyed out and cannot be selected. When the
current value is later than `max`, the picker reports a
`rangeOverflow` validity error.

```html
<esp-date-picker id="max-example"></esp-date-picker>
<script>
  const picker = document.getElementById("max-example");
  picker.max = Temporal.Now.plainDateISO().add({ weeks: 2 }).toString();
</script>
```

attributes.name: disabled
attributes.description:
Disables the picker. When `true`, the trigger button cannot be
clicked and the popover will not open. The picker also sets
`aria-disabled` on its internals.

```html
<esp-date-picker disabled></esp-date-picker>
```

attributes.name: name
attributes.description:
The name used when the picker participates in a `<form>`.
The selected value is submitted under this name.

```html
<form>
  <esp-date-picker name="departure" value="2025-08-15"></esp-date-picker>
</form>
```

attributes.name: required
attributes.description:
Makes the picker required for form validation. When `true` and
no value is set, the picker reports a `valueMissing` validity
error through the Constraint Validation API.

```html
<esp-form-item label="Check-in date">
  <esp-date-picker name="checkin" required></esp-date-picker>
</esp-form-item>
```

attributes.name: required-message
attributes.description:
A custom message to display when the picker is required but no
date is selected. Defaults to `"Please select a date."` when
not set.

```html
<esp-date-picker required required-message="A departure date is required."></esp-date-picker>
```

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

Fired when the details element is opened or closed. ```html

Watch the console for toggle events.

 ``` ### Scroll-stability regression harness ```html Manual verification (Firefox):
  1. Scroll so the details summary and top of the grid are both visible.
  2. Open and close the details panel several times — the window should stay put.
  3. Leave the panel open and click a sortable grid header — the window should not jump.
  4. Toggle a checkbox, click Apply, and repeat the sort — the viewport should remain stable.
↑ Spacer — scroll down to reach the filter panel ↑
↓ Spacer — extra page length below the grid ↓
```

declarations.description:
Used to focus content modally. By default, on large screens the
dialog content is centered horizontally near the top of the
screen. On small screens, the content always tries to take up
the full screen width.

When opened, the dialog:
- Moves focus to the first `[autofocus]` element inside the slotted
  content, then the first focusable element (or the dialog container
  itself if none is found).
- Traps focus so that `Tab` and `Shift+Tab` cycle only through
  elements inside the dialog.
- Closes when the user presses `Escape`.
- Marks all sibling content as `inert` to prevent screen reader
  and keyboard access to the page behind the overlay.
- Restores focus to the element that was focused before the
  dialog was opened.
declarations.name: EspalierDialog

cssProperties.description: The background color of the dialog overlay.
cssProperties.name: --esp-color-dialog-bg

slots.description:
Place content to show modally in the default slot. ```html

Showing something

There could be controls or a confirmation or anything here.

 ``` Dialogs work well as containers for complex forms. Picker menus, inputs, and other interactive controls render correctly inside the dialog overlay. ```html

Add Item

 ```

members.name: _overlay
members.default: new OverlayController({ host: this, getFocusTrapContainer: () => this.vellumRef.value ?? null, getFocusFallback: () => this.vellumRef.value ?? null, })

members.name: _closeReason
members.description:
Tracks why the dialog is closing so lifecycle events can
report it. Reset to `"api"` after each close cycle.

members.name: _lifecycleToken
members.description:
Monotonic token used to invalidate async open/close lifecycle
callbacks from earlier toggle cycles.

members.name: fullScreen
toggleOpen
members.description:
Show or hide the dialog content. When opening, focus moves
into the dialog and sibling content is marked `inert`. When
closing, focus is restored to the element that was focused
before the dialog opened.

Dispatches lifecycle events:
- `esp-dialog-opened` after the dialog is fully rendered and
  focus has moved in.
- `esp-dialog-closing` (cancelable) before the close begins.
- `esp-dialog-closed` after the close completes.

members.name: _moveFocusIntoDialog
members.description:
Move focus to the first autofocus element inside the slotted
content, then the first focusable element. If no focusable
element is found, focus the vellum container itself (which has
`tabindex="-1"`).

events.name: esp-dialog-opened
events.description: Fired after the dialog has been promoted to its final DOM position, rendered, and focus has moved in. Safe to initialize third-party widgets (e.g. Stripe Elements) or measure layout at this point.

esp-dialog-closing - Fired before the dialog begins closing. Call `event.preventDefault()` to cancel the close (e.g. for unsaved-changes confirmation). The `reason` indicates what triggered the close: `"escape"` for the Escape key, `"close-dialog"` for a `closeDialog` event from slotted content (typically `<esp-form method="dialog">`), or `"api"` for a direct `toggleOpen()` call.
esp-dialog-closed - Fired after the dialog has fully closed, inert state has been restored, and focus has returned to the previously focused element. Safe to tear down resources.

Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

`image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp`

slots.description: The flyout's content.

members.name: open
members.description:
Whether the flyout is open. Reflected so the initial state can
be declared in markup; `esp-page` mirrors it (via the lifecycle
events and the slot) onto its own `flyout-open` attribute to
drive the grid.

members.name: heading
members.description:
Heading text for the panel's header row. Also becomes the
flyout's accessible name unless the author set an `aria-label`.
An open overlay without either uses the fallback name "Flyout" so
its dialog is never exposed without an accessible name.

members.name: mode
members.description:
Placement behavior.

- `"auto"` (default) — the page's placement ladder: gutter,
  docked sidebar, then overlay drawer below the `50em` viewport
  threshold.
- `"overlay"` — always the fixed overlay drawer, at any width.
  The page never reserves a grid track for an overlay-mode
  flyout.

members.name: anchor
members.description:
Triggering element whose block-start edge the in-grid panel should
align with. Anchored panels stay in normal page flow, shift upward
only while needed to fit the visible scrollport, and return to their
natural alignment as the page scrolls. Oversized content scrolls
inside the panel. Overlay drawers ignore this geometry and remain
fixed to the viewport.

members.name: matchSurface
members.description:
When set on a flyout slotted into an `esp-page`, the panel lifts
its **trailing** edge with the page's surface edge shadow, so it
reads as a raised peer of the content surface it flies out from
rather than the shadowless tear-off. The **leading** edge keeps
its dotted perforation and casts no shadow, so the panel stays
attached at the tear line instead of floating over the content.
The trailing-corner radius is still `--esp-flyout-radius`. No
effect on a standalone flyout — there is no page surface to match.

members.name: standalone
members.description:
Whether this flyout ignores the shared `showFlyout()` /
`closeFlyout()` bus and is driven only through its own
`show()` / `close()` / `toggle()` API.

By default a flyout services the bus, so a single flyout in a
page "just works" as the target of `showFlyout()` — the same
zero-config model as `<esp-toaster>` and `showToast()`. Because
the bus is a global broadcast, **every** listening flyout on the
page answers a `showFlyout()` call; set `standalone` on any
flyout you drive directly so only your one designated help
surface responds.

members.name: returnFocusTo
members.description:
Element to return focus to when the flyout closes. In the in-grid
modes it is focused only if focus is inside the flyout at close;
in the overlay-drawer mode it overrides the modal treatment's
automatic restore-to-opener. Usually supplied through
`showFlyout({ returnFocusTo })`.

members.name: overlayReturnFocusTo
members.description:
`returnFocusTo` captured at close time for the overlay teardown,
which runs after `close()` (in `updated()`): the modal treatment
restores focus to the pre-open element, and this explicit target
must win over that.

members.name: lastGeneratedAriaLabel
members.description:
The last `aria-label` this component generated from `heading` or
the overlay fallback, or `null` if none. The flyout only rewrites
`aria-label` when the current value is absent or still equals what
it last generated, so a label the author sets at any time — in
markup or later — wins.

members.name: busSubscribed
members.description:
Whether the bus handlers are currently registered. EspBus does
not dedupe subscriptions, so this guards against double-adds.

members.name: overlay
members.default: new OverlayController({ host: this, getFocusTrapContainer: () => this.panelRef.value ?? null, getFocusFallback: () => this.panelRef.value ?? null, promote: false, })
members.description:
Modal treatment for the overlay-drawer presentation only. It does
not promote out of the page slot, so the flyout keeps its grid
placement and `flyout-state-changed` sync; inert anchors on the
flyout's own position, making its page siblings inert.

members.name: overlayMediaQuery
members.description: Media query matching the overlay-drawer viewport range.

members.name: overlayActive
members.description: Whether the modal (overlay) treatment is currently applied.

members.name: baseRole
members.description:
The landmark role to restore when leaving the overlay treatment
(the author's role, or the default `complementary`).

members.name: effectiveOverlay
members.description:
Whether the flyout is presenting as an overlay drawer — either
forced via `mode="overlay"` or below the mobile threshold. The
modal a11y treatment applies exactly in this state; the in-grid
modes stay a non-modal `complementary` landmark.

members.name: syncBusSubscription
members.description:
Subscribe to the shared bus unless `standalone`; idempotent so
it is safe to call from both connect and a `standalone` change.

members.name: show
members.description:
Open the flyout with its current content. In the in-grid modes it
does not move focus; in the overlay-drawer mode it moves focus
into the drawer as a modal.

members.name: close
members.description:
Close the flyout. In the in-grid modes, if focus is inside the
flyout it is returned to `returnFocusTo` when one was provided; in
the overlay-drawer mode the modal treatment restores focus to
wherever it was when the drawer opened, unless `returnFocusTo`
names an explicit target — the explicit target wins.

members.name: toggle
members.description: Toggle the flyout between open and closed.

members.name: handleShowRequest
members.description:
Service a `"show-flyout"` bus request: swap in the requested
heading/content (never stacking) and open.

members.name: syncAnchorGeometry
members.description:
Position an anchored in-grid flyout at the trigger's natural
block-start, then shift it upward only enough to keep its bottom in
the visible scrollport. The shift is recalculated during scrolling,
so it returns to zero when the panel fits at its natural top.

members.name: getVisibleBlockBounds
members.description:
Intersect the visual viewport with every scrolling ancestor. This
keeps the flyout usable both on the document and inside a bounded
live-example pane such as WTFM's demo container.

members.name: syncOverlayModal
members.description:
Apply modal a11y exactly when the flyout is open **and** presenting
as an overlay drawer, and remove it otherwise. Called on open/mode
changes and when the viewport crosses the overlay threshold.

Modal treatment = background inert, scroll lock, `role="dialog"` +
`aria-modal`, focus moved into the drawer and trapped, and focus
restored on teardown — all via the (non-promoting) Overlay
controller, so the flyout never leaves its page slot. The in-grid
modes stay a non-modal `complementary` landmark with no focus
management.

members.name: updateGeneratedAriaLabel
members.description:
Mirror `heading` into `aria-label`, or apply a stable fallback
while this is an overlay dialog, without clobbering a label the
author controls (see lastGeneratedAriaLabel).

members.name: variantTokenOriginalValues
members.default: new Map< string, { generatedPriority: string; generatedValue: string; originalPriority: string; originalValue: string | null; } >()

members.name: scheme
members.description:
The scheme to use for the component. This property is used internally
to control the light/dark scheme so it matches the scheme of
[esp-root](/components/root). It should not be set manually, it
exists as an attribute for styling purposes.

members.name: variant
members.description: The [color variant](/guides/color/variants) of the element.

members.name: traverseToClosest

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: The selector of the element to look for

members.description:
Traverse up the DOM tree to find the closest element that
matches the selector. This method is aware of shadow DOM
boundaries and will traverse through them to find the element.

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a

palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary

events.name: flyout-opened
events.description: Fired by `show()` when the flyout opens.

events.name: flyout-closed
events.description: Fired by `close()` when the flyout closes. `reason` is `"escape"`, `"vellum"`, `"button"`, or `"programmatic"`.

events.name: flyout-state-changed
events.description: Fired on every `open`, `mode`, or `anchor` change, including direct property assignment (not just `show()`/`close()`). `esp-page` uses it to keep its layout in sync; most consumers want `flyout-opened`/`flyout-closed` instead.

declarations.name: WEB_SAFE_FONTS
declarations.default: [ { family: "Arial / Helvetica", stack: "Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif", category: "sans-serif", kind: "web-safe", }, { family: "Georgia / Times New Roman", stack: 'Georgia, "Times New Roman", Times, serif', category: "serif", kind: "web-safe", }, { family: "Verdana / Geneva", stack: "Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif", category: "sans-serif", kind: "web-safe", }, { family: "Tahoma / Geneva", stack: "Tahoma, Geneva, sans-serif", category: "sans-serif", kind: "web-safe", }, { family: "Trebuchet MS", stack: '"Trebuchet MS", Arial, sans-serif', category: "sans-serif", kind: "web-safe", }, { family: "Courier New", stack: '"Courier New", Courier, monospace', category: "monospace", kind: "web-safe", }, { family: "System Sans", stack: '-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif', category: "sans-serif", kind: "web-safe", }, { family: "System Serif", stack: 'ui-serif, Georgia, Cambria, "Times New Roman", Times, serif', category: "serif", kind: "web-safe", }, { family: "System Monospace", stack: 'ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", "Courier New", monospace', category: "monospace", kind: "web-safe", }, ]

declarations.name: getGoogleFonts
declarations.description:
Load the font catalog from the build-generated `font-definitions.json`
file served alongside the font CSS files.  The JSON is fetched from
`{fontCSSRoot}font-definitions.json` where `fontCSSRoot` comes from
the nearest `<esp-root>` element's `font-css-root` attribute.

Results are cached at the module level so only one fetch occurs per page.

declarations.name: previewFontFamily
declarations.description:
Build the CSS `font-family` value used by picker items.
Consumers should reference this when setting `styles.fontFamily`
on picker items to match the preview `@font-face` name.

Font picker component backed by the Google Fonts catalog by default.

The font catalog is loaded at runtime from a build-generated
`font-definitions.json` file served alongside the individual font CSS
files.  Run `npm run build-fonts` to generate both assets.

Set `font-source="web-safe"` for email editors or other places where
the picker should offer only CSS font stacks that do not need font
downloads.

**Required setup:**
1. Serve the `css/fonts/` directory under a public path.
2. Point `<esp-root font-css-root="/your/path/">` at that path.

```html
<esp-root font-css-root="/css/fonts/">
  <esp-form-item label="Pick a font">
    <esp-font-picker></esp-font-picker>
  </esp-form-item>
</esp-root>
```

```html
<esp-form-item label="Email font">
  <esp-font-picker font-source="web-safe"></esp-font-picker>
</esp-form-item>
```
declarations.name: EspalierFontPicker

members.name: _rangeGeneration
members.description:
Monotonically increasing counter used to detect stale
`handleItemsInView` invocations.  Each call bumps the
counter; after the async font-load loop the handler checks
whether a newer call has started and, if so, skips the
unload phase so it doesn't remove fonts that the newer call
just loaded.

members.name: _initGeneration
members.description:
Monotonically increasing counter used to detect stale `initFonts`
invocations when source/category changes while async catalog or
preview-font work is in flight.

members.name: _fontsReady
members.description:
Promise that resolves once the async work kicked off in
`firstUpdated` (font catalog fetch + initial CSS load)
has completed.  Surfaced through `getUpdateComplete()` so
that `await el.updateComplete` waits for the full init.

members.name: category
members.description:
Category of fonts to display in the picker. If not set, all font categories are displayed. ```html

Monospace Fonts

 ```

members.name: fontSource
members.description:
Source of fonts to display. `google` preserves the default Google Fonts catalog behavior. `web-safe` shows email-safe/system CSS font stacks and does not load Google font assets. ```html

 ```

members.name: value
members.description:
The selected font family or CSS font stack. In the default Google Fonts mode this is the font family name, such as `"Aclonica"`. In `font-source="web-safe"` mode this is the full CSS font stack, such as `"Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif"`, so pre-selection via the `value` attribute must use the stack value. ```html

Have Aclonica pre-selected

 ```

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

declarations.description:
A responsive site footer with structured spaces for branding, feature artwork, link groups, supporting actions, and legal or utility content. Its background remains full-bleed while its content automatically aligns to the surface of a containing [``](/components/page/). Use [``](/components/footer/link-group/) children for ordinary navigation columns. `columns` is a maximum: the grid always drops columns when they no longer fit. ```html Components API Getting started Styling

Themeable web components built on web standards.

Copyright 2026 Example Organization
 ``` A slotted brand replaces configured branding, while the media slot accepts a responsive `picture`, native image, or `esp-image`. Media spans the surface-aligned content well; use the decorative background layer for edge-to-edge artwork: ```html Components ``` Decorative art uses an image layer independent of the base color. The same API supports a repeating landscape band or one large cover image: ```html ``` Surface alignment is automatic inside `esp-page`; opt out only when the footer's content should also span the entire bar: ```html
Surface-aligned page content
Work About ```

declarations.description:
A form wrapper that renders a native `
` element in the light DOM. All form-associated custom elements placed inside participate natively in form submission, validation, and reset. `esp-form` has no visual presentation of its own — all styling, labels, and layout should be handled independently (e.g. via [esp-form-item](/components/form-item), `esp-box`, or plain CSS). ### Standard submission ```html ``` ### Fetch submission ```html ``` ### Dialog integration When `method="dialog"`, submitting the form dispatches a `closeDialog` event that `esp-dialog` listens for, closing the dialog without a network request. ### Multi-field form with validation Required fields are validated on submit. The first invalid field is scrolled into view and focused. Errors clear as the user corrects each field. Use the `required-message` attribute on any form control to customize the error text. ```html Yes, I accept
``` ### Skipping validation Add `formnovalidate` to a submit button to bypass constraint validation. This is useful for "Save Draft" buttons that should persist incomplete data. ```html
``` ### Handling the response Listen for `esp-submit-response` and `esp-submit-error` to react to the server's reply when using fetch submission. ```html ``` ### Form with diverse input types Combines email, telephone, number, and date inputs with validation and custom messages. ```html
```
declarations.name: EspalierForm

members.name: action
members.description:
The URL that processes the form submission. When `use-fetch`
is true this is the URL `fetch()` sends the request to.

members.name: method
members.description:
The HTTP method for submission. Standard values are `get`,
`post`, and `dialog`. When set to `dialog`, submitting the
form closes the nearest `esp-dialog` ancestor without making
a network request.

members.name: novalidate
members.description:
When true, constraint validation is skipped during
submission.

members.name: useFetch
members.description:
When true, the form uses `fetch()` instead of native
browser navigation for submission. An `esp-submit` event
is fired with the `FormData` in the detail, allowing
consumers to cancel or modify the request.

members.name: useJson
members.description:
When `use-fetch` is true and this is also true, the request
body is serialized as JSON instead of `FormData`.

members.name: enctype
members.description:
The encoding type for form submission. Maps to the native
`enctype` attribute on the inner `<form>`.

members.name: label
members.description:
An accessible label applied as `aria-label` on the inner
`<form>` element so the form is discoverable as an ARIA
`form` landmark by screen readers.

members.name: checkValidity
members.description:
Run constraint validation on all controls without showing
any UI feedback.

members.name: reportValidity
members.description:
Run constraint validation, display error messages via each
control's `esp-form-item`, and scroll the first invalid
item into view.

members.name: reset
members.description: Programmatically reset the form and all its controls.

members.name: submit
members.description: Programmatically trigger form submission (with validation).

events.name: closeDialog
events.description: Fired when `method="dialog"` is used and the form requests its containing `<esp-dialog>` to close. The event detail is an empty object.

events.name: esp-submit-response
events.description: Fired after a successful `fetch` submission.

events.name: esp-submit-error
events.description: Fired when a `fetch` submission fails.
Fired when `use-fetch` is true and the form passes validation. Cancelable; calling `preventDefault()` aborts the fetch.
events.name: esp-submit

declarations.description:
Wraps fields with label and error message functionality. For more
information about forms, read our
[guide work working with forms](/guides/forms).

```html
<style>
esp-box.demo-form::part(box) {
  display: grid;
  gap: var(--esp-size-tiny-to-small);
}
</style>
<esp-box class="demo-form">
  <esp-form-item label="Form item" autofocus>
    <esp-input></esp-input>
  </esp-form-item>
  <esp-button label="Form Things"></esp-button>
</esp-box>
```
