members.name: _errorSource
members.description:
Tracks the source that last set the error so different
mechanisms do not fight each other.

- `"native"` — set by the `validity-changed` or `invalid` event
- `"pool"` — set by the `errorPool` setter
- `"manual"` — set directly via the `error` property

members.name: label
members.description: Label text to display for the given form control.

members.name: autofocus
members.description:
When true, the first control where autofocus is true has
focus captured.

members.name: hint
members.description:
Persistent helper text shown with the field — the bottom rung of
the help ladder. Hints are static authored guidance such as an
expected format; validation messaging belongs to `error` and
`warning` and must not be routed through the hint. The hint stays
visible while an error or warning badge is showing, and is
announced to assistive technology as the field's description via
`aria-describedby`.

```html
<esp-box class="demo-form">
  <esp-form-item
    label="Employer ID"
    hint="Use the format XX-XXXXXXX.">
    <esp-input></esp-input>
  </esp-form-item>
  <esp-button label="Form Things"></esp-button>
</esp-box>
```

For rich hint content, slot markup into the `hint` slot instead;
it replaces the attribute text when present.

members.name: hintPlacement
members.description:
Where the hint renders. `below` (the default) places it directly
under the field, above the error/warning badge rows. `above`
places it between the label text and the field. Any other value
falls back to `below`.

```html
<esp-box class="demo-form">
  <esp-form-item
    label="Employer ID"
    hint="Use the format XX-XXXXXXX."
    hint-placement="above">
    <esp-input></esp-input>
  </esp-form-item>
  <esp-button label="Form Things"></esp-button>
</esp-box>
```

members.name: hintId
members.description:
Stable id for the hint text element, referenced by the slotted
field's `aria-describedby`.

members.name: managedHintEl
members.description:
The light-DOM span this component creates to carry the `hint`
attribute text. See `syncManagedHint` for why the text cannot
stay in the shadow root.

members.name: appliedHintTokens
members.description: The describedby tokens this component added on the last sync.

members.name: hintContentObserver
members.description: Keep a shadow-local mirror current when rich hint text changes.

members.name: hasHint
members.description: A hint exists when either the attribute or the slot provides one.

members.name: consumerHintElements
members.description: Consumer-provided `slot="hint"` elements (excluding the managed span).

members.name: error
members.description:
An error message to show for the given form control.

```html
<esp-box class="demo-form">
  <esp-form-item
    label="Form item"
    error="There's something wrong!">
    <esp-input></esp-input>
  </esp-form-item>
  <esp-button label="Form Things"></esp-button>
</esp-box>
```

members.name: _setError
members.description:
Central writer that keeps `_errorSource` in sync with every
error change — pool, native, and manual all go through here.

members.name: warning
members.description:
A warning message to show for the given form control.
Warning is hidden when an error is also present.

```html
<esp-box class="demo-form">
  <esp-form-item
    label="Form item"
    warning="This might not be right.">
    <esp-input></esp-input>
  </esp-form-item>
  <esp-button label="Form Things"></esp-button>
</esp-box>
```

members.name: fieldName
members.description:
Identifies this form item's field. Serves two purposes:
1. Filters errors from the bound `errorPool` to show only
   errors whose `fieldName` matches.
2. Propagates as the `name` attribute on the slotted form
   field, so there is no need to set `name` separately.

members.name: errorPool
members.description:
Bind to an array of `ValidationError` to automatically filter
and show errors whose `fieldName` matches this item's
`field-name` attribute.

```html
<esp-form-item
  label="Path"
  field-name="Path"
  .errorPool=${this.validationErrors}
>
  <esp-input .value=${this.pagePath}></esp-input>
</esp-form-item>
```

members.name: handleHintSlotChange
members.description: Re-evaluate hint presence when `hint` slot content changes.

members.name: observeConsumerHintContent
members.description: Watch rich hint text and ids without observing the managed fallback span.

members.name: syncManagedHint
members.description:
Keep the `hint` attribute text in the light DOM. An
`aria-describedby` IDREF on the slotted field cannot reach into
this component's shadow root (nor can ARIA element reflection —
browsers drop references that point down into a shadow root), so
the description text must live in the field's own tree. The
attribute text is projected through the `hint` slot via a span
this component owns; consumer-slotted hint content replaces the
attribute text entirely.

members.name: hintDescriptionIds
members.description:
Ids of the light-DOM elements carrying the hint text, assigning
generated ids to consumer elements that lack one. Consumer ids are
minted fresh from the module counter — never `hintId`, which is
reserved for the managed span so a consumer element that keeps a
generated id after it stops being a hint can never duplicate it.

members.name: hintDescriptionText
members.description: Plain text mirrored into an Espalier control's own shadow root.

members.name: syncFieldDescribedBy
members.description:
Mirror the hint onto the slotted field as its accessible
description, preserving any consumer-set `aria-describedby`
tokens (the same merge `esp-tooltip` performs on its trigger).

members.name: focus
members.description: Focus the assigned slotted field.

members.name: renderHint
members.description:
The hint renders inside the `<label>` when placed `above`, so it
is wrapped in `aria-hidden` there to keep it out of the field's
accessible-name computation — it stays announced as a description
because nodes directly referenced by `aria-describedby` are
included even when hidden.

attributes.name: label
attributes.description: Label text to display for the given form control.

attributes.name: autofocus
attributes.description:
When true, the first control where autofocus is true has
focus captured.

attributes.name: hint
attributes.description:
Persistent helper text shown with the field — the bottom rung of
the help ladder. Hints are static authored guidance such as an
expected format; validation messaging belongs to `error` and
`warning` and must not be routed through the hint. The hint stays
visible while an error or warning badge is showing, and is
announced to assistive technology as the field's description via
`aria-describedby`.

```html
<esp-box class="demo-form">
  <esp-form-item
    label="Employer ID"
    hint="Use the format XX-XXXXXXX.">
    <esp-input></esp-input>
  </esp-form-item>
  <esp-button label="Form Things"></esp-button>
</esp-box>
```

For rich hint content, slot markup into the `hint` slot instead;
it replaces the attribute text when present.

attributes.name: hint-placement
attributes.description:
Where the hint renders. `below` (the default) places it directly
under the field, above the error/warning badge rows. `above`
places it between the label text and the field. Any other value
falls back to `below`.

```html
<esp-box class="demo-form">
  <esp-form-item
    label="Employer ID"
    hint="Use the format XX-XXXXXXX."
    hint-placement="above">
    <esp-input></esp-input>
  </esp-form-item>
  <esp-button label="Form Things"></esp-button>
</esp-box>
```

attributes.name: error
attributes.description:
An error message to show for the given form control.

```html
<esp-box class="demo-form">
  <esp-form-item
    label="Form item"
    error="There's something wrong!">
    <esp-input></esp-input>
  </esp-form-item>
  <esp-button label="Form Things"></esp-button>
</esp-box>
```

attributes.name: warning
attributes.description:
A warning message to show for the given form control.
Warning is hidden when an error is also present.

```html
<esp-box class="demo-form">
  <esp-form-item
    label="Form item"
    warning="This might not be right.">
    <esp-input></esp-input>
  </esp-form-item>
  <esp-button label="Form Things"></esp-button>
</esp-box>
```

attributes.name: field-name
attributes.description:
Identifies this form item's field. Serves two purposes:
1. Filters errors from the bound `errorPool` to show only
   errors whose `fieldName` matches.
2. Propagates as the `name` attribute on the slotted form
   field, so there is no need to set `name` separately.

attributes.name: error-pool
attributes.description:
Bind to an array of `ValidationError` to automatically filter
and show errors whose `fieldName` matches this item's
`field-name` attribute.

```html
<esp-form-item
  label="Path"
  field-name="Path"
  .errorPool=${this.validationErrors}
>
  <esp-input .value=${this.pagePath}></esp-input>
</esp-form-item>
```

declarations.description:
Defines grid columns for the [esp-grid](/components/grid).
The grid pulls a JSON dataset, then uses column definitions to
map data to table cells.
declarations.name: EspalierGridColumn

slots.description: Grid column has a default slot for a template to use when rendering the row. For example, use data from three fields to build an email link, and add a button in another column: ```html ${first_name} ${last_name} ```

members.name: fieldName
members.description:
The name of the field in the dataset to get the value from.

```html
<esp-grid data-url="/assets/data/grid-data.json">
  <esp-grid-column field-name="first_name"></esp-grid-column>
</esp-grid>
```

members.name: header
members.description:
The header text of the grid column.

```html
<esp-grid data-url="/assets/data/grid-data.json">
  <esp-grid-column header="First name" field-name="first_name"></esp-grid-column>
</esp-grid>
```

members.name: sortable
members.description:
Whether or not the user can sort on this column.

```html
<esp-grid data-url="/assets/data/grid-data.json">
  <esp-grid-column header="First name" field-name="first_name" sortable></esp-grid-column>
</esp-grid>
```

members.name: sortField
members.description:
If the column is sortable it will sort on the `field-name`
by default. Define a `sort-field` to specify a different
field to sort on. Both these columns sort by last name:

```html
<esp-grid data-url="/assets/data/grid-data.json">
  <esp-grid-column header="First name" field-name="first_name" sort-field="last_name" sortable></esp-grid-column>
  <esp-grid-column header="Last name" field-name="last_name" sort-field="last_name" sortable></esp-grid-column>
</esp-grid>
```

members.name: sortOrder
members.description:
If sorting on this column, the direction to sort in. By default,
the grid will sort on the first column with a sort-order specified.

members.name: sortType
members.description:
Determines how to parse the value in the field for sort
comparisons.

```html
<esp-grid data-url="/assets/data/grid-data.json">
  <esp-grid-column header="Id" field-name="id" sortable sort-type="number"></esp-grid-column>
  <esp-grid-column header="First name" field-name="last_name" sort-column="last_name" sortable></esp-grid-column>
</esp-grid>
```

members.name: textAlign
members.description:
How to align text in the column.

```html
<esp-grid data-url="/assets/data/grid-data.json">
  <esp-grid-column text-align="right" header="Id" field-name="id" sortable sort-type="number"></esp-grid-column>
</esp-grid>
```

members.name: width
members.description:
The width of the grid column. Set to 0 for the column width to
be as small as the value inside will allow.

```html
<esp-grid data-url="/assets/data/grid-data.json">
  <esp-grid-column header="Id" field-name="id" width="0"></esp-grid-column>
  <esp-grid-column header="First name" field-name="last_name"></esp-grid-column>
</esp-grid>
```

members.name: pin
members.description:
Pin the column to the left or right side of the grid so it
remains visible while scrolling horizontally. Pinned columns
should have an explicit `width` set when multiple columns are
pinned to the same side, so the grid can compute proper offsets.

```html
<esp-grid data-url="/assets/data/grid-data.json">
  <esp-grid-column pin="left" width="0">
    <esp-button icon-only grid-event="action">
      <svg><use href="/assets/icons.svg#user-circle" /></svg>
    </esp-button>
  </esp-grid-column>
  <esp-grid-column header="Name" field-name="first_name"></esp-grid-column>
  <esp-grid-column header="Email" field-name="email"></esp-grid-column>
</esp-grid>
```

members.name: grow
members.description:
Allow the column to grow and fill remaining horizontal space.
The column keeps its declared `width` as a minimum but expands
to absorb any extra room. Multiple growable columns share the
extra space equally.

```html
<esp-grid data-url="/assets/data/grid-data.json">
  <esp-grid-column header="Id" field-name="id" width="60px"></esp-grid-column>
  <esp-grid-column header="Email" field-name="email" width="200px" grow></esp-grid-column>
</esp-grid>
```

attributes.name: field-name
attributes.description:
The name of the field in the dataset to get the value from.

```html

  <esp-grid-column field-name="first_name"></esp-grid-column>
</esp-grid>
```

declarations.name: GRID_LOAD_START_EVENT
declarations.default: "esp-grid-load-start"

declarations.name: GRID_LOAD_SUCCESS_EVENT
declarations.default: "esp-grid-load-success"

declarations.name: GRID_LOAD_ERROR_EVENT
declarations.default: "esp-grid-load-error"

declarations.name: GRID_ITEMS_CHANGED_EVENT
declarations.default: "esp-grid-items-changed"

declarations.description:
Renders data in a table. Specify grid column definitions by
adding [esp-grid-column](/components/grid/grid-column) elements to
the default slot.
declarations.name: EspalierGrid

cssProperties.description: Border style of the grid's outer edge. Defaults to `2px solid var(--esp-color-border)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-grid-border-outer

cssProperties.description: Border style of the grid's internal section separators. Defaults to `2px solid var(--esp-color-border)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-grid-border-inner

cssProperties.description: Background color of the grid container. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-layer-1)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-grid-background

cssProperties.description: Text color used by the grid. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-text)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-grid-text-color

cssProperties.description: Background color of header rows and pinned header cells. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-layer-4)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-grid-header-background

cssProperties.description: Background color used for sorted or hovered sortable headers. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-layer-3)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-grid-header-active-background

cssProperties.description: Background color of odd rows. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-layer-1)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-grid-row-background-odd

cssProperties.description: Background color of even rows. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-layer-2)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-grid-row-background-even

cssProperties.description: Background color of hovered rows. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-layer-3)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-grid-row-hover-background

cssProperties.description: Cell divider color used throughout the grid. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-layer-3)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-grid-cell-border-color

cssProperties.description: Divider color used while rows are hovered. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-layer-4)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-grid-row-hover-border-color

cssProperties.description: Link color used inside grid cells. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-link)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-grid-link-color

cssProperties.description: Link hover decoration color used inside grid cells. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-link-hover)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-grid-link-hover-color

cssProperties.description: Background color of the shared field shell. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-layer-2)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-background

cssProperties.description: Border color of the shared field shell. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-border)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-border-color

cssProperties.description: Border width of the shared field shell. Defaults to `1px`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-border-width

cssProperties.description: Text color used inside the shared field shell. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-text)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-text-color

cssProperties.description: Hover background color of the shared field shell. Derived from `--esp-field-background`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-hover-bg

cssProperties.description: Focus background color of the shared field shell. Derived from `--esp-field-background`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-focus-bg

cssProperties.description: Shadow color used for shared field focus treatment. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-shadow)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-focus-shadow

slots.description: Grid has a default slot that expects `` elements to define the columns in the grid: ```html ${first_name} ${last_name} Edit Settings Pin Delete ``` The grid also supports cursor-based infinite scrolling. Instead of fetching all data up front, provide a `fetchPage` callback that returns a page of items and a cursor for the next page. The grid virtualizes the rows and fetches more data as the user scrolls. Search and sort are delegated to the server via the callback parameters. Every column in an infinite-scroll grid must have an explicit `width` attribute. Use the `grow` attribute on one or more columns to fill remaining horizontal space when the grid is wider than its content. ```html ${first_name} ${last_name} ``` When the total number of items is smaller than the configured `grid-height`, the grid automatically shrinks to fit after data loads. This also works after sort, search, and reload — the grid re-measures and adjusts its height each time. ```html ``` The grid also regrows when a reload returns more items. In this example the grid starts with 1 row (compact), then clicking "Load more" replaces the data with 10 rows and the grid expands to fit. ```html ``` Columns can use a `.template` callback to render custom web components inside each cell. The grid explicitly waits for Lit-based custom elements (those exposing `updateComplete`) to finish rendering before measuring row heights. For other custom elements, a zero-height guard prevents the grid from collapsing if the element hasn't painted yet. In the example below, a `` custom element renders a two-line contact cell with the person's name and email. ```html ```
The header buttons slot is for adding buttons next to the search box at the top of the grid. Useful for things like an add item button: ```html ${first_name} ${last_name} ```
slots.name: header-buttons

members.name: dataField
members.description:
If there is a `data-url` specified, the name of the
field on the response to get the array of items from.
If it is empty, the API response should simply return
an array of data items.

members.name: dataUrl
members.description: Specify a URL the grid will fetch data from.

members.name: items
members.description:
If using a `data-url`, the items retrieved are available
in the items property. An items array can be assigned
instead of using a `data-url`.

Assigning a new items array preserves the grid's current
intrinsic state: an active search term is re-applied to the
new items, the active sort column re-sorts them, and the
current page is kept when it still exists (clamping to the
nearest valid page otherwise). Infinite grids delegate search
and sort to the server through `fetchPage` instead.

members.name: pageSize
members.description:
The number of items to display on a page. In paged mode this
controls how many rows are shown per page. In infinite scroll
mode this controls how many items are fetched per request.

members.name: gridHeight
members.description:
The height of the grid when in infinite scroll mode. Accepts
any valid CSS height value. Defaults to `60vh`.

If the loaded content is shorter than this value (and there
are no more pages to fetch), the grid shrinks to fit.

members.name: searchFields
members.description: The fields to include when filtering results. It defaults to an empty array, which includes all fields. The test dataset includes an address field that is not displayed, so to only filter results by fields displayed in the grid, specify those fields in the `search-fields` attribute. When specifying arrays in plain HTML, the attributes should be wrapped in single quotes. ```html ${first_name} ${last_name} ```

members.name: fetchPage
members.description:
Provide a callback to enable cursor-based infinite scrolling.
When set, the grid virtualizes rows and fetches pages on demand
as the user scrolls. Search and sort are delegated to the server
via the callback parameters.

members.name: isAdjustingHeight
members.description:
After data loads, sets the grid height appropriately:
- If more data is available, uses the full configured `gridHeight`.
- If all data is loaded and content is shorter than `gridHeight`,
  shrinks to fit.
- Waits for custom elements inside cells to finish rendering
  before measuring row heights.

members.name: delete
addOrReplace
addInOrder
members.description:
Inserts or replaces an item in sorted order. If an existing item
matches the `lookup` function, it is replaced in-place. Otherwise
the new item is inserted at the position determined by the
`compare` function (same semantics as `Array.prototype.sort`).

Returns the index of the inserted or replaced item, which can be
passed to scrollToIndex to scroll the item into view.

members.name: scrollToIndex
members.description:
Scrolls to the item at the given index.

In **infinite mode** this waits for the virtualizer to
complete its layout pass, then delegates to its
`scrollToIndex` method.

In **paged mode** this navigates to the page containing
the item at `index`.

members.name: clear
members.description: Clears all items from the grid.

members.name: reload
members.description:
Clears all loaded data and re-fetches from the beginning.

In **infinite mode** this resets the cursor and triggers a
fresh `fetchPage` call.  In **paged mode** this is a no-op
(use the `data-url` attribute to reload).

members.name: pinnedOffsets
members.description: Cached pinned offsets measured from the rendered header cells.

members.name: measurePinnedOffsets
members.description:
Computes sticky offsets for pinned columns.

In **paged mode** (native `<table>`), offsets are measured from
the rendered header cell widths since table layout may differ
from declared widths.

In **infinite mode** (CSS grid rows), offsets are computed from
the declared column `width` values using `calc()` so they
exactly match the grid track sizes — avoiding sub-pixel
mismatches that cause gaps between adjacent pinned columns.

members.name: computePinnedOffsetsFromWidths
members.description:
Computes pinned offsets from declared column widths using `calc()`
expressions. Used in infinite mode where CSS grid track sizes are
set from these widths directly.

members.name: measurePinnedOffsetsFromCells
members.description:
Measures pinned offsets from rendered header cell widths.
Used in paged mode where table layout determines final sizes.

members.name: computeGridTemplateColumns
members.description:
Computes a CSS grid-template-columns value from the column
definitions so all rows in infinite mode share aligned tracks.

Every column should have an explicit width. Widths are used
directly as CSS grid track sizes. Columns with `grow` get
`minmax(width, 1fr)` so they expand to fill remaining space.
Columns without a width fall back to `auto`.

members.name: hasGrowableColumns
members.description: Whether any column has the `grow` attribute set.

members.name: handleVirtualizerWheel
members.description:
Prevents the virtualizer from capturing wheel events when
the grid is only partially visible. The page continues
scrolling until the grid is scroll-eligible, then the
virtualizer handles scroll normally.

The grid is scroll-eligible when it either fits entirely
within the viewport, or is taller than the viewport and
spans it (top at or above viewport top, bottom at or below
viewport bottom). This handles grids with a `grid-height`
larger than the window.

members.name: applySearchTerm
members.description:
Filters `items` by the current intrinsic search term, matching
against `searchFields` when configured (all fields otherwise).
Returns the input array untouched when no search term is active.

members.name: variantTokenOriginalValues
members.default: new Map< string, { generatedPriority: string; generatedValue: string; originalPriority: string; originalValue: string | null; } >()

members.name: scheme
members.description:
The scheme to use for the component. This property is used internally
to control the light/dark scheme so it matches the scheme of
[esp-root](/components/root). It should not be set manually, it
exists as an attribute for styling purposes.

members.name: variant
members.description: The [color variant](/guides/color/variants) of the element.

members.name: traverseToClosest

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: The selector of the element to look for

members.description:
Traverse up the DOM tree to find the closest element that
matches the selector. This method is aware of shadow DOM
boundaries and will traverse through them to find the element.

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

events.description: The grid listens for click events, and if a child element has a `grid-event` attribute, grid publishes an event with the grid, clicked element, and associated data.
events.name: grid-event

events.description: Fired before the grid starts fetching a page of data.
events.name: esp-grid-load-start

events.description: Fired after fetched items have been applied to the grid.
events.name: esp-grid-load-success

events.description: Fired when a grid data fetch fails.
events.name: esp-grid-load-error

events.description: Fired whenever the grid's current item set changes.
events.name: esp-grid-items-changed

attributes.name: data-field
attributes.description:
If there is a `data-url` specified, the name of the
field on the response to get the array of items from.
If it is empty, the API response should simply return
an array of data items.

attributes.name: data-url
attributes.description: Specify a URL the grid will fetch data from.

attributes.name: items
attributes.description:
If using a `data-url`, the items retrieved are available
in the items property. An items array can be assigned
instead of using a `data-url`.

Assigning a new items array preserves the grid's current
intrinsic state: an active search term is re-applied to the
new items, the active sort column re-sorts them, and the
current page is kept when it still exists (clamping to the
nearest valid page otherwise). Infinite grids delegate search
and sort to the server through `fetchPage` instead.

attributes.name: page-size
attributes.description:
The number of items to display on a page. In paged mode this
controls how many rows are shown per page. In infinite scroll
mode this controls how many items are fetched per request.

attributes.name: grid-height
attributes.description:
The height of the grid when in infinite scroll mode. Accepts
any valid CSS height value. Defaults to `60vh`.

If the loaded content is shorter than this value (and there
are no more pages to fetch), the grid shrinks to fit.

attributes.name: search-fields
attributes.description: The fields to include when filtering results. It defaults to an empty array, which includes all fields. The test dataset includes an address field that is not displayed, so to only filter results by fields displayed in the grid, specify those fields in the `search-fields` attribute. When specifying arrays in plain HTML, the attributes should be wrapped in single quotes. ```html ${first_name} ${last_name} ```

attributes.name: scheme
attributes.description:
The scheme to use for the component. This property is used internally
to control the light/dark scheme so it matches the scheme of
[esp-root](/components/root). It should not be set manually, it
exists as an attribute for styling purposes.

attributes.name: variant
attributes.description: The [color variant](/guides/color/variants) of the element.

declarations.description:
A styled icon button designed for the `buttons` slot of [``](/components/header/). Each button wraps an SVG icon and dispatches a `clicked` event when activated. The button sizes itself to match the header height and transitions its background color on hover. ```html
brand
 ```

slots.description:
Optional custom SVG icon. Slotted icons override the generated SVG from the `icon` attribute and are automatically sized to `var(--esp-size-medium)`. ```html

 ```

members.name: ariaLabel
members.description:
Accessible label for icon-only buttons. When set, the inner
`<button>` receives an `aria-label` so screen readers announce
the button's purpose instead of silence.

Slotted SVG content remains supported and overrides this value.

declarations.description:
Used to build the top navigation for a site. An empty header displays a bar. It has three slots for populating header content: `topbar`, `brand`, `menu`, and `buttons`. The header works together with several companion components: [``](/components/menu/) provides the navigation container, [``](/components/menu/item/) renders individual links, and [``](/components/header/button/) adds icon buttons for global actions. ```html
brand
buttons
 ```

slots.description: Optional utility row displayed above the primary header row. Combine with `scroll-behavior="collapse-topbar"` when the row should collapse after scroll.
slots.name: topbar

slots.description:
Place site brand here. Typically an anchor wrapping a logo SVG or image. When omitted, `brand-logo`, `brand-text`, and `brand-href` render a configured brand automatically. The brand container supports vertical alignment via `--esp-header-brand-placement`. ```html
buttons
 ```
slots.name: brand

slots.description:
Place site navigation here. The `menu` slot should be populated with an [``](/components/menu/) containing [``](/components/menu/item/) children: ```html
brand
buttons
 ``` When there are too many ``s on the screen, a burger button automatically appears that slides the full menu into view. Resize the screen to see the responsive behavior: ```html
brand
buttons
 ``` Published-site headers can be configured without custom brand markup. Pair `layout`, `menu-display`, `theme-toggle`, and `scroll-behavior` with `esp-page header-position` when the shell should stay fixed or sticky: ```html ```
slots.name: menu

slots.description:
Place icon buttons for global actions here. The `buttons` slot should contain [``](/components/header/button/) instances. Each button can reference the configured SVG sprite with `icon` and fires a `clicked` event when activated: ```html
brand
 ``` The built-in theme toggle emits `esp-theme-toggle` only for a visitor activation, so a host can persist explicit choices without treating automatic scheme resolution as intent: ```js const root = document.querySelector("esp-root"); root.addEventListener("esp-theme-toggle", (event) => { localStorage.setItem("scheme", event.detail.scheme); }); ```
slots.name: buttons

members.name: showBurger
members.description:
Force the burger button to be visible regardless of the
menu width. Use this when the burger controls an external
element (e.g. a sidebar drawer) instead of the built-in
`<esp-menu>` overlay.

members.name: layout
members.description:
Header layout style. - `standard` places brand, menu, and actions on one row. - `centered-brand` centers the brand between left navigation and actions. - `extended` puts navigation on a second row on wide screens. - `extended-centered` centers the brand and second-row navigation. - `minimal` keeps only brand/actions inline and uses the drawer menu. ```html
```

members.name: brandText
members.description: Configured brand text used when the `brand` slot is empty.

members.name: brandLogo
members.description: Configured brand logo URL used when the `brand` slot is empty.

members.name: lightBrandLogo
members.description:
Configured brand logo URL used in light scheme. A non-empty value
overrides `brand-logo`; an empty value falls back to it.

members.name: darkBrandLogo
members.description:
Configured brand logo URL used in dark scheme. A non-empty value
overrides `brand-logo`; an empty value falls back to it.

members.name: brandHref
members.description: Optional brand link used by configured logo/text branding.

members.name: brandAlt
members.description:
Accessible text for a logo-only configured brand. When brand text
is also present, the logo is decorative unless this is set.

members.name: brandColor
members.description:
Optional configured brand color. Prefer theme fields for
product-wide brand typography and this property for a local
header override.

members.name: lightBrandColor
members.description:
Configured brand color used in light scheme. A non-empty value
overrides `brand-color`; an empty value falls back to it and then
`--esp-header-brand-color`.

members.name: darkBrandColor
members.description:
Configured brand color used in dark scheme. A non-empty value
overrides `brand-color`; an empty value falls back to it and then
`--esp-header-brand-color`.

members.name: brandAlign
members.description: Horizontal placement of the brand content inside its grid area.

members.name: brandWrap
members.description: Long-brand behavior for configured brand text.

members.name: menuDisplay
themeToggle
members.description:
Whether the header renders its built-in light/dark toggle.
Consumers can still provide their own control in the `buttons`
slot when this is `hidden`.

members.name: scrollBehavior
members.description:
Space-separated scroll behavior tokens. Supported tokens:
`compact`, `elevate`, `transparent`, `reveal`, `dock`,
`collapse-topbar`, and `progress`.

members.name: scrollThreshold
members.description: Scroll distance in pixels before scroll-reactive behavior applies.

members.name: dockOffset
members.description:
Scroll distance in pixels before `scroll-behavior="dock"` applies.
This is commonly set to the hero height when a transparent header
should dock after the first section.

members.name: drawerTarget
members.description:
Optional ID of an external drawer-capable menu controlled by the
header burger. When set, burger open/close events call
`openDrawer()` / `closeDrawer()` on that element instead of the
menu slotted into the header.

members.name: fullBleedContent
members.description:
Opt out of surface alignment. When an `esp-header` is slotted into an
`esp-page`, it constrains its bar content to the page's content
surface by default (the bar background stays full-bleed). Set this to
let the content span the full width instead, as it did before the
page grew alignment modes. Standalone headers (outside `esp-page`) are
already full-bleed and unaffected.

members.name: menuHasContent
members.description:
Whether the header carries a menu with navigable content. Read from
light DOM rather than the resolved `this.menu` (which is populated
only after the menu slot assigns) so burger visibility does not hang
on slot-resolution timing — otherwise a real menu whose slot has not
assigned yet would look empty and hide its burger. An empty or
absent `<esp-menu>` has nothing to reveal.

members.name: hasDrawerableContent
members.description:
Whether the burger has something to open: real menu items, an
external drawer target, or an explicit `show-burger` request. The
compact-width media query alone must not force the burger on when
there is nothing behind it.

members.name: burgerVisibleForTarget
members.description:
Whether the header is currently rendering a burger for its external
drawer target — the same predicate as the `too-wide` class (the target
already satisfies `hasDrawerableContent`). A vertical target may only
collapse while this holds, so a collapsed rail always has a visible,
accessible opener. Notably `menu-display="drawer"`/`show-burger` force
it, and the compact (36rem) query or an overflowing inline menu trip it
— a width band where the menu's own 50em collapse query matches but no
burger shows must NOT grant control.

members.name: syncExternalDrawerControl
members.description:
Grant/revoke a vertical drawer-target menu's permission to collapse,
tracking whether the burger is actually visible. Re-run whenever the
target, menu-display, compact-width, or fit state changes.

members.name: burger
members.description:
The burger element inside the header. External code can use
this to programmatically reset the burger's visual state
(e.g. when a drawer is closed via swipe).

members.name: variantTokenOriginalValues
members.default: new Map< string, { generatedPriority: string; generatedValue: string; originalPriority: string; originalValue: string | null; } >()

members.name: scheme
members.description:
The scheme to use for the component. This property is used internally
to control the light/dark scheme so it matches the scheme of
[esp-root](/components/root). It should not be set manually, it
exists as an attribute for styling purposes.

members.name: variant
members.description: The [color variant](/guides/color/variants) of the element.

members.name: traverseToClosest

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: The selector of the element to look for

members.description:
Traverse up the DOM tree to find the closest element that
matches the selector. This method is aware of shadow DOM
boundaries and will traverse through them to find the element.

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

```

declarations.description: A single responsive source for [esp-image](/components/image). Each option contributes one `srcset` candidate (`url` + a `width` descriptor); the parent `esp-image` reads its options and builds a native `` so the browser can pick the best variant for the viewport and device pixel ratio. This is the **editor / fallback** authoring form. Published markup should project a real light-DOM `` through `esp-image` instead (it is crawlable and preload-discoverable); `esp-image-option` is the convenient shape for editor previews and stored content before a `` exists. ```html ``` Set the optional `type` attribute to enable format negotiation. Give every option for a format the same `type` (e.g. `image/avif`), list the most preferred format first, and the browser will use the first format it supports — falling back to the `low-res` `` on the parent.
declarations.name: EspalierImageOption

members.name: width
members.description:
The intrinsic pixel width of this version of the image. Emitted as the
`srcset` width descriptor (e.g. `width="800"` → `… 800w`), which lets the
browser account for both viewport width and device pixel ratio.

members.name: imageUrl
members.description: The URL to retrieve this version of the image from.

members.name: type
members.description:
An optional MIME type for format negotiation (e.g. `image/avif`,
`image/webp`). When set, `esp-image` emits a dedicated
`<source type="…">` per format. Leave empty for plain resolution
switching. Do not mix typed and untyped options on the same `esp-image`.

attributes.name: width
attributes.description:
The intrinsic pixel width of this version of the image. Emitted as the
`srcset` width descriptor (e.g. `width="800"` → `… 800w`), which lets the
browser account for both viewport width and device pixel ratio.

attributes.name: url
attributes.description: The URL to retrieve this version of the image from.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description:
An optional MIME type for format negotiation (e.g. `image/avif`,
`image/webp`). When set, `esp-image` emits a dedicated
`<source type="…">` per format. Leave empty for plain resolution
switching. Do not mix typed and untyped options on the same `esp-image`.

declarations.description: A responsive image with a native ``. `esp-image` accepts two authoring shapes: 1. **Project a light-DOM ``** — the recommended form for published / server-rendered markup. Provide a real `` (or a bare ``) as a child and `esp-image` projects it through a ``, adding only the layout box, border, and lightbox wiring. Because the image lives in the served HTML it is discoverable by the preload scanner and visible to crawlers, and it is fetched exactly once (the component renders no competing internal image). 2. **Declare [esp-image-option](/components/image/image-option) children** — the editor / fallback form. `esp-image` builds a shadow `` and `srcset` from them. Useful for editor previews before a native `` has been generated. Source precedence, most specific first: `local-image` → a slotted `` / `` → `esp-image-option` children. In every mode the host reserves the layout box up front via `aspect-ratio` (from `original-width` / `original-height`), so there is no layout shift while the image loads.
declarations.name: EspalierImage

cssProperties.description: Set the border property of the image. ```html <style> esp-image.with-custom-border { --esp-image-border: 5px dashed orange; } </style> <esp-image class="with-custom-border" original-width="1200" original-height="800" low-res="https://picsum.photos/300/200" sizes="100vw"> <esp-image-option width="400" url="https://picsum.photos/600/400"></esp-image-option> <esp-image-option width="900" url="https://picsum.photos/900/600"></esp-image-option> <esp-image-option width="1200" url="https://picsum.photos/1200/800"></esp-image-option> </esp-image> ```
cssProperties.name: --esp-image-border

cssProperties.description: Background color of the shared field shell. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-layer-2)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-background

cssProperties.description: Border color of the shared field shell. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-border)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-border-color

cssProperties.description: Border width of the shared field shell. Defaults to `1px`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-border-width

cssProperties.description: Text color used inside the shared field shell. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-text)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-text-color

cssProperties.name: --esp-field-hover-bg

cssProperties.description: Focus background color of the shared field shell. Derived from `--esp-field-background`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-focus-bg

slots.description: A light-DOM `` or `` to project, or [esp-image-option](/components/image/image-option) children describing each available size. When projecting, `alt` lives on your ``; the component's `caption` still drives the [esp-lightbox](/components/lightbox) drawer (the two may differ). ```html ``` ```html ``` The component picks the responsive variant for its **own rendered box**: after it upgrades it measures its width (via a `ResizeObserver`) and sets `sizes` to that, so a small card or gallery column does not over-fetch — no hand-authored `sizes` required. The `sizes` attribute is only a fallback: it covers the brief window before the first measurement and the server-rendered *initial* fetch (the browser's preload scanner runs before the component upgrades). Set it to the layout's server-side estimate when that initial fetch matters: ```html ```

members.name: originalHeight
members.description:
The original height of the image; used to calculate the orientation of
the image and set the element aspect ratio.

members.name: originalWidth
members.description:
The original width of the image; used to calculate the orientation of
the image and set the element aspect ratio.

members.name: imageUrl
members.description: The fallback image URL. Used as the `` — the variant the browser loads when it cannot evaluate the responsive `srcset` (very old browsers), and the SEO/no-JS source. Named `low-res` because a small, fast-loading variant is the sensible default to point it at.

members.name: localImage
members.description: A local image URL (e.g. a `blob:` URL) that takes priority over responsive selection. When set, a plain `` with no responsive sources is rendered so the local preview always wins. Used during upload to show the user's local file immediately while CDN variants are generated in the background.

members.name: caption
members.description: A caption for the image. Drives the visible caption in the [esp-lightbox](/components/lightbox) drawer in every mode. In the fallback / `local-image` paths it is also applied as the internal ``; when projecting a light-DOM `` / ``, put `alt` on your own `` (it may legitimately differ from the drawer caption).

members.name: sizes
members.description:
Fallback `sizes` hint used only until the element measures its own rendered
width (and for the server-rendered *initial* fetch, which the browser
performs before this component upgrades). Once measured, the element
advertises its actual box width instead — in both the built-from-options
and projection paths — so the browser picks the smallest variant that
covers the box (× DPR), in any layout, with no hand-authored `sizes`. Set
this when you need a specific server-side hint (e.g. the generator's
per-layout value); otherwise leave it and the component sizes itself.

members.name: loading
members.description: The native `loading` strategy for the **internally rendered** `` (the fallback and `local-image` paths). Use `lazy` to defer offscreen gallery images until they approach the viewport. Defaults to `eager`. Has no effect in projection mode, where the projected `` carries its own `loading`, `decoding`, and `fetchpriority` directly.

members.name: renderedWidth
members.description:
The element's rendered content width in CSS px, measured at runtime. Once
known it drives `sizes` (see sizes); `0` before the first
measurement.

members.name: handleSlotChange
members.description: Re-evaluate the source mode / rebuild the `srcset` when children change.

members.name: findProjected
members.description: The author-provided light-DOM `` or `` to project, if any. Its presence (with no `local-image`) switches the component into projection mode, where it renders no internal image of its own.

members.name: effectiveSizes
members.description: The `sizes` to advertise: the measured box width once known, else the author hint.

members.name: buildSources
members.description:
Build one `<source>` per format from the slotted `esp-image-option`
children. Options are grouped by `type` (untyped options form a single
resolution-switching source) and ordered ascending by width within a group.

members.name: projectionStyles
members.default: ` esp-image > picture, esp-image > img { display: block; width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: var(--_esp-image-object-fit, cover); } esp-image > picture > img { display: block; width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: var(--_esp-image-object-fit, cover); } `
members.description: Light-DOM styles for projected content. `::slotted()` cannot reach the `` inside a projected `` (it matches only the top slotted node), so these rules are injected into the element's tree scope to make projected markup fill the host box and crop with `object-fit: cover`, identical to the built-from-options output.

members.name: variantTokenOriginalValues
members.default: new Map< string, { generatedPriority: string; generatedValue: string; originalPriority: string; originalValue: string | null; } >()

members.name: scheme
members.description:
The scheme to use for the component. This property is used internally
to control the light/dark scheme so it matches the scheme of
[esp-root](/components/root). It should not be set manually, it
exists as an attribute for styling purposes.

members.name: variant
members.description: The [color variant](/guides/color/variants) of the element.

members.name: traverseToClosest

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: The selector of the element to look for

members.description:
Traverse up the DOM tree to find the closest element that
matches the selector. This method is aware of shadow DOM
boundaries and will traverse through them to find the element.

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

members.name: accept
members.description:
Comma-separated list of accepted file types, in the same format as the
native input `accept` attribute: MIME types (`image/png`), MIME
wildcards (`image/*`), or extensions (`.png`). Files outside this list
are skipped and reported via the `files-rejected` event.

members.name: _rejectionNotice
members.description: Transient inline message describing skipped files.

members.name: _selectionGeneration
members.description:
Bumped whenever the image list is replaced or the component unmounts so
still-decoding selections from before the change are discarded instead
of landing in the new list.

members.name: _chromeWidth
members.description: Cached horizontal chrome (previews padding + field border); re-measured after resize.

members.name: _dragPointerId
members.description: The pointerId that initiated the drag — ignore events from other pointers.

members.name: _ghostHalfW
members.description: Cached ghost half-width/height so pointermove avoids offsetWidth/Height.

members.name: _cachedPreviews
members.description: Cached preview elements + bounding rects, set once when drag activates.

members.name: _lastDropHit
members.description: Last drop-target state to avoid redundant DOM updates in pointermove.

members.name: _savedBodyUserSelect
members.description: Saved body userSelect value to restore after drag.

members.name: _getPreviewIndex
members.description: Find the preview index from a composedPath.

members.name: _createGhost
members.description: Create a ghost thumbnail that follows the cursor.

members.name: _arrowColor
members.description: Cached arrow color so we don't call getComputedStyle on every update.

members.name: _updateDropTargets
members.description: Show a drop-target overlay on the hovered preview.

members.name: _clearDropTargets
members.description: Clear all drop-target overlays.

members.name: getContainerWidth
members.description:
Gets the container width for layout calculation based on responsive breakpoints.
Breakpoints chosen to maintain consistent visual appearance across devices:
- Mobile (< 640px): Full width minus padding
- Tablet (640px - 1024px): 90% width
- Desktop (> 1024px): 85% width with max 1400px

members.name: _isAccepted
members.description: Check a file against the `accept` list (MIME, MIME wildcard, or extension).

members.name: _addSelectedImage
members.description:
Insert a processed image at its selection-order position. Anchors on
already-inserted batch neighbors so concurrent completions, removals,
and reorders mid-batch cannot scramble the order.

members.name: _reportRejectedFiles
members.description: Dispatch `files-rejected` and show the transient inline notice.

members.name: _makeCallbacks
members.description:
Create upload callbacks bound to a specific image.
Closures capture the image reference directly — no filename lookup.

members.name: _THUMBNAIL_UPSCALE_SLACK
members.description: Allowed upscale factor before a preview switches to its full-resolution source.

members.name: _fullPreviewUrls
members.description: Full-resolution object URLs for selected images rendered beyond thumbnail capacity.

members.name: _previewUrlFor
members.description:
Resolve the URL a preview should display at the given rendered height.
Thumbnails cover rows near the target height; images stretched well
beyond thumbnail capacity (clamped tall rows, single images) lazily
switch to a full-resolution object URL so they don't upscale blurry.

members.name: _pickExistingPreviewUrl
members.description:
For a server image, pick the smallest responsive variant that covers the
preview's rendered box (× DPR), falling back to the largest variant — and to
the single `url` when no responsive set was supplied. The justified grid
sizes each box to the image's aspect, so the displayed width is
`renderedHeight × aspect`.

members.name: _releaseFullPreviewUrl
members.description: Revoke the full-resolution preview URL for one image, if it exists.

members.name: _releaseSelectedImages
members.description: Abort in-flight uploads and revoke preview object URLs for selected images.

members.name: setExistingImages
members.description:
Populate the component with images that already exist on the server.
Each entry must provide a display URL, dimensions for layout, and
an `uploadedId` that identifies the server-side image.

Entries with missing `url`/`uploadedId` or non-finite/non-positive
dimensions are silently filtered out.

events.description: Fired when an image has been removed by the user. Narrow on `detail.source` to distinguish newly selected images from pre-existing server images. If the image's upload was still in flight, its `signal` aborts.
events.name: file-removed

events.description: Fired when an image has been selected by the user. `detail.image` is the actual object stored in `uploadedImages`; `detail.onProgress`, `detail.onComplete`, and `detail.onFailed` are callbacks bound to that image. `detail.signal` aborts if the image is removed mid-upload — pass it to fetch/XHR.
events.name: file-selected

events.description: Fired when the user clicks Retry on a failed upload. `detail.image` is the same object reference; fresh callbacks and a fresh `signal` are provided. The component automatically resets the image to indeterminate progress.
events.name: upload-retry

events.description: Fired after the user drags an image to a new position. `detail.images` is the reordered array (same references, new order).
events.name: images-reordered

declarations.name: getImageDetails
declarations.description:
Measure a selected file and produce a `SelectedUploadImage` whose `url`
is a lightweight object URL suitable for previews (downscaled for large
raster images). Rejects when the file cannot be read or decoded.

The returned `url` is an object URL owned by the caller — release it
with releasePreviewUrl when the image is discarded.

declarations.name: releasePreviewUrl
declarations.description:
Release the object URL backing a selected image's preview. Safe to call
on existing images — server URLs are never revoked.

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

declarations.name: detectDatePattern
declarations.description:
Detect the date field order and separator for the given locale
(defaults to the browser's locale).

Uses an unambiguous probe date — November 25, 2023 — where
both month (11) and day (25) are > 12, making it trivial to
distinguish which part is which.

declarations.name: parseDateInput
declarations.description:
Parse a user-typed date string into a `Temporal.PlainDate`,
using the detected locale pattern to interpret field positions.

Accepts two formats:
- **With separators** — e.g. `06/15/2025`, `15-06-2025`
- **Digits only** — exactly 8 digits (e.g. `06152025` for MDY)

Years must be four digits. Two-digit years are rejected because
they're ambiguous (is `88` → 1988 or 2088?).

Returns `null` for anything that doesn't produce a valid date.

declarations.name: formatDateForLocale
declarations.description:
Format a `Temporal.PlainDate` for display using the given
locale pattern (field order and separator).

Wraps a standard HTML input field, providing value binding, input validation, and programmatic focus for Espalier forms and UIs. It supports several input types, and has an `icon` attribute plus a slotted icon escape hatch. ```html

esp-input example

Hello from the input!

 ``` Input can have [color variants](/guides/colors/variants). Here are some examples: ```html ``` ### Email validation Set `input-type="email"` to get built-in email validation. You can customize the error message with `validation-message`: ```html ``` ### Number input with min/max ```html ``` ### Telephone with locale-aware formatting The `tel` type uses phone localities to parse and format phone numbers on blur. The value is submitted as E.164 (e.g. `"+15551234567"`). Use `tel-localities` to specify which regions are accepted (space-separated region codes); when omitted, a sensible default set of 10 common regions is used. ```html ``` ### Date input with locale-aware mask The `date` type displays a placeholder based on the user's locale (e.g. "MM/DD/YYYY"). The user types freely and the input parses the date on blur, submitting the value as ISO 8601 (`2025-01-15`). ```html ```
declarations.name: EspalierInput

slots.description: Optional custom SVG icon. Slotted icons override the generated SVG from the `icon` attribute. ```html <esp-input icon="user-circle" icon-label="Open user details"></esp-input> ``` ```html <esp-input name="affiliateLink" value="https://example.com/?ref=taproot" readonly icon="external-link" icon-label="Open affiliate link"> </esp-input> ```

members.name: formCtrl
members.default: new FormFieldController({ host: this, internals: this.internals, getFormValue: () => this.value || null, getValidity: () => this._getValidity(), getValidationAnchor: (): HTMLElement => this.theInput.value ?? this, onReset: () => { this.value = ""; this._dateDisplayText = ""; this._dateManaged = false; this._telDisplayText = ""; this._telManaged = false; this._hasVisibleError = false; }, onRestore: (state: string) => { this.value = state; if (this.inputType === "date" && state) { this._restoreDateDisplay(state); } if (this.inputType === "tel" && state) { this._restoreTelDisplay(state); } }, onDisabled: (isDisabled: boolean) => { this.disabled = isDisabled; }, })

members.name: formItemDescription
members.default: new FormFieldDescriptionController({ host: this, getTarget: () => this.theInput.value, })

members.name: fieldName
members.description:
A string value used for the id of the input field.

```html
<esp-input field-name="username"></esp-input>
```

members.name: icon
members.description:
Optional icon name from the configured Espalier SVG sprite.
Slotted content remains supported and overrides this value.

members.name: iconLabel
members.description:
Accessible label for the icon action button. Consumers should
provide a meaningful label whenever `icon` or a slotted icon
represents an action. The trimmed value is applied as both
`aria-label` and `title`; if omitted, the button falls back to
a label derived from `icon`, then to a generic action label.

```html
<esp-input icon="external-link" icon-label="Open affiliate link"></esp-input>
```

members.name: inputType
members.description:
Specifies the type of input, such as text, email, password, etc.
The `date` type provides a locale-aware placeholder mask —
the user types a date freely, and the value is parsed and
submitted as ISO 8601 on blur. The `tel` type provides
phone locality matching — the user types digits and
separators, and the value is parsed and submitted as E.164
on blur.

```html
<esp-input input-type="email"></esp-input>
```

members.name: placeholder
members.description:
Placeholder text shown when no value is set. For `date`
and `tel` inputs, an explicitly provided placeholder
overrides the locale-derived placeholder. Set
`placeholder=""` to suppress the derived placeholder.

```html
<esp-input placeholder="Enter a value"></esp-input>
```

members.name: forceLowercase
members.description:
A boolean value. When true, values the user enters are
forced to lowercase.

```html
<esp-input force-lowercase></esp-input>
```

members.name: value
members.description:
The value of the input. For `input-type="date"`, this is
an ISO 8601 date string (e.g. `"2025-06-15"`). For
`input-type="tel"`, this is an E.164 phone number
(e.g. `"+15551234567"`).

```html
<esp-input value="Hello World"></esp-input>
```

members.name: name
members.description:
The name used when the input participates in a `<form>`.

```html
<form>
  <esp-input name="username" value="Jane Doe"></esp-input>
</form>
```

members.name: required
members.description:
When true, the field must have a value before the form can be
submitted.

```html
<esp-input required></esp-input>
```

members.name: requiredMessage
members.description:
A custom message to display when the field is required but empty.
Defaults to `"Please fill out this field."` when not set.

```html
<esp-input required required-message="You must enter your password to continue!"></esp-input>
```

members.name: validationMessage
members.description:
A custom message to display when the value doesn't match
the expected type or pattern. The default depends on the
input type (e.g. "Please enter a valid email address."
for `input-type="email"`).

```html
<esp-input input-type="email"
  validation-message="This doesn't look like a valid email.">
</esp-input>
```

members.name: disabled
members.description:
When true, the input is disabled and cannot be interacted with.

```html
<esp-input disabled></esp-input>
```

members.name: readonly
members.description:
When true, the input value is submitted with forms but cannot
be edited by the user. The icon action remains enabled unless
`disabled` is also set.

```html
<esp-input readonly value="Copy-only value"></esp-input>
```

members.name: pattern
members.description:
A regular expression the value must match. Only applies to
text-like types (text, email, url, tel, password, search).

```html
<esp-input pattern="[A-Za-z]{3}" validation-message="Enter exactly 3 letters."></esp-input>
```

members.name: minlength
members.description: Minimum number of characters. Only applies to text-like types.

members.name: maxlength
members.description: Maximum number of characters. Only applies to text-like types.

members.name: min
members.description:
Minimum value. For `input-type="number"` this is passed to
the native input. For `input-type="date"` it is an ISO 8601
date used for custom range validation.

```html
<esp-input input-type="number" min="0"></esp-input>
<esp-input input-type="date" min="2025-01-01"></esp-input>
```

members.name: max
members.description:
Maximum value. For `input-type="number"` this is passed to
the native input. For `input-type="date"` it is an ISO 8601
date used for custom range validation.

```html
<esp-input input-type="number" max="100"></esp-input>
<esp-input input-type="date" max="2030-12-31"></esp-input>
```

members.name: step
members.description: Step value for `input-type="number"`.

members.name: inputmode
members.description:
Hint the browser about which virtual keyboard to show.
When not set, a sensible default is chosen based on
`input-type` (e.g. `"email"` for email, `"numeric"` for
number and date).

members.name: telLocalities
members.description:
Space-separated list of region codes that defines which
phone localities are accepted for `input-type="tel"`. The
first locality whose format matches wins. When omitted,
defaults to a set of 10 common regions.

```html
<esp-input input-type="tel" tel-localities="US CA GB"></esp-input>
```

members.name: _nativeInputType
members.description:
The native `<input type="...">` to use. Date inputs render
as `type="text"` so we can manage the display ourselves.

members.name: _effectiveInputmode
members.description:
Resolve the effective `inputmode`. If the author set one
explicitly, use that; otherwise pick a sensible default
based on the input type.

members.name: _displayValue
members.description:
The display value shown in the inner `<input>`. For date
inputs this is the locale-formatted text; for tel inputs
this is the formatted phone display; for everything else
it's `this.value`.

members.name: _effectivePlaceholder
members.description:
The placeholder text. For date inputs, shows the locale
pattern (e.g. "MM/DD/YYYY"). For tel inputs, shows the
first locality's phone format placeholder.

members.name: _syncInput
members.description:
Sync the form value and fire `value-changed`. If the field
is already showing an error, validate eagerly so the error
clears immediately when the user fixes it. Otherwise just
sync silently — errors will appear on blur.

members.name: _finalizeDateValue
members.description: Parse and reformat the raw date text on blur.

members.name: _finalizeTelValue
members.description: Parse and reformat the raw phone text on blur.

members.name: _getDateValidity
members.description:
Custom validation for `input-type="date"`. The inner
`<input>` is `type="text"` so the browser won't validate
dates — we do it ourselves.

members.name: _getTelValidity
members.description:
Custom validation for `input-type="tel"`. The inner
`<input>` is `type="tel"` which browsers don't validate —
we do it ourselves using the phone locality system.

members.name: focus
validate
members.description: Re-run constraint validation and dispatch `validity-changed`.

members.name: checkValidity
members.description: Check whether the current state is valid (delegates to ElementInternals).

members.name: formResetCallback
members.description: Called by the browser when the owning `<form>` is reset.

members.name: formStateRestoreCallback
members.description: Called by the browser to restore form state (bfcache, etc.).

members.name: formDisabledCallback
members.description: Called by the browser when a parent `<fieldset>` is enabled or disabled.

members.name: _restoreDateDisplay
members.description: Parse an ISO value and set the locale display text.

members.name: _restoreTelDisplay
members.description: Parse an E.164 value and set the locale display text.

declarations.name: getPhoneLocalities
declarations.description:
Build an ordered array of `PhoneLocalityConfig` from a list
of region code strings. Unknown codes are silently ignored.

When no regions are provided, defaults to the ten most common
regions.

declarations.name: parsePhoneInput
declarations.description:
Extract raw digits from user input, then try to match against
the given localities in priority order.

Returns a `PhoneParseResult` on match, or `null` if no
locality can interpret the digits.

declarations.name: formatPhoneForLocale
declarations.description:
Format an E.164 string for display using the given localities.
Used to restore display text from a stored E.164 value.

Returns the formatted display string, or the raw `e164` if
no locality matches.

A full-screen lightbox for viewing high-res versions of [esp-image](/components/image) elements. Point the `for` attribute at a CSS selector for the container that holds the images. Clicking any image in that container opens the lightbox at that image. On mobile, swipe left or right to navigate. The gallery wraps around — swiping past the last image goes back to the first. A subtle caret at the bottom of the screen reveals a drawer overlay containing the image caption and a `comments` slot. ```html
```

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

events.name: esp-lightbox-changed
events.description: Fired when the displayed image changes — on open, navigation (prev/next buttons, keyboard arrows, swipe, dot click). The `index` in the detail is the zero-based position of the now-visible image in the source container.
