declarations.description:
Shared navigation primitive for top navigation, sidebar navigation,
and drawer navigation.

Use `mode="horizontal"` for top navigation, `mode="vertical"` for
sidebar navigation, and `mode="drawer"` for drawer-only navigation.
The same tree of `esp-menu-item` and `esp-menu-group` children can
render inline and as a drawer so consumers do not need duplicate
navigation markup for desktop and mobile. An inline vertical rail keeps
its intrinsic height so a page-level layout can move a tall sidebar with
document scroll; the fixed drawer presentation supplies its own vertical
scrolling instead.

**2.4 migration:** inline vertical rails no longer create their own bounded
scroller. A standalone menu inside a fixed-height or overflow-hidden shell
can restore that presentation by bounding and scrolling the host:

```html
<esp-menu mode="vertical" style="max-height: 100dvh; overflow-y: auto;">
  ...
</esp-menu>
```

```html
<esp-menu mode="horizontal" overflow="left-drawer">
  <esp-menu-item label="Home" url="/"></esp-menu-item>
  <esp-menu-group label="Products" url="/products">
    <esp-menu-item label="Overview" url="/products"></esp-menu-item>
    <esp-menu-item label="Pricing" url="/pricing"></esp-menu-item>
  </esp-menu-group>
</esp-menu>
```

members.name: _mobileCollapsed
members.description: True while the viewport is narrow enough to collapse a vertical rail.

members.name: mode
members.description:
Rendering mode: horizontal top navigation, vertical sidebar
navigation, or drawer-only navigation.

members.name: overflow
members.description:
Overflow behavior for horizontal navigation.

`left-drawer` and `right-drawer` force drawer access from the
chosen side. `wrap` and `scroll` keep navigation inline.
`auto` lets an owning component such as `esp-header` opt into
drawer fallback when inline content does not fit.

members.name: side
members.description: Which side the drawer is attached to.

members.name: ariaLabel
members.description: Accessible label for the navigation landmark.

members.name: autoExpand
members.description: Automatically expand groups to reveal the current page.

members.name: sliding
members.description:
Compatibility hook used by `esp-header` when inline navigation
becomes too wide. Prefer `mode`/`overflow` in application code.

members.name: collapsed
members.description: Compatibility alias for the old vertical menu drawer state.

members.name: setMobileCollapsed
members.description:
React to the vertical-rail collapse breakpoint. Only a vertical menu
changes presentation, so non-vertical menus just record the state
without a re-render.

members.name: applyMobileCollapse
members.description:
Re-sync swipe/rendering after a change to the vertical-collapse
inputs. When the menu un-collapses back to a rail, any open drawer is
closed.

members.name: openDrawer
members.description:
Open the drawer overlay when this menu is drawer-capable.

Pass the control that opened the drawer so focus can return to
it when the drawer closes.

members.name: closeDrawer

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

`auto` lets an owning component such as `esp-header` opt into

declarations.description:
A Lit ReactiveController that adds swipe-to-reveal drawer behavior.

On coarse-pointer (touch) devices, an edge-originated horizontal
swipe opens the drawer, and a swipe in the closing direction — from
the scrim or the drawer panel itself — closes it. On fine-pointer
(mouse) devices, the controller still listens for clicks outside the
drawer to close it.

Once the user swipes past `COMMIT_THRESHOLD_VW` the gesture commits
and the drawer animates to its final state — it is never left
partially open.

While the drawer is open, a touch that starts inside it is tracked
only far enough to detect a horizontal close swipe; vertical motion
is left to the browser so the drawer scrolls normally. Taps outside
the drawer (on the scrim or anywhere else) close it.
declarations.name: SwipeRevealController

members.name: _drawerElement
members.description: The drawer DOM element — touches inside it are left alone for scrolling.

members.name: gestureStartedInDrawer
members.description:
Whether the current gesture began inside the open drawer. Such a
gesture is a candidate close swipe: it is tracked so pushing the
drawer panel shut works, but default touch behavior is left alone
until the swipe is confirmed horizontal, so vertical scrolling
inside the drawer is preserved.

members.name: tracking
members.description: Whether we are actively tracking a potential swipe (touchmove attached).

members.name: horizontalScrollTarget
members.description:
If the touchstart landed on a horizontally-scrollable element,
we store it here so that `handleTouchMove` can check whether the
element is at its scroll edge in the swipe direction.

members.name: lastTouchEndTime
members.description:
Timestamp of the most recent touchend. Used to suppress the
synthetic `click` event that mobile browsers fire after every
touch sequence. We ignore any click that arrives within
CLICK_COOLDOWN_MS of the last touchend — this is more reliable
than tracking from the open/close call, because the click can
originate from an *earlier* touch (e.g. stopping a fling
scroll) that completed before the drawer was opened.

members.name: openedAt
members.description:
Timestamp of the last open() call. Used as a last-resort guard
to prevent any close trigger (synthetic click, stale touchend,
etc.) from immediately closing the drawer right after it opens.

members.name: closedViaTap
members.description:
Set to `true` when the drawer is closed via a tap (touchend)
outside the drawer. The next `click` event (the synthetic click
that mobile browsers fire after every touch) must be suppressed
entirely so it doesn't reach other handlers (e.g. the burger
button underneath the scrim).

members.name: drawerElement
members.description: Set the drawer DOM element so the controller can ignore touches inside it.

members.name: enabled
members.description:
Whether the controller processes swipe gestures.

When disabled, touch events are ignored entirely — no tracking,
no `preventDefault`, and `open()` is a no-op. This prevents
non-sidebar `esp-menu` instances (e.g. inside a
popover dropdown) from hijacking the swipe gesture and blocking
the real sidebar drawer.

`esp-menu` syncs this with drawer-capable modes so swipe is only
enabled when the menu is in drawer mode or drawer fallback.

members.name: attachTouchListeners
members.description:
Attach only touchstart and touchend on document (both passive).
The non-passive touchmove listener is added on-demand in
`startTracking()` and removed in `stopTracking()`, so it never
interferes with native scrolling inside the drawer.

members.name: startTracking
members.description:
Start tracking a potential swipe gesture by adding a non-passive
touchmove listener.  Only called when the touchstart landed
*outside* the drawer.

members.name: stopTracking
members.description:
Stop tracking — removes the non-passive touchmove listener so
native scrolling is unblocked.

members.name: isHorizontalDragControl
members.description:
Check whether the touch landed on a form control that uses
horizontal dragging natively — such as `<input type="range">`.
These elements must not be hijacked by the swipe gesture.

Walks the composed path (which crosses shadow DOM boundaries)
so sliders rendered inside web component shadow roots are found.

members.name: isInsideDatePickerCalendar
members.description:
Check whether the touch landed inside a date picker's calendar
popover. Horizontal swipes on the calendar should navigate
months, not open/close the drawer.

Walks the composed path (which crosses shadow DOM boundaries)
looking for a `.picker-popover` element whose shadow root host
is an `esp-date-picker`.

members.name: hasNoSwipeAncestor
members.description:
Check whether the touch landed inside an element (or ancestor)
marked with the `data-no-swipe` attribute. This is a general-
purpose opt-out: any component that needs to prevent the drawer
gesture from firing can simply add `data-no-swipe` to itself or
to a wrapper element (e.g. a fullscreen editor shell, a dialog
vellum, or a color picker).

Walks the composed path (which crosses shadow DOM boundaries)
until it reaches `document.body`.

members.name: findHorizontallyScrollable
members.description:
Find the nearest horizontally-scrollable ancestor of the touch
target (e.g. a wide `<pre>` or `<code>` block).

Walks the composed path (which crosses shadow DOM boundaries)
and checks each element for actual horizontal overflow — i.e.
`scrollWidth > clientWidth` and a CSS overflow style that
allows scrolling.

Returns the element if found, or `null`.

members.name: canScrollInDirection
members.description:
Check whether a horizontally-scrollable element can still scroll
in the given direction. If the element is already at its scroll
edge, the swipe gesture should open the menu instead.

For a left-side menu, opening swipes right (deltaX > 0).
The content would also pan right (showing content to the left),
which requires `scrollLeft > 0`. If `scrollLeft === 0`, the
content is already at its left edge and can't scroll further in
that direction, so we allow the drawer gesture.

members.name: isPointInDrawer
members.description:
Check if a point falls inside the drawer element's bounds.
Uses coordinates because the drawer lives inside a shadow DOM
popover in the top layer, so composedPath() from a document-level
listener won't include it.

members.name: isWithinOpenEdge
members.description:
Whether a touch at `clientX` began close enough to the drawer's
leading edge to be allowed to *open* it. For a left drawer that is
the left edge; for a right drawer, the right edge.

members.name: handleDocumentClick
members.description:
Handle clicks anywhere on the document. If the drawer is open and
the click landed outside the drawer, close it.

This listener runs in the **capture** phase so it fires before
any element-level click handlers. When we close the drawer here,
we also stop the click from propagating — otherwise it would
reach the burger button and immediately reopen the drawer.

On touch devices, tap-to-close is handled by `handleTouchEnd`.
The synthetic `click` that follows is suppressed here via the
`closedViaTap` flag.

members.name: lockScroll
members.description:
Lock the page so it can't scroll behind the scrim.

Instead of `overflow: hidden` on body (which can suppress all
native scrolling on Android — even inside top-layer popovers),
we pin the body in place with `position: fixed` and restore the
scroll position on unlock.

members.name: inertElements
members.description:
Toggle `inert` on page content so underlying elements cannot
receive touch events, scroll, or allow text selection while the
drawer is open.

Walks the composed host chain to find the `esp-page` region
containing this menu, then toggles `inert` on that region's
siblings. Sidebar menus exclude their aside; header menus
nested inside `esp-header` exclude the page header region.

declarations.description:
A Lit ReactiveController that implements bi-directional sticky behavior
for sidebar elements. Short sidebars stick below the header. Tall sidebars
(taller than viewport) stick to the bottom when scrolling down, stick to
the top when scrolling up, and scroll naturally when direction reverses.

Uses only `position: sticky` (never `absolute`) so the element always
remains in normal flow, preserving `min-content` grid column widths.

When the header is not fixed (no `fixed-menus` attribute on `esp-page`),
the effective top offset tracks the header's visible bottom edge. As the
header scrolls out of view, the offset decreases from `headerHeight` to
`0`, so the sidebar smoothly rises to the top of the viewport with no gap.
declarations.name: BiDirectionalStickyController

members.name: onFixedMenusChanged
members.description: Bound handler so we can unsubscribe cleanly.

members.name: getEffectiveTopOffset
members.description:
Compute the effective top offset for the sticky element.

When the header is fixed, it always occupies space at the top of the
viewport, so we return the full header height.

When the header is *not* fixed (scrolls with the page), we return the
visible portion of the header's bottom edge clamped to `[0, headerHeight]`.
This means:
- Header fully visible → offset = headerHeight (sidebar sits below header)
- Header partially scrolled → offset = remaining visible height
- Header fully scrolled out → offset = 0 (sidebar reaches viewport top)

members.name: detectHeaderState
members.description:
Detect header height and whether it is fixed-position.
The `fixed-header`/`fixed-menus` class on `.esp-page` makes the
header `position: fixed`.

declarations.description: Used to lay out standard page structure.
declarations.name: EspalierPage

cssProperties.name: --esp-page-background

cssProperties.description: The maximum width of the main content well. Set automatically by the `kind` attribute: `1536px` (wide), `768px` (narrow), or `none` (full). This cap now sizes the main grid track; surplus width beyond it becomes the canvas gutters.
cssProperties.name: --esp-page-max-width

cssProperties.description: The background image to display behind page content. Defaults to `none`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-page-background-image

cssProperties.description: The opacity of the background image layer. Defaults to `1`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-page-background-image-opacity

cssProperties.description: The background color of the canvas gutters (the outer regions revealed when the viewport exceeds the cap). Defaults to `transparent`, so gutters match the page until styled and narrow viewports are unaffected.
cssProperties.name: --esp-page-canvas-background

cssProperties.description: A background image for the canvas gutters, mirroring the page background-image knob. Defaults to `none`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-page-canvas-background-image

cssProperties.description: The opacity of the canvas gutter image layer. Defaults to `1`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-page-canvas-background-image-opacity

cssProperties.description: The box shadow drawn on the left and right edges of the content surface. On by default; casts into the gutters and is clipped away when the surface fills the viewport, so it only shows above the cap. Set to `none` to remove it.
cssProperties.name: --esp-page-surface-shadow

cssProperties.description: An optional border on the inline edges of the surface, for themes preferring a hairline over a shadow. Defaults to `none` (e.g. `1px solid var(--esp-color-border)`). Combine with `--esp-page-surface-shadow: none` to switch the content frame from a drop shadow to a hairline, or turn both off for no frame.
cssProperties.name: --esp-page-surface-border

cssProperties.description: The background of the main content well. Defaults to `transparent` (the well shows the page background). Set it to give the content well its own card color, or pair the transparent default with `--esp-page-surface-shadow: none` for content that floats directly on the page with no frame.
cssProperties.name: --esp-page-main-background

cssProperties.description: The width of the open flyout track (and of the `esp-flyout` overlay drawer). Defaults to `20rem`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-page-flyout-width

cssProperties.description: Offset reserved for fixed headers. Defaults to `var(--esp-header-height)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-page-fixed-header-offset

cssProperties.description: Top inset for sticky headers. Defaults to `0`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-page-sticky-header-top

cssProperties.description: Z-index for fixed/sticky header regions. Defaults to `20`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-page-header-z-index

cssProperties.description: Background color of the shared field shell. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-layer-2)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-background

cssProperties.description: Border color of the shared field shell. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-border)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-border-color

cssProperties.description: Border width of the shared field shell. Defaults to `1px`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-border-width

cssProperties.description: Text color used inside the shared field shell. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-text)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-text-color

cssProperties.description: Hover background color of the shared field shell. Derived from `--esp-field-background`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-hover-bg

cssProperties.description: Focus background color of the shared field shell. Derived from `--esp-field-background`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-focus-bg

cssProperties.description: Shadow color used for shared field focus treatment. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-shadow)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-field-focus-shadow

The page wrapper. It defaults to a grid with a height of 100vh. For the demo page, the height is set to 400px by styling the CSS part: ```html
header
sidebar
right
content
```
cssParts.name: wrapper

cssParts.description: The two canvas gutter regions flanking the surface.
cssParts.name: canvas

cssParts.description:
The surface backdrop carrying the edge shadow/border. ```html
header
sidebar
right

With background image

 ``` Use `align` to place the surface within the page once it hits its cap, and the canvas tokens to style the gutters. A low `--esp-page-max-width` is set here only so the gutters are visible inside the narrow demo frame; the surface edge shadow is on by default. ```html
header
sidebar

```
cssParts.name: surface

slots.description:
Global navigation content (typically an `esp-header`). ```html
right
content
```
slots.name: header

slots.description: Contextual navigation placed in the left aside.
slots.name: sidebar

slots.description: Content to place in the right aside.
slots.name: right

slots.description: A transient `esp-flyout` panel that lives on the canvas, outside the content surface. Closed it costs no width; open it claims the right canvas gutter first — the surface keeps its alignment weighting and shifts only as far as the flyout's width requires — then docks as a width-competing right sidebar when no gutter exists, and becomes an overlay drawer on small viewports. The persistent `right` aside and the transient flyout are complementary, not alternatives. A flyout opened with an `anchor` aligns to that trigger and stays in the same document scroll flow.
slots.name: flyout

slots.description: Content to place in the footer. Slot `esp-footer` directly to receive the page's surface-alignment custom-property contract. A wrapper can preserve landmark semantics but does not receive that direct contract.
slots.name: footer

slots.description:
The main page content. The main content region applies `contain: inline-size` so child components cannot push the content well wider than its grid column. In `narrow` mode, slotted children are constrained to `max-inline-size: 66ch` for optimal reading measure. ```html

Long content

 ```

members.name: syncFlyoutState
members.description:
Mirror the slotted flyout's open, overlay, and anchor state onto
host attributes read by the grid CSS. State is recomputed from the
slot so multiple flyouts and slot changes stay correct.

members.name: footerElementProvidesLandmark
members.description:
Preserve the legacy footer slot's contentinfo semantics without nesting
landmarks around an `esp-footer` (or another explicit footer landmark).

members.name: kind
members.description:
The layout mode of the page.

- `wide` (default) — constrains the main content well to 1536px.
  Optimized for high-density dashboards and complex data grids.
- `narrow` — constrains the main content well to 768px and applies
  a `max-inline-size: 66ch` reading measure. Optimized for
  long-form reading content.
- `full` — no max-width constraint; the content well spans the
  full available space. Optimized for immersive canvases like
  maps or design tools.

members.name: align
members.description:
Horizontal alignment of the content **surface** (left aside + main
well + right aside) within the page once the viewport is wider than
the content cap. The surplus width becomes styleable **canvas**
gutters on the outer edges, weighted by this attribute.

- `start` (default) — the surface hugs the leading edge; all spare
  width collects in the trailing gutter. This reproduces today's
  layout.
- `center` — spare width is split evenly into both gutters, centering
  the surface.
- `end` — the surface hugs the trailing edge; spare width collects in
  the leading gutter.

Has no effect when `kind="full"` (no cap means no gutters) or on
viewports narrower than the cap (the surface already fills the width).

members.name: contained
members.description:
When set, the header and footer sit **on** the content surface —
the same width as the left aside + main well + right aside — instead
of spanning the full viewport. Combined with a styled canvas this
frames the whole page as one contained band floating on the canvas,
with a single continuous surface edge shadow running from the header
through the content to the footer.

Below the cap there are no gutters, so a contained page renders
identically to the default full-bleed chrome. Designed for
`header-position="normal"` or `"sticky"`; a fixed header cannot be
banded to the surface.

members.name: headerPosition
members.description:
Header positioning behavior for the `header` slot.

- `normal` lets the header scroll with content.
- `sticky` keeps the header stuck to the top after it reaches it.
- `fixed` pins the header to the viewport and offsets page content.

`fixed-menus` remains as a compatibility alias for `fixed`.

members.name: fixedMenus
members.description:
Compatibility alias for the original fixed header/sidebar behavior.
Prefer `header-position="fixed"` for new code.

declarations.description:
Component for selecting a single option from a list of options. For selecting multiple options, see [esp-pick-some](/components/pickers/pick-some/). ```html ``` ### Initial state Set the initially selected item using the `selected` attribute on the `esp-picker-item`: ```html ``` ### Variable-height items Items with longer text wrap naturally. The menu sizes itself to fit the actual content height of each item: ```html
``` ### Typeahead (local filtering) Add the `typeahead` attribute to let the user type to filter the option list. The input becomes editable and the picker switches to a filter icon on focus: ```html ``` ### Typeahead (remote filtering) Set the `fetchItems` property to a callback that returns matching items from a server. The picker debounces input (300 ms by default) and shows a loading indicator while the request is in flight. Use the provided `AbortSignal` to cancel stale requests: ```html ```

members.name: suppressAutoSelect
members.description: Suppresses the auto-select on the next focus event.

members.name: typeaheadCtrl
members.default: new TypeaheadController({ host: this, onFilteredItemsChanged: (items) => { // Create new item objects with selection state so the menu // highlights the current pick. We must NOT mutate the // original items — pickerItems may carry `selected: true` // flags that the updated() lifecycle reads. if (this.selectedItem) { const selectedValue = this.selectedItem.value; this.filteredItems = items.map((item) => ({ ...item, selected: item.value === selectedValue, })); } else { this.filteredItems = items.map((item) => ({ ...item })); } }, onLoadingChanged: (loading) => { this.typeaheadLoading = loading; }, })

members.name: _hasPendingValue
members.description:
Whether a `.value` was set that could not be matched to a picker
item. This can happen when:
- `.value` is set before pickerItems are extracted from the slot
- `.value` is set to a string that doesn't match any current item
- `.value` is explicitly set to `undefined` (clear)

Tracked separately from the value itself so that
`.value = undefined` (an explicit clear) is distinguishable
from "no pending value". When pickerItems change, `updated()`
re-applies the pending value. Cleared on form reset and when a
match is found.

members.name: _pendingValue
members.description:
The unmatched value from the last `.value` setter call.
Only meaningful when `_hasPendingValue` is `true`. Re-applied in
`updated()` when pickerItems change; cleared on form reset and
when a match is found.

members.name: value
members.description:
The currently selected item value, or `undefined` when no item
is selected.

Setting this updates the selected state across the canonical
picker items and any active filtered menu items.

members.name: typeahead
members.description:
When true, the picker input becomes a typeahead search field.
The user types to filter the option list. The match is
constrained — the user must pick from the filtered results.

members.name: fetchItems
members.description:
Async callback for fetching items from a remote source.
When set alongside `typeahead`, the picker delegates to this
callback instead of filtering locally.

The callback receives the current query string and an
`AbortSignal` that the component will abort when a newer
query arrives before the previous request completes.

members.name: debounceMs
members.description:
Debounce delay in milliseconds for typeahead input. Defaults
to `0` for local filtering and `300` for remote fetching.

members.description: Return the form submission value. Override in subclasses.

members.description: Return validity state. Override in subclasses.

members.description: Handle form reset. Override in subclasses.

members.name: handlePickerRestore
members.description: Handle form state restore. Override in subclasses.

members.name: resetTypeaheadInput
members.description: Revert the input text to the currently selected item and clear the query.

members.name: formCtrl
members.default: new FormFieldController({ host: this, internals: this.internals, getFormValue: () => this.getPickerFormValue(), getValidity: () => this.getPickerValidity(), onReset: () => this.handlePickerReset(), onRestore: (state: string) => this.handlePickerRestore(state), onDisabled: (isDisabled: boolean) => { this.disabled = isDisabled; }, })

members.description: Called by the browser when the owning `<form>` is reset.

members.name: formStateRestoreCallback
members.description: Called by the browser to restore form state (bfcache, etc.).

members.name: formDisabledCallback
members.description: Called by the browser when a parent `<fieldset>` is enabled or disabled.

members.name: KEYBOARD_THRESHOLD
members.description:
Minimum viewport‐height drop (px) before we treat it as a
mobile keyboard event rather than an address-bar toggle or
animation micro-frame. Mobile keyboards are typically
200–400 px; address bars are ≈ 50–80 px.

members.name: _lastViewportHeight
members.description:
Viewport height baseline for detecting significant shrink
events while the menu is open.  Set when the menu opens and
updated when we act on a shrink or the viewport grows.  Small
shrinks (< `KEYBOARD_THRESHOLD` px) are ignored so that
only real keyboard events trigger scroll adjustment.

members.name: popoverCtrl
members.default: new PopoverController({ host: this, closeStrategy: "source-identity", isOpen: () => this._showOptions, onShouldClose: () => { this.showOptions = false; }, onPositionUpdate: () => { this.pickerMenu.value?.updatePosition(this); const vpH = window.innerHeight || document.documentElement.clientHeight; if (this._lastViewportHeight !== 0 && vpH !== this._lastViewportHeight) { const shrunk = this._lastViewportHeight - vpH; if (shrunk >= EspalierPickerBase.KEYBOARD_THRESHOLD) { // Viewport shrank significantly since we last acted (e.g. // mobile keyboard appeared). Scroll to keep the popover // visible, then update the baseline. this._scrollToContainPopover(); this._lastViewportHeight = vpH; } else if (shrunk < 0) { // Viewport grew — update the baseline so the next shrink // is measured from the current (larger) viewport. this._lastViewportHeight = vpH; } // Small shrinks (< KEYBOARD_THRESHOLD): leave baseline // unchanged so the delta accumulates across animation // frames until it hits the threshold. This avoids reacting // to address-bar show/hide or keyboard animation // micro-frames. } }, getInsideElements: () => [this.pickerMenu.value ?? null], onOutsideClick: () => { this.showOptions = false; }, })

members.name: name
members.description: The name used when the picker participates in a `<form>`.

members.name: required
members.description:
When true, a selection must be made before the form can
be submitted.

members.name: requiredMessage
members.description:
A custom message to display when the picker is required but no
selection has been made. Defaults to
`"Please select an option."` for `esp-pick-one` and
`"Please select at least one option."` for `esp-pick-some`
when not set.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When true, the picker is disabled and cannot be interacted with.

members.name: pickerItems
members.description:
The list of picker items to display in the picker.
It can either be bound programmatically or computed
from the child `esp-picker-item` elements.

members.name: placeholder
members.description:
The text that is displayed in the input prompting the user
to choose options.

members.name: width
members.description:
An explicit width for the picker trigger. Accepts any valid
CSS width value (e.g. `"5em"`, `"80px"`). When set, the host
element is sized to this width while the dropdown menu is
allowed to be wider to accommodate longer items.

```html
<esp-pick-one width="5em">
  <esp-picker-item text="Apple" value="apple"></esp-picker-item>
</esp-pick-one>
```

members.name: focus
members.description: Focus the picker.

members.name: validate
members.description: Re-run constraint validation and dispatch `validity-changed`.

members.name: checkValidity
members.description: Check whether the current state is valid (delegates to ElementInternals).

members.name: updateActiveDescendant
members.description:
Update `ariaActiveDescendantElement` on the input to point
at the currently highlighted picker item.

members.name: clearActiveDescendant
members.description: Clear `ariaActiveDescendantElement` on the input.

members.name: _scrollToContainPopover
members.description:
If the picker trigger + menu extend beyond the viewport,
scroll the page so both are visible.  Called when the
viewport shrinks by ≥ KEYBOARD_THRESHOLD while the menu is open
(e.g. mobile keyboard appears).  Small changes such as
address-bar toggles or animation micro-frames are filtered
out by the threshold in `onPositionUpdate`.

members.name: extractSlotItems
members.description:
Schedule extraction of `esp-picker-item` elements from the slot.
Deferred to a microtask so that multiple synchronous slot
mutations (e.g. several `appendChild` calls in a row) are
batched into a single extraction.

The slot is kept alive so that future updates (e.g. Lit
re-rendering with different items) trigger another
`slotchange` and are picked up automatically.

exists as an attribute for styling purposes.

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: The selector of the element to look for

members.description:
Traverse up the DOM tree to find the closest element that
matches the selector. This method is aware of shadow DOM

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

declarations.description:
Component for selecting multiple options from a list of options.

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-form-item label="Pick some fruit">
    <esp-pick-some>
      <esp-picker-item text="Apple" value="apple" icon="apple"></esp-picker-item>
      <esp-picker-item text="Cherry" value="cherry" icon="cherry"></esp-picker-item>
      <esp-picker-item text="Lemon" value="banana" icon="lemon"></esp-picker-item>
    </esp-pick-some>
  </esp-form-item>
</esp-box>
```

### Initial state (HTML)

Set the initially selected items using the `selected` attribute
on the `esp-picker-item` elements:

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-form-item label="Pick a fruit">
    <esp-pick-some>
      <esp-picker-item text="Apple" value="apple" icon="apple"></esp-picker-item>
      <esp-picker-item text="Cherry" value="cherry" icon="cherry" selected></esp-picker-item>
      <esp-picker-item text="Lemon" value="lemon" icon="lemon" selected></esp-picker-item>
    </esp-pick-some>
  </esp-form-item>
</esp-box>
```

### Initial state (programmatic / pickerItems)

When populating options via the `pickerItems` property (common
in Lit templates that load data from an API), set `selected: true`
on items that should be pre-selected. The picker automatically
detects items with `selected: true` and renders them as chips:

```ts
const allTopics = await fetchTopics();
const mySelectedIds = new Set(user.topicIds);

picker.pickerItems = allTopics.map(t => ({
  text: t.name,
  value: t.id,
  selected: mySelectedIds.has(t.id),
}));
```

This works with all modes — plain, typeahead (local), and
typeahead (remote via `fetchItems`). The component reads the
`selected` flag from `pickerItems` during `willUpdate()` and
populates `selectedItems` automatically. **Do not** separately
assign `selectedItems` — just set `selected: true` on the
items in `pickerItems`.

### Typeahead (local filtering)

Add the `typeahead` attribute to let the user type to filter
the option list. The input becomes editable and the picker
switches to a filter icon on focus:

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-form-item label="Pick some fruit">
    <esp-pick-some typeahead>
      <esp-picker-item text="Apple" value="apple"></esp-picker-item>
      <esp-picker-item text="Cherry" value="cherry"></esp-picker-item>
      <esp-picker-item text="Lemon" value="lemon"></esp-picker-item>
      <esp-picker-item text="Banana" value="banana"></esp-picker-item>
      <esp-picker-item text="Mango" value="mango"></esp-picker-item>
    </esp-pick-some>
  </esp-form-item>
</esp-box>
```

### Typeahead (remote filtering)

Set the `fetchItems` property to a callback that returns
matching items from a server. The picker debounces input
(300 ms by default) and shows a loading indicator while
the request is in flight. Use the provided `AbortSignal`
to cancel stale requests:

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-form-item label="Search users">
    <esp-pick-some typeahead placeholder="Type a name…">
    </esp-pick-some>
  </esp-form-item>
</esp-box>
<script>
  const picker = findByTagName("esp-pick-some")[0];
  picker.fetchItems = async (query, signal) => {
    const res = await fetch("/assets/data/grid-data.json", { signal });
    const all = await res.json();
    const q = query.toLowerCase();
    return all
      .filter(u =>
        u.first_name.toLowerCase().includes(q) ||
        u.last_name.toLowerCase().includes(q))
      .slice(0, 20)
      .map(u => ({
        text: `${u.first_name} ${u.last_name}`,
        value: String(u.id),
      }));
  };
</script>
```

### Add new value

Set the `addNewValue` property to a callback that creates
a new picker item from the typed text. When the user types
a value that does not match any existing item, the dropdown
caret becomes a plus icon. Clicking the plus (or pressing
Enter) invokes the callback and adds the returned item as
a checked selection:

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-form-item label="Pick some tags">
    <esp-pick-some typeahead placeholder="Type to search or add…">
      <esp-picker-item text="Bug" value="bug"></esp-picker-item>
      <esp-picker-item text="Feature" value="feature"></esp-picker-item>
      <esp-picker-item text="Docs" value="docs"></esp-picker-item>
    </esp-pick-some>
  </esp-form-item>
</esp-box>
<script>
  const picker = findByTagName("esp-pick-some")[0];
  picker.addNewValue = (text) => ({
    text,
    value: text.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, "-"),
    selected: true,
  });
</script>
```

members.name: typeaheadCtrl
members.default: new TypeaheadController({ host: this, onFilteredItemsChanged: (items) => { // Create new item objects with selection state from // selectedItems so the menu shows correct checkmarks. // We must NOT mutate the original items — pickerItems may // carry `selected: true` flags that the willUpdate() lifecycle // reads to populate the initial chip selection. const selectedValues = new Set(this.selectedItems.map((i) => i.value)); this.filteredItems = items.map((item) => ({ ...item, selected: selectedValues.has(item.value), })); }, onLoadingChanged: (loading) => { this.typeaheadLoading = loading; }, })

members.name: addNewValue
members.description:
Callback for adding a new value when the user types text
that does not match any existing item. The callback receives
the typed text and should return a `PickerItem` (or a
`Promise<PickerItem>`). The returned item is immediately
added to the list as a checked selection.

Requires `typeahead` to be enabled.

members.description:
Whether the plus icon should be shown (add-new-value mode).
True when the addNewValue callback is set, the input has
non-empty text, and no item exactly matches the query
(case-insensitive).

Checks both `pickerItems` (canonical list) and, in remote
mode, `filteredItems` (current server results) so the plus
icon does not appear when either source already contains an
exact match.

out by the threshold in `onPositionUpdate`.

members.name: extractSlotItems
members.description:
Schedule extraction of `esp-picker-item` elements from the slot.
Deferred to a microtask so that multiple synchronous slot
mutations (e.g. several `appendChild` calls in a row) are
batched into a single extraction.

The slot is kept alive so that future updates (e.g. Lit
re-rendering with different items) trigger another
`slotchange` and are picked up automatically.

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

members.name: KEYBOARD_THRESHOLD
members.description:
Minimum viewport‐height drop (px) before we treat it as a
mobile keyboard event rather than an address-bar toggle or
animation micro-frame. Mobile keyboards are typically
200–400 px; address bars are ≈ 50–80 px.

members.name: _lastViewportHeight
members.description:
Viewport height baseline for detecting significant shrink
events while the menu is open.  Set when the menu opens and
updated when we act on a shrink or the viewport grows.  Small
shrinks (< `KEYBOARD_THRESHOLD` px) are ignored so that
only real keyboard events trigger scroll adjustment.

members.name: popoverCtrl
members.default: new PopoverController({ host: this, closeStrategy: "source-identity", isOpen: () => this._showOptions, onShouldClose: () => { this.showOptions = false; }, onPositionUpdate: () => { this.pickerMenu.value?.updatePosition(this); const vpH = window.innerHeight || document.documentElement.clientHeight; if (this._lastViewportHeight !== 0 && vpH !== this._lastViewportHeight) { const shrunk = this._lastViewportHeight - vpH; if (shrunk >= EspalierPickerBase.KEYBOARD_THRESHOLD) { // Viewport shrank significantly since we last acted (e.g. // mobile keyboard appeared). Scroll to keep the popover // visible, then update the baseline. this._scrollToContainPopover(); this._lastViewportHeight = vpH; } else if (shrunk < 0) { // Viewport grew — update the baseline so the next shrink // is measured from the current (larger) viewport. this._lastViewportHeight = vpH; } // Small shrinks (< KEYBOARD_THRESHOLD): leave baseline // unchanged so the delta accumulates across animation // frames until it hits the threshold. This avoids reacting // to address-bar show/hide or keyboard animation // micro-frames. } }, getInsideElements: () => [this.pickerMenu.value ?? null], onOutsideClick: () => { this.showOptions = false; }, })

members.name: maxAvailableHeight
members.description: The maximum height (px) the menu may occupy, computed in `positionSelf`.

members.name: lastViewportHeight
members.description:
The viewport height (px) used when `maxAvailableHeight` was last
computed.  `updatePosition` compares against this so it only
recalculates height when the viewport itself changes — not on
every scroll tick (where only the trigger's rect shifts).

members.name: label
members.description:
An accessible label for the listbox, typically passed from
the parent picker's placeholder or form-item label.

members.name: multiSelect
members.description:
Whether the menu is in multi or single selection mode.

### Single selection:

```html
<esp-picker-menu>
  <esp-picker-item text="Apple" value="apple" icon="apple"></esp-picker-item>
  <esp-picker-item text="Cherry" value="cherry" icon="cherry"></esp-picker-item>
  <esp-picker-item text="Lemon" value="banana" icon="lemon"></esp-picker-item>
</esp-picker-menu>
```

### Multi-selection:

```html
<esp-picker-menu multi-select="true">
  <esp-picker-item text="Apple" value="apple" icon="apple"></esp-picker-item>
  <esp-picker-item text="Cherry" value="cherry" icon="cherry"></esp-picker-item>
  <esp-picker-item text="Lemon" value="banana" icon="lemon"></esp-picker-item>
</esp-picker-menu>
```

members.name: loading
members.description:
When true, the menu shows a "Searching..." indicator instead
of the item list. Used by typeahead mode during remote fetch.

members.name: emptyMessage
members.description:
Message to display when the item list is empty (e.g.
`"No matches"`).  When empty string, no message is shown.

members.name: pickerItems
members.description: Get all picker items in the picker.

members.name: setHighlight
members.description:
Clamp `highlightIndex`, scroll the virtualizer, and apply
the `.highlighted` CSS class.  This is synchronous — use it
when the menu is already open and the virtualizer has items
in the DOM (i.e. keyboard navigation).

members.name: setHighlightAsync
members.description:
Same as setHighlight but awaits the virtualizer's
`layoutComplete` promise first. Use this when the menu has
just been shown and the virtualizer hasn't rendered items yet.

members.name: applyHighlightClass
members.description:
Clear any existing highlight class, then add it to the
element at the current `highlightIndex`.

members.name: resetHighlight
members.description:
Reset the highlight index, clearing any visual highlight.
Called when the menu is opened to start fresh.

members.name: applyHighlightWithRetry
members.description:
Try to apply the highlight class, retrying up to `remaining`
times on subsequent animation frames if the target element
isn't in the DOM yet (the virtualizer may still be rendering).

members.name: getHighlightedElement
members.description:
Returns the `esp-picker-item` DOM element at the current
`highlightIndex`, or `null` if no element is rendered at
that index. Used by the parent picker to set
`ariaActiveDescendantElement`.

members.name: positionSelf

parameters.name: relativeTo
parameters.description: The element to place the picker above or below.

members.description:
Position the picker menu above or below the given element depending
on whether or not there is more room above or below the element.
Uses the Popover API to render in the top layer, escaping any
ancestor `overflow: hidden` clipping.

//TODO: Show the menu full screen on mobile...

members.name: resizeAndHighlight
members.description:
Serialized resize-then-highlight flow.  Resizing must finish
before the highlight scrolls, otherwise `scrollToIndex` runs
while the host is still small and creates a stale scroll
offset that persists after the host expands.

members.name: sizeToContent
members.description:
Measure the total content height via measureContentHeight
and set the host height to match, capped at `maxHeight`.

members.name: shrinkToFit
members.description:
After the virtualizer has laid out items, shrink the host if
the actual content is shorter than the allocated height. This
prevents a white gap below short lists (e.g. 3 items) while
keeping the full height for long lists that scroll.

members.name: measureContentHeight
members.description:
Compute the border-box height needed to contain all picker
items.  Uses a hybrid strategy:

- **All items rendered** (`renderedCount >= totalCount`):
  pixel-perfect measurement via the last item's bounding
  rect relative to the virtualizer top.

- **Partial render** (`renderedCount < totalCount`):
  falls back to the virtualizer's `scrollHeight`, which
  the flow layout computes from measured items plus an
  average-size estimate for off-screen items.

Both branches add the host's vertical border because the
host uses `border-box` sizing.

members.name: widenToFitContent
members.description:
After the virtualizer has laid out items, widen the menu if
any item's content is wider than the trigger element. This
allows narrow pickers (e.g. time-picker columns) to show
full item text in the dropdown. The menu is clamped so it
does not overflow the right edge of the viewport.

members.name: updatePosition
members.description:
Lightweight position update for scroll tracking. Only updates
the coordinates — and recalculates max-height only when the
viewport height itself has changed (e.g. mobile keyboard),
not on every scroll tick where only the trigger's rect shifts.

members.name: hideMenu
members.description: Hide the picker menu popover.

The key press
to respond to.

attributes.name: loading
attributes.description:
When true, the menu shows a "Searching..." indicator instead
of the item list. Used by typeahead mode during remote fetch.

attributes.name: empty-message
attributes.description:
Message to display when the item list is empty (e.g.

declarations.name: computeHighlightRanges
declarations.description:
Find all case-insensitive occurrences of `needle` within `haystack`
and return an array of `[start, end]` ranges suitable for
`PickerItem.highlightRanges`.

Reactive controller that manages typeahead filtering and remote
fetching for a picker component.

- **Local mode** (default): filters the canonical `allItems`
  list by case-insensitive substring match on `item.text`.
- **Remote mode**: when a `fetchItems` callback is set, debounces
  the query and delegates to the callback. Uses `AbortController`
  to cancel in-flight requests when new input arrives.

Both modes compute `highlightRanges` on the output items so the
picker can visually highlight matched text.
declarations.name: TypeaheadController

members.name: setAllItems
members.description: Store the canonical (unfiltered) items. Re-filters if a query is active.

members.name: setFetchItems
members.description: Set or clear the remote fetch callback.

members.name: setDebounceMs
members.description: Override the debounce interval (milliseconds).

members.name: setQuery
members.description: Update the search query. Debounces, then runs local filter or remote fetch.

members.name: clearQuery
members.description: Clear the query and restore the full items list.

members.name: resetQuery
members.description:
Reset internal query state without emitting an items change.
The currently displayed result set is preserved so the menu
can reuse it on the next open (e.g. after a remote selection).

members.name: currentQuery
members.description: The current search text.

Fetch the default / initial result set from the remote source
by sending an empty-string query.  No-op in local mode or if

members.name: resolvedDebounceMs

declarations.description:
Display a contextual UI overlay that appears when a user interacts with an element, providing a temporary space for additional information, options, or actions without blocking the underlying content. Uses the native Popover API to render in the browser's top layer, escaping all ancestor `overflow` and `z-index` contexts. ```html

I'm a popover!

It can have anything in it.

I'm a nested popover!

I will not cause my parent popover to close.

I'm a very nested popover!

I will not cause my parent popovers to close.

 ```
declarations.name: EspalierPopover

slots.description: The target listens for events to show the popover.
slots.name: target

slots.description: The content to be shown in a popover.
slots.name: popover

members.name: popoverId
members.description:
A stable unique ID for the popover content element.
Useful for wiring `aria-describedby` or `aria-controls`
on the trigger.

members.name: popoverCtrl
members.default: new PopoverController({ host: this, closeStrategy: "skip-list", isOpen: () => this.popoverOpen, onShouldClose: () => this.closePopover(), onPositionUpdate: () => this.positionPopover(), })

members.name: attach
members.description:
Attach determines which side of the target the popover will appear on. The popover will try to fit itself on the specified side, but if there is not enough space, it may appear on the other side. It can be one of the following: #### Place above ```html

POP!

 ``` #### Place below (default) ```html

POP!

 ``` #### Place to left ```html

POP!

 ``` #### Place to right ```html

POP!

 ```

members.name: align
members.description:
Align determines how the popover is aligned relative to the target. For example, if attach is "below" and align is "start", the popover will appear below the target, aligned to the left edge of the target. #### Place below, align start ```html

POP!

 ``` #### Place below, align center ```html

POP!

 ``` #### Place below, align end ```html

POP!

 ``` #### Place right, align start ```html

POP!

 ``` #### Place right, align center ```html

POP!

 ``` #### Place right, align end ```html

POP!

 ```

members.name: offset
members.description:
The offset value determines the distance between the popover and the target when the popover is shown. ```html

I'm a popover!

I am offset 50px from the target.

 ```

members.name: offsetX
members.description:
Additional horizontal pixel offset applied after the
popover is positioned. Positive moves right, negative left.
Useful for anchoring a popover to a dynamic point within
a large target element.

members.name: offsetY
members.description:
Additional vertical pixel offset applied after the
popover is positioned. Positive moves down, negative up.
Useful for anchoring a popover to a dynamic point within
a large target element.

members.name: trigger
members.description:
The event to listen for on the target to open the popover.

- `"click"` — toggle on click (default).
- `"hover"` — open on mouseenter, close on mouseleave.
- `"focus"` — open on focusin, close on focusout.
- `"focus-hover"` — open on hover *or* focus; close only
  when both hover and focus are lost. Ideal for tooltips.
- `"none"` — no automatic trigger; open/close programmatically.

members.name: showDelay
members.description:
Delay in milliseconds before showing the popover after
the trigger event fires. Useful for tooltips so they don't
flash on quick mouse-overs.

members.name: hideDelay
members.description:
Delay in milliseconds before hiding the popover after the
trigger condition is lost. Gives the user time to move the
cursor from the trigger into the popover content.

members.name: closePopover
members.description: Close the popover.

members.name: openPopover
members.description:
Open the popover programmatically (without going through a
trigger event). Respects `show-delay`.

members.name: updatePosition
members.description:
Re-position the popover without closing/reopening it.
Useful after changing `offset-x` / `offset-y` while the
popover is already visible.

members.name: positionPopover
members.description:
Position the popover relative to the target element using
fixed viewport coordinates. The popover renders in the
browser's top layer via the native Popover API.

events.name: popover-opened
events.description: Emitted when the popover is opened.
Emitted when the popover is closed.
events.name: popover-closed

cssProperties.description: Diameter of the circular progress ring in circle mode. Automatically set by the `size` attribute.
cssProperties.name: --esp-progress-circle-size

cssProperties.description: Color of the progress fill. Defaults to the active action hue at the semantic muted lightness so the filled and unfilled portions remain distinguishable in light and dark themes.
cssProperties.name: --esp-progress-fill-color

cssProperties.description: Font size for the value text. Defaults to `var(--esp-type-small)`.
cssProperties.name: --esp-progress-font-size

cssProperties.description: Height of the progress bar track. Automatically set by the `size` attribute.
cssProperties.name: --esp-progress-height

cssProperties.description: Color of the percentage text in both bar and circle modes. Defaults to `var(--esp-color-text)`.

members.name: value
members.description:
The current progress value. Set to `null` (or omit) for
indeterminate mode. Values are clamped to `0..max`.

members.name: max
members.description: The maximum value. Defaults to `100`.

members.name: label
members.description:
An accessible label describing what the progress bar
represents. Required for screen readers.

members.name: showValue
members.description:
Show the percentage value as text. In bar mode the text
appears below the track; in circle mode it appears in the
center of the ring.

members.name: size
members.description:
Size preset for the progress indicator.

In bar mode:
- `small` — 6px
- `medium` — 12px (default)
- `large` — 20px

In circle mode:
- `small` — 48px diameter
- `medium` — 80px diameter (default)
- `large` — 120px diameter

members.name: mode
members.description:
Display mode for the progress indicator.

- `bar` — linear progress bar (default)
- `circle` — circular SVG ring

members.name: variantTokenOriginalValues
members.default: new Map< string, { generatedPriority: string; generatedValue: string; originalPriority: string; originalValue: string | null; } >()

members.name: scheme
members.description:
The scheme to use for the component. This property is used internally
to control the light/dark scheme so it matches the scheme of
[esp-root](/components/root). It should not be set manually, it
exists as an attribute for styling purposes.

members.name: variant
members.description: The [color variant](/guides/color/variants) of the element.

members.name: traverseToClosest

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: The selector of the element to look for

members.description:
Traverse up the DOM tree to find the closest element that
matches the selector. This method is aware of shadow DOM
boundaries and will traverse through them to find the element.

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

declarations.description:
Groups multiple [esp-radio-button](/components/radio-button)
components and ensures only one can be selected at a time.
Works with [esp-form-item](/components/form-item) for label
and error message integration.

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-form-item label="Choose a size">
    <esp-radio-button-group>
      <esp-radio-button value="small">Small</esp-radio-button>
      <esp-radio-button value="medium" checked>Medium</esp-radio-button>
      <esp-radio-button value="large">Large</esp-radio-button>
    </esp-radio-button-group>
  </esp-form-item>
</esp-box>
```

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-form-item label="Disabled group">
    <esp-radio-button-group disabled>
      <esp-radio-button value="a">Option A</esp-radio-button>
      <esp-radio-button value="b" checked>Option B</esp-radio-button>
      <esp-radio-button value="c">Option C</esp-radio-button>
    </esp-radio-button-group>
  </esp-form-item>
</esp-box>
```
declarations.name: EspalierRadioButtonGroup

slots.description: Place `esp-radio-button` elements in the default slot.

members.name: formCtrl
members.default: new FormFieldController({ host: this, internals: this.internals, getFormValue: () => this.value || null, getValidity: () => { if (this.required && !this.value) { return { flags: { valueMissing: true }, message: this.requiredMessage || "Please select an option.", }; } return null; }, onReset: () => { for (const rb of this.getRadioButtons()) { rb.checked = false; } }, onRestore: (state: string) => { for (const rb of this.getRadioButtons()) { rb.checked = rb.value === state; } }, onDisabled: (isDisabled: boolean) => { this.disabled = isDisabled; }, })

members.name: name
members.description: The name used when the group participates in a `<form>`.

members.name: required
members.description:
When true, one radio button must be selected before the
form can be submitted.

members.name: requiredMessage
members.description:
A custom message to display when the group is required but no
radio button is selected. Defaults to
`"Please select an option."` when not set.

members.name: disabled
members.description: Disables all child radio buttons in the group.

members.name: value
members.description:
Returns the value of the currently selected radio button,
or an empty string if none is selected.

members.name: focus
members.description:
Focus the checked radio button, or the first non-disabled
radio button if none is checked.

members.name: validate
members.description: Re-run constraint validation and dispatch `validity-changed`.

members.name: checkValidity
members.description: Check whether the current state is valid (delegates to ElementInternals).

members.description: Called by the browser when the owning `<form>` is reset.

members.name: formStateRestoreCallback
members.description: Called by the browser to restore form state (bfcache, etc.).

members.name: formDisabledCallback
members.description: Called by the browser when a parent `<fieldset>` is enabled or disabled.

declarations.description:
A radio button control for choosing one option from a set.
Uses Tabler SVG icons for selected and unselected states.
Should be used inside an
[esp-radio-button-group](/components/radio-button/group)
for single-select behavior.

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-form-item label="Favorite color">
    <esp-radio-button-group>
      <esp-radio-button value="red">Red</esp-radio-button>
      <esp-radio-button value="green">Green</esp-radio-button>
      <esp-radio-button value="blue">Blue</esp-radio-button>
    </esp-radio-button-group>
  </esp-form-item>
</esp-box>
```

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-radio-button-group>
    <esp-radio-button checked value="small">Small</esp-radio-button>
    <esp-radio-button value="medium">Medium</esp-radio-button>
    <esp-radio-button disabled value="large">Large</esp-radio-button>
  </esp-radio-button-group>
</esp-box>
```

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

declarations.description:
General-purpose virtualized repeater for rich repeated content such as
cards, image rows, or other custom-element item layouts. Consumers
provide the item renderer and own the visual design of each repeated item.

Because the list is virtualized, repeated item content is supplied through
the `renderItem` property instead of light-DOM children. This lets the
repeater create item DOM on demand as the user scrolls.

Use `layout="grid"` to opt into responsive card layouts. Consumers can
provide a fixed fallback with `grid-columns` and override the live column
count with `--esp-repeater-grid-columns` in media queries.

Use `scroll-model="page"` when the repeater should participate in the
document's natural page scroll instead of owning a nested scrolling
viewport. In page mode, `list-height` is ignored.
declarations.name: EspalierRepeater

members.name: items
members.description:
Items to render when the repeater is used without cursor paging.
If `fetchPage` is set, the repeater manages its own loaded item
collection internally.

members.name: renderItem
members.description:
Callback used to render each repeated item. This is the recommended
API for rich custom-element content because the repeater virtualizes
items and instantiates them on demand.

members.name: layout
members.description:
Visual layout mode for the rendered content.

Use `grid` to opt into responsive card-style rows while
preserving virtualization and paging behavior.

members.name: scrollModel
members.description:
Scroll behavior model for the repeater viewport.

Use `contained` for the current nested scrolling behavior driven by
`list-height`. Use `page` when the repeater should scroll with the
document/page instead.

members.name: gridColumns
members.description:
Fixed grid column count fallback used when `layout="grid"`
and no `--esp-repeater-grid-columns` custom property override
is applied.

members.name: ariaLabel
members.description: Accessible label forwarded to the internal virtualized list.

members.name: listHeight
members.description:
The height of the repeater viewport. Accepts any valid CSS height
value. Defaults to `60vh`. Ignored when `scroll-model="page"`.

members.name: pageSize
members.description: Number of items requested per page when `fetchPage` is used.

members.name: prefetchThreshold
members.description:
Number of items from the end of the currently loaded set at which the
repeater should prefetch the next page.

members.name: loading
members.description:
External loading flag for non-paged usage or for cases where the
consumer wants to force a loading state.

members.name: loadingMessage
members.description:
Default loading message used when no custom `loading` or `skeleton`
slot content is provided.

members.name: emptyMessage
members.description:
Default empty-state message used when no custom `empty` slot content
is provided.

members.name: errorMessage
members.description:
Optional consumer-provided error message. When set it takes priority
over internal fetch errors for display purposes.

members.name: fetchPage
members.description:
Provide a callback to enable cursor-based infinite paging. When set,
the repeater fetches additional items as the user scrolls near the end
of the currently loaded range.

members.name: clear
members.description:
Clear the currently rendered items. In cursor-paged mode this also
clears the cursor and any pending load error.

members.name: reload
members.description:
Clear any loaded pages and request the first page again when
cursor paging is enabled.

members.name: scrollToIndex
members.description: Delegate scrolling to the underlying virtualizer.

members.name: variantTokenOriginalValues
members.default: new Map< string, { generatedPriority: string; generatedValue: string; originalPriority: string; originalValue: string | null; } >()

members.name: scheme
members.description:

parameters.name: selector

members.description:
Traverse up the DOM tree to find the closest element that
matches the selector. This method is aware of shadow DOM
boundaries and will traverse through them to find the element.

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve
