declarations.description:
Root container for the Espalier design system. Provides the full design-token pipeline — colors, scales, and fonts — to every descendant component. All tokens are JS-computed from a pair of Base64-encoded theme objects and emitted as CSS custom properties on the host element. ### Theme API Supply a `light-theme` and/or `dark-theme` attribute containing a **Base64-encoded JSON** partial theme. Fields you omit fall back to built-in defaults. Switch between them with `scheme`. ```html

Themed content

 ```
declarations.name: EspalierRoot

slots.description: Place Espalier components and content here.

members.name: correlationId
members.description: Unique ID for correlating bus events across multiple roots.

members.name: lightThemeAttr
members.description:
Base64-encoded JSON partial theme for the **light** scheme. Only fields you wish to override need to be present — all others inherit from the built-in light defaults. ```html

Purple light theme

 ```

members.name: darkThemeAttr
members.description:
Base64-encoded JSON partial theme for the **dark** scheme. ```html

Purple dark theme

 ```

members.name: fontCSSRoot
members.description:
Root path for locally-cached font CSS files used by
`<esp-font-picker>` to load font previews.

The directory must contain the individual font CSS files
generated by `npm run build-fonts`.  These files must be
served at stable, predictable paths (no filename hashing)
because the picker constructs URLs by font family name.

members.name: fontDefinitionsUrl
members.description:
Full URL to the `font-definitions.json` catalog used by
`<esp-font-picker>`.

When set, the picker fetches from this URL instead of
`{fontCSSRoot}font-definitions.json`.  This allows the JSON
to be served through a cache-busting asset pipeline while
the individual font CSS files remain at stable paths.

members.name: googleFontLoading
members.description:
Controls whether Espalier may inject runtime Google Fonts CDN
stylesheet links for selected Google Fonts.

`auto` preserves the default behavior: theme font families and
selected Google font-picker values may load full fonts from
`fonts.googleapis.com`.  `none` prevents those runtime Google
Fonts links, leaving local preview CSS and web-safe font stacks
available for locked-down, offline, or strict-CSP environments.

members.name: iconSpriteUrl
members.description:
Full URL to the SVG sprite used by component-owned `icon`
attributes.

Components append the validated icon name as a fragment, so
this should point to the sprite file itself, without `#icon`.
Use this when your application serves the sprite from a
subpath, CDN, or cache-busted asset pipeline. Set it to an
empty string to disable generated sprite icon URLs for this root.

```html
<esp-root icon-sprite-url="/dist/icons.abc123.svg">
  <esp-button icon="save" label="Save"></esp-button>
</esp-root>
```

members.name: defaultScheme
members.description:
Initial color scheme to apply when `scheme` is not set.

Use `light` or `dark` for a fixed published-site default, or
`system` to follow the visitor's `prefers-color-scheme` before
they toggle the active `scheme`.

members.name: scheme
members.description:
The active color scheme. Light scheme ```html

Light Scheme

 ``` Dark scheme ```html

Dark Scheme

 ```

members.name: seedColor
members.description:
The effective seed color from the active theme.

Read-only — supply a theme attribute with a different
`seedColor` value to change.

members.name: extractFamily
members.description:
Extract the bare font family name from a CSS `font-family`
value like `"Roboto", sans-serif` → `Roboto`.

members.name: injectGoogleFontLink
members.description:
Inject (or replace) a Google Fonts `<link>` for a given slot.

Each slot (`body`, `headings`, `brand`, `monospace`) gets at most one
`<link>` scoped to this root instance by its correlationId,
identified by `data-esp-font="<correlationId>-<slot>"`.
This ensures multiple `<esp-root>` instances on the same page
do not overwrite each other's font links.

members.name: syncGoogleFontLinks
members.description:
Ensure Google Font `<link>` tags match the active theme's
font families.  Called from `willUpdate()` after theme
resolution.

members.name: activeTheme
members.description: Returns the resolved theme for the active scheme.

members.name: resolveThemes
members.description:
Re-merge default → theme-attribute for both schemes.
Called in `willUpdate()` whenever any input property changes.

members.name: resizeObserver
members.default: new ResizeObserver(() => { getEspBus().publish("window-resized", { emWidth: window.innerWidth / parseFloat(getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).fontSize), emHeight: window.innerHeight / parseFloat(getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).fontSize), pxWidth: window.innerWidth, pxHeight: window.innerHeight, }); })

members.name: lightDomStyles
members.default: ` /* ── Base typography ──────────────────────────────────── */ esp-root { font-family: var(--esp-font-body); font-weight: var(--esp-font-weight-body); line-height: 1.5; font-size: var(--esp-type-normal); color: var(--esp-color-text); } /* ── Lists ────────────────────────────────────────────── */ esp-root ul[role="list"], esp-root ol[role="list"] { list-style: none; } esp-root ul li::marker, esp-root ol li::marker { font-weight: bold; } /* ── Form controls ────────────────────────────────────── */ esp-root input, esp-root select, esp-root button { font-family: var(--esp-font-body); } /* ── Links ────────────────────────────────────────────── */ esp-root a:not([class]) { text-decoration-skip-ink: auto; font-weight: bold; color: var(--esp-color-link); } esp-root a { text-decoration: underline; text-decoration-skip-ink: auto; color: var(--esp-color-link); } esp-root a:hover { background-color: var(--esp-color-link-hover-bg); color: var(--esp-color-link-hover); text-decoration: none; } /* ── Inherited text styles ────────────────────────────── */ esp-root input, esp-root button, esp-root textarea, esp-root select, esp-root p, esp-root ul, esp-root li { font-family: var(--esp-font-body); color: var(--esp-color-text); font-size: inherit; } esp-root textarea:not([rows]) { min-height: 10em; } /* ── Headings ─────────────────────────────────────────── */ esp-root h1, esp-root h2, esp-root h3, esp-root h4, esp-root h5 { font-family: var(--esp-font-headings); font-weight: var(--esp-font-weight-headings); color: var(--esp-color-headings); margin: var(--esp-size-small-to-normal) 0 var(--esp-size-tiny-to-small); } esp-root h5 { font-size: var(--esp-type-small); line-height: 1.4; } esp-root h4 { font-size: var(--esp-type-medium); line-height: 1.3; } esp-root h3 { font-size: var(--esp-type-big); line-height: 1.2; } esp-root h2 { font-size: var(--esp-type-large); line-height: 1.15; } esp-root h1 { font-size: var(--esp-type-huge); line-height: 1.1; } /* ── Code / Pre ───────────────────────────────────────── */ esp-root pre, esp-root code { font-family: var(--esp-font-monospace); font-weight: var(--esp-font-weight-monospace); } esp-root code { font-size: var(--esp-type-small); background: var(--esp-color-layer-1); border-radius: var(--esp-size-border-radius); padding: 0 0.35em; display: inline-block; border: 1px dotted var(--esp-color-border); } esp-root pre { background-color: var(--esp-color-layer-1); border-radius: var(--esp-size-border-radius); border: 1px dotted var(--esp-color-border); } esp-root pre > code { font-size: var(--esp-type-tiny); display: block; background: none; border: none; hyphens: none; tab-size: 2; white-space: pre; padding: var(--esp-size-padding); overflow: auto; line-height: 1.8; } /* ── Images ────────────────────────────────────────────── */ esp-root img, esp-root picture { max-width: 100%; display: block; } `
members.description:
Light-DOM styles injected into `document.head` so that
slotted content (headings, links, code blocks, lists, etc.)
inherits the design-system tokens defined on `:host`.
Selectors are scoped to `esp-root` descendants to avoid
leaking into content outside the root.

declarations.description:
Modal search overlay with a Cmd+K / Ctrl+K interaction pattern.

`<esp-search>` is a pure UI shell with no search-engine dependency.
Consumers wire it to any search backend (Pagefind, MiniSearch, API,
etc.) by listening for `search-requested` events and calling
`setResults()` with the matches.

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

events.description: — The user changed the query input. Fires on every keystroke (not debounced) so consumers can track the latest query and discard stale async results. Consumers that need debouncing should apply it themselves (e.g. Pagefind's `debouncedSearch()`). `detail: { query: string }`
events.name: search-requested

events.description: — The user chose a result. `detail: { url: string }`
events.name: result-selected

events.description: — The overlay was closed (Escape, backdrop click, or result selection). Consumers should use this to cancel in-flight searches or discard pending results. `detail: {}`
events.name: search-closed

declarations.name: getEspBus
parseOklch
declarations.description:
Parse an OKLCH CSS string into its components.

Accepts:
- `oklch(0.7 0.125 216)`
- `oklch(70% 0.125 216)`

Returns `null` if the string is not valid OKLCH.

declarations.name: serializeOklch
declarations.description:
Serialize an OKLCH color to a CSS `oklch()` string.
Lightness is emitted as a 0–1 decimal (not percent).

declarations.name: oklchToSRGB
declarations.description:
Convert an OKLCH color to gamma-encoded sRGB.
Channels may be out of [0, 1] if the color is outside the sRGB gamut.

declarations.name: srgbToOklch
declarations.description: Convert a gamma-encoded sRGB color to OKLCH.

declarations.name: isInSRGBGamut
declarations.description: Check whether an OKLCH color falls within the sRGB gamut.

declarations.name: gamutMapToSRGB
declarations.description:
**Gamut-map** an OKLCH color into sRGB by binary-search
reduction of chroma, preserving lightness and hue.

If the color is already in gamut it is returned unchanged.
Otherwise chroma is reduced until the color fits.

declarations.name: clampChromaAndGamutMap

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: The source OKLCH color

parameters.name: min
parameters.description: Minimum chroma (floor)

parameters.name: max
parameters.description: Maximum chroma (ceiling)

declarations.description: Clamp chroma to a [min, max] range, then gamut-map into sRGB.

declarations.name: apcaContrast

parameters.name: textColor
parameters.description: OKLCH color of the text

parameters.name: bgColor
parameters.description: OKLCH color of the background

declarations.description:
Calculate the APCA $L_c$ (Lightness-Contrast) value for a
text/background pair.

declarations.name: ensureContrast

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The text OKLCH color to adjust

parameters.name: bg
parameters.description: The background OKLCH color (fixed)

parameters.name: targetLc
parameters.description: The minimum absolute $L_c$ score (default 60)

declarations.description:
Ensure that a text color achieves at least the target
$|L_c|$ against a background color.

If the current contrast is insufficient, lightness is
adjusted (made darker on light bg, lighter on dark bg)
until the target is met or the lightness boundary is hit.

declarations.name: rotateHue
declarations.description: Rotate a hue angle by `degrees`, wrapping within 0–360.

declarations.name: deriveVariant
declarations.description:
Derive a variant color from a seed by rotating the hue.
Lightness and chroma are preserved from the seed.

declarations.name: deriveSemantic

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: The variant base color (provides hue + source chroma)

parameters.name: lightness
parameters.description: The lightness to apply (0–1)

parameters.name: chromaMin
parameters.description: Minimum chroma floor

parameters.name: chromaMax
parameters.description: Maximum chroma ceiling

declarations.description:
Derive a semantic color from a base (variant) color
by overriding lightness, clamping chroma, and gamut-mapping.

declarations.name: deriveSemanticWithContrast

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: The variant base color

parameters.name: lightness
parameters.description: Desired lightness

parameters.name: chromaMin
parameters.description: Minimum chroma

parameters.name: chromaMax
parameters.description: Maximum chroma

parameters.name: bg
parameters.description: Background color to contrast against

parameters.name: targetLc
parameters.description: Minimum |Lc| score (default 60)

declarations.description:
Derive a semantic color with automatic APCA contrast
enforcement against a reference background.

declarations.description:
Resolve the configured-brand logo and color for the active scheme.
Non-empty scheme values take precedence over generic values. Empty
scheme values are treated as absent so generic configuration and the
consuming component's CSS-token defaults remain available.

declarations.name: renderConfiguredBrand
declarations.description:
Render the shared configured-brand fallback used by header and footer.
Plain anchors and images are intentional: a brand identity should not gain
the interaction chrome of Espalier's application-navigation controls.

Shared base class for Espalier elements.

The reusable `.esp-field` shell used by text inputs, textareas,
pickers, date pickers, buttons, and similar controls exposes the
following shared styling hooks:

declarations.name: ESP_EVENTS

type.text:
{
  // ── Form value events ───────────────────────────────────────
  VALUE_CHANGED: "value-changed",
  VALIDITY_CHANGED: "validity-changed",

  // ── Click events ────────────────────────────────────────────
  CLICKED: "clicked",

  // ── Form submission events ──────────────────────────────────
  ESP_SUBMIT: "esp-submit",
  ESP_SUBMIT_RESPONSE: "esp-submit-response",
  ESP_SUBMIT_ERROR: "esp-submit-error",

  // ── Navigation / layout events ──────────────────────────────
  ESP_TAB_CHANGED: "esp-tab-changed",
  ESP_TAB_UPDATED: "esp-tab-updated",
  ESP_TOGGLE: "esp-toggle",
  ESP_ACCORDION_CHANGE: "esp-accordion-change",
  DRAWER_OPENED: "drawer-opened",
  DRAWER_CLOSED: "drawer-closed",
  ESP_THEME_TOGGLE: "esp-theme-toggle",
  FLYOUT_OPENED: "flyout-opened",
  FLYOUT_CLOSED: "flyout-closed",
  FLYOUT_STATE_CHANGED: "flyout-state-changed",

  // ── Popover DOM events ──────────────────────────────────────
  POPOVER_OPENED: "popover-opened",
  POPOVER_CLOSED: "popover-closed",

  // ── Grid events ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  GRID_EVENT: "grid-event",
  GRID_LOAD_START: "esp-grid-load-start",
  GRID_LOAD_SUCCESS: "esp-grid-load-success",
  GRID_LOAD_ERROR: "esp-grid-load-error",
  GRID_ITEMS_CHANGED: "esp-grid-items-changed",

  // ── Info events ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  DESTROY: "destroy",

  // ── Upload events ───────────────────────────────────────────
  FILE_SELECTED: "file-selected",
  FILE_REMOVED: "file-removed",
  FILES_SELECTED: "files-selected",
  FILES_REJECTED: "files-rejected",
  UPLOAD_RETRY: "upload-retry",
  RETRY_UPLOAD: "retry-upload",
  IMAGES_REORDERED: "images-reordered",

  // ── Search events ───────────────────────────────────────────
  SEARCH_REQUESTED: "search-requested",
  RESULT_SELECTED: "result-selected",
  SEARCH_CLOSED: "search-closed",

  // ── Picker internal events ──────────────────────────────────
  SELECTION_CHANGED: "selection-changed",
  CLOSE_MENU: "close-menu",
  REMOVE_IMAGE: "remove-image",
}

declarations.description:
Canonical event name strings for every CustomEvent in the library.

Use these instead of raw strings to catch typos at compile time:

```ts
el.addEventListener(ESP_EVENTS.VALUE_CHANGED, handler);
```

declarations.name: showFlyout

parameters.name: config
parameters.description: Flyout configuration.

declarations.description:
Open the page's flyout surface. Publishes a `"show-flyout"`
event on `EspBus` which is picked up by `<esp-flyout>`.

```ts
import { showFlyout } from "@taprootio/espalier";

showFlyout({ heading: "Details", content: detailsFragment });
```

If no `<esp-flyout>` is connected, the event goes unserviced —
callers that need a fallback (e.g. opening a URL in a new tab)
should check for a connected flyout themselves.

declarations.name: closeFlyout
declarations.description:
Close the page's flyout surface. Publishes a `"close-flyout"`
event on `EspBus`.

declarations.name: WEIGHT_LABELS
declarations.description:
Human-readable labels for the nine standard CSS `font-weight`
values (100–900).

Useful for building weight-picker UIs where the numeric value
alone isn't descriptive enough.

declarations.name: extractWeights

parameters.name: font
parameters.description: A Google Font entry, or `null`.

declarations.description:
Extract available non-italic weights from a GoogleFont,
sorted numerically.

Google Fonts encodes weights as variant strings: `"regular"` maps
to `"400"`, italic variants (e.g. `"300italic"`) are excluded,
and every other variant string is its numeric weight.

declarations.name: normalizeWeight

parameters.name: weight
parameters.description: The weight to normalize.

parameters.name: defaultWeight
parameters.description: Fallback when `weight` is falsy.

declarations.description:
Normalize a CSS `font-weight` keyword to its numeric equivalent.

- `"bold"` → `"700"`
- `"normal"` → `"400"`
- Falsy / `undefined` → `defaultWeight`
- Everything else passes through unchanged.

declarations.name: bestAvailableWeight

parameters.name: desired
parameters.description: The target weight (numeric string).

parameters.name: available
parameters.description:
Sorted weight strings from {@link extractWeights}.
 *   Typically non-empty — {@link extractWeights}always returns at
 *   least `["400"]`.
 *

declarations.description:
Find the closest available weight to a desired one.

If the desired weight appears in `available`, it is returned
directly.  Otherwise the weight with the smallest absolute
numeric distance is returned.  If `available` is empty, returns
`"400"` as a safe default.

declarations.name: extractFamily
declarations.description:
Extract the first family name from a CSS `font-family` string,
stripping both single and double quotes and whitespace.

declarations.name: getFallbackFont
declarations.description:
Return the generic CSS fallback for a Google Font category.

- `"serif"` → `"serif"`
- `"monospace"` → `"monospace"`
- `"handwriting"` → `"cursive"`
- Everything else (including `"sans-serif"`, `"display"`) →
  `"sans-serif"`

declarations.name: FormFieldController

members.name: syncValue
members.description:
Push the current value into `ElementInternals` for form
participation, then run constraint validation and dispatch
a `validity-changed` event.

Call this whenever the host's value changes.

members.name: syncValueSilently
members.description:
Push the current value into `ElementInternals` and update
validity **without** dispatching any events. Use this when
the form value must stay in sync but no UI feedback should
be shown (e.g. while the user is still typing and no error
is currently displayed).

members.name: validate
members.description:
Run constraint validation against the host's current state
and dispatch a `validity-changed` event.

members.name: checkValidity
members.description:
Check whether the host's current state is valid.
Delegates to `ElementInternals.checkValidity()`.

Some browsers don't auto-expose `checkValidity()` on the
host element itself, so form controls should expose a public
method that delegates here.

members.name: syncValidity
members.description:
Update `ElementInternals` validity from the host's current
state **without** dispatching any events.  Use this when the
internals must reflect the correct validity (e.g. so that
`checkValidity()` works) but no UI feedback should be shown.

members.name: handleFormReset
members.description:
Delegate for the host's `formResetCallback()`.
Calls the `onReset` hook, silently re-syncs the form value
and validity, then dispatches `validity-changed` with
`valid: true` so that `esp-form-item` clears any displayed
errors. The internals are updated to reflect the real
(possibly invalid) state, but the event always reports
valid so the UI returns to its pristine appearance.

members.name: handleFormStateRestore
members.description:
Delegate for the host's `formStateRestoreCallback()`.
Calls the `onRestore` hook, then re-syncs value and validation.

members.name: handleFormDisabled
members.description:
Delegate for the host's `formDisabledCallback()`.
Calls the `onDisabled` hook so the host can update its
disabled property to match the `<fieldset>` state.

declarations.description:
Mirrors an `esp-form-item` hint into the same shadow root as a form
control's real focus target. ARIA ID references do not cross shadow
boundaries, so setting `aria-describedby` on the custom-element host
alone cannot describe an inner input, textarea, or ARIA control.
declarations.name: FormFieldDescriptionController

members.name: setDescription
members.description: Set or clear the description supplied by the wrapping form item.

declarations.name: DEFAULT_ICON_SPRITE_URL
declarations.default: "/assets/icons.svg"

declarations.name: INTENT_VARIANTS
declarations.default: [ "neutral", "success", "warning", "danger", "info", ]

declarations.name: getIconHrefForHost
declarations.default: getIconHref

declarations.name: resetWarnedInvalidIconNames
declarations.description:
Reset the per-process record of which invalid icon names have
already produced a `console.warn`. Tests should call this in a
`beforeEach` (or equivalent) so assertions on warning counts are
not order-dependent across the worker's module-cached state.

declarations.name: calculatePhotoLayout

parameters.name: images
parameters.description: Items with intrinsic `width`/`height` (extra fields preserved)

parameters.name: containerWidth
parameters.description: Available width in px that each row must fill

parameters.name: targetRowHeight
parameters.description: Preferred row height in px before scaling

parameters.name: gap
parameters.description: Horizontal gap in px between images within a row

parameters.name: maxRowHeight
parameters.description:
Ceiling for row heights — keeps a stretched final row
(or a single image) from ballooning past e.g. half the viewport height

declarations.description:
Compute justified layout rows for a list of images.

Algorithm:
1. Add images to a row one at a time, scaled to the target row height
2. When the combined width overflows the container, the row breaks either
   before or after the overflowing image — whichever leaves the row height
   closer to the target. Keeping the image shrinks the row slightly below
   target; pushing it to the next row stretches the row above target.
3. Scale the finished row so it fills the container width exactly.

Every row — including the last — is scaled to fill `containerWidth`
exactly, except rows clamped by `maxRowHeight`: those keep their aspect
ratios at the clamped height and no longer span the full width. Items are
returned by reference in their original order.

declarations.name: FOCUSABLE_SELECTOR
declarations.description:
Selector for elements that can receive focus. Used by the
focus trap to determine which elements participate in the
Tab cycle inside an overlay.

Shared reactive controller for full-screen modal overlays such as
`esp-dialog` and `esp-lightbox`. Manages:

- **Inert siblings** — marks sibling content `inert` while the
  overlay is open, with shadow-root traversal to find the correct
  anchor. After drop-zone promotion the inert anchor is the
  drop-zone element itself so its siblings (main page content) are
  correctly inerted. Uses reference-counted locking so nested
  overlays don't prematurely un-inert background content when the
  inner overlay closes.
- **Focus trap** — constrains Tab / Shift+Tab to cycle only
  through focusable elements inside the overlay, including slotted
  light-DOM content and one level of child shadow roots.
- **Focus save / restore** — captures `activeElement` on open
  and restores it on close.
- **Body scroll lock** — reference-counted lock on
  `document.body.style.overflow` that supports nested overlays
  and preserves any pre-existing overflow value.
- **Dialog drop-zone promotion** — moves the host into
  `<esp-page>`'s dialog drop-zone (if present) so the overlay
  escapes nested stacking contexts. Re-appends on every open so
  the most recently opened overlay is last in DOM order. Waits
  for `esp-page` to be defined if it hasn't upgraded yet.
- **Disconnect cleanup** — restores inert + scroll if the host
  is removed from the DOM while open.
declarations.name: OverlayController

members.name: _pendingReactivation
members.description:
Set by `hostDisconnected()` when it tears down a still-open
overlay (a DOM move). The next `open()` is then a reactivation
and preserves `_previouslyFocused` instead of recapturing; a
`close()` in any state cancels it.

members.name: _dropZone
members.description:
After promotion into the dialog drop-zone, this stores the
drop-zone element so `_setInert` can use it as the anchor
(inert the drop-zone's siblings — i.e. the main page content —
rather than siblings of the host within the drop-zone).

members.name: _promoting
members.description:
`true` while the host is being moved to the dialog drop-zone.
During this window the browser fires `disconnectedCallback` /
`connectedCallback` synchronously. The controller (and host)
must skip teardown/setup during a promotion move.

members.name: isPromoting
members.description:
Whether the host is currently being moved to the dialog
drop-zone. Components should check this in their own
`disconnectedCallback` / `connectedCallback` to avoid
tearing down state (e.g. click handlers) during a DOM move.

members.name: isOpen
members.description: Whether the overlay is currently open.

members.name: open
members.description:
Open the overlay: save focus, lock scroll, promote to the
dialog drop-zone, then set siblings inert.

Promotion happens **before** inert so that `_setInert` operates
on the final DOM position (inside the dialog drop-zone) rather
than the original location. If promotion is deferred (waiting
for `esp-page` to upgrade), inert is applied immediately at the
current position and then recomputed after the async promotion
completes.

If called while already open this is a no-op — callers should
handle navigation (e.g. lightbox image changes) themselves.

members.name: close
members.description:
Close the overlay: unlock scroll, restore inert state, and
return focus to the previously focused element.

members.name: trapFocus
members.description:
Constrain Tab / Shift+Tab to cycle only through focusable
elements inside the overlay container. Call this from the
host's keydown handler when `ev.key === "Tab"`.

members.name: moveFocusInto
members.description:
Move focus into the overlay. Focuses the first autofocus
descendant, then the first focusable descendant; if none exist
falls back to `getFocusFallback` (if provided), then to the
container itself.

members.name: _getAutofocusElement
members.description:
Query the first autofocus element inside the overlay container,
including slotted light-DOM content and one level of child
shadow roots.

members.name: getFocusableElements
members.description:
Query all focusable elements inside the overlay container,
including those inside shadow roots of child custom elements
and slotted light-DOM content.

members.name: _setInert
members.description:
Toggle `inert` on siblings so background content cannot receive
focus, be read by screen readers, or be interacted with.

The anchor element — whose siblings are inerted — is determined
by priority:
1. If promoted into a dialog drop-zone, the drop-zone itself
   is the anchor so its siblings (main page content) are
   inerted.
2. Otherwise the host element is the anchor, with shadow-root
   traversal to find the nearest light-DOM parent.

members.name: _resolveInertAnchor
members.description:
Determine the correct anchor element for inert management.

After drop-zone promotion, the anchor is the drop-zone element
so we inert the drop-zone's siblings (which are the main page
content elements inside the esp-page shadow root).

Without promotion, walk up through shadow hosts from the host
element to find the nearest light-DOM ancestor whose siblings
should be marked inert.

members.name: _getDeepActiveElement
members.description:
Walk `activeElement` down through nested shadow roots to find
the deepest focused element. `document.activeElement` reports
the host element in shadow-DOM contexts, not the actual focused
node inside the shadow tree.

members.name: _promoteToDropZone
members.description:
Move the host into `<esp-page>`'s dialog drop-zone so it
escapes nested stacking contexts (e.g. fixed headers). If the
host is already in the drop-zone, re-appends it so it becomes
the last child (top-most in DOM order when z-indexes tie).

Waits for the `esp-page` custom element to be defined so this
works regardless of script load order. If promotion happens
asynchronously, inert state is recomputed against the new DOM
position.

members.name: _tryPromote
members.description:
Attempt to call `AddDialog` on the esp-page element.
Returns `true` if promotion succeeded (first time only).

declarations.description:
Shared reactive controller that manages the duplicated lifecycle
scaffolding for any component with a popover / dropdown:

1. **Bus coordination** — subscribes to `"close-popovers"` on
   connect, unsubscribes on disconnect.
2. **Scroll / resize tracking** — rAF-throttled listeners that
   call back for repositioning (and optionally auto-close when
   the trigger leaves the viewport).
3. **Outside-click dismiss** — capture-phase document click
   listener with shadow-DOM–aware containment checks.
declarations.name: PopoverController

members.name: publishCloseOthers
members.description: Publish `close-popovers` identifying this host as the source.

members.name: publishCloseExcept
members.description: Publish `close-popovers` keeping the listed elements open.

declarations.name: STEP_NAMES
declarations.default: ["tiny", "small", "normal", "medium", "big", "large", "huge"]
declarations.description: Ordered scale step names shared by type and spacing scales.

declarations.name: DEFAULT_SCALE_CONFIG
declarations.default: { rootFontSize: 16, typeRatio: 1.25, spaceRatio: 1.618, borderRadius: 0.2, viewportMin: 320, viewportMax: 1200, }
declarations.description: Sensible defaults matching the existing Espalier system.

declarations.name: generateScaleProperties

parameters.name: config
parameters.description: Scale configuration (should already be merged with defaults).

declarations.description:
Generate all scale-related CSS custom properties.

Returns a flat `Record<string, string>` keyed by custom-property
name (including the `--` prefix) with computed `clamp()` values.

All rem values are authored for a 16 px browser root.  When
`rootFontSize` differs from 16 every rem value is scaled by
`rootFontSize / 16` so the rendered pixel size grows
proportionally (e.g. `rootFontSize: 24` → 1.5× larger).

### Properties emitted

| Pattern                      | Count | Description                     |
| ---------------------------- | ----: | ------------------------------- |
| `--esp-type-{step}`          |     7 | Type-scale steps                |
| `--esp-size-{step}`          |     7 | Spacing-scale steps             |
| `--esp-size-{lo}-to-{hi}`    |     6 | Cross-step fluid sizes          |
| `--esp-size-font`            |     1 | Alias → `--esp-type-normal`     |
| `--esp-size-padding`         |     1 | Alias → `--esp-size-tiny-to-small` |
| `--esp-size-padding-page`    |     1 | Alias → `--esp-size-small-to-normal` |
| `--esp-size-border-radius`   |     1 | Static rem value                |

declarations.name: DEFAULT_SEMANTIC_MAPPINGS
declarations.default: { background: { source: "primary", lightness: "surface" }, layer1: { source: "primary", lightness: "raised1" }, layer2: { source: "primary", lightness: "raised2" }, layer3: { source: "primary", lightness: "raised3" }, layer4: { source: "primary", lightness: "raised4" }, actionBackground: { source: "primary", lightness: "raised3" }, actionText: { source: "primary", lightness: "ink" }, border: { source: "primary", lightness: "border" }, shadow: { source: "primary", lightness: "shadow" }, text: { source: "primary", lightness: "text" }, dangerText: { source: "danger", lightness: "text" }, headings: { source: "primary", lightness: "muted" }, headingsHover: { source: "primary", lightness: "text" }, link: { source: "complementary", lightness: "accent" }, linkHover: { source: "complementary", lightness: "text" }, linkHoverBg: { source: "triadic-left", lightness: "raised2" }, inputCaret: { source: "triadic-right", lightness: "ink" }, inputSelection: { source: "complementary", lightness: "text" }, inputSelectionBg: { source: "triadic-left", lightness: "raised2" }, }

declarations.name: parseTheme

parameters.name: base64
parameters.description: A Base64-encoded JSON string.

declarations.description:
Decode a Base64-encoded JSON string into a PartialTheme.

Returns `null` if decoding or JSON parsing fails — never throws.

Unsafe property names (`__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`)
are stripped during parsing via a JSON reviver to prevent
prototype pollution from untrusted encoded themes.

declarations.name: encodeTheme

parameters.name: partial
parameters.description: The partial theme to encode.

declarations.description:
Encode a PartialTheme as a Base64 JSON string.

The inverse of parseTheme.

declarations.name: mergeTheme

parameters.name: defaults
parameters.description: The fully-resolved base theme.

parameters.name: overrides
parameters.description: User-supplied partial overrides.

declarations.description:
Deep-merge a PartialTheme over a set of defaults.

Primitive fields are replaced; nested objects (`angles`,
`semanticHues`, `lightness`, `chroma`, `semanticMappings`)
are merged key-by-key.  Arrays (`stylesheets`) are replaced
wholesale.

declarations.name: validateTheme

parameters.name: base64
parameters.description:
A Base64-encoded JSON string representing a
partial or full theme.

declarations.description:
Validate a Base64-encoded theme string.

Checks structure, types, and value ranges.  The function is
intentionally lenient on unknown keys (future-proofing) but
strict on the keys it recognises.

declarations.name: NESTED_THEME_KEYS
declarations.default: [ "angles", "chroma", "lightness", "semanticHues", "semanticMappings", "variantChroma", ]
declarations.description:
Keys of EspalierTheme whose values are nested objects
and therefore need key-by-key merging instead of wholesale
replacement when combining two PartialTheme objects.

declarations.name: mergePartials

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: The base partial theme.

parameters.name: override
parameters.description: Partial overrides to apply on top.

declarations.description:
Deep-merge two PartialTheme objects.

Top-level primitive fields are replaced by the override when the
override value is not `undefined`.  Override keys that are
explicitly `undefined` are skipped so they don't erase
previously-layered values.

Nested-object fields listed in NESTED_THEME_KEYS are
merged key-by-key (also skipping `undefined` inner values) so
that the override adds to (rather than replaces) the base.

This differs from mergeTheme, which merges a
PartialTheme over a fully-resolved EspalierTheme.
`mergePartials` keeps the result partial — useful when layering
multiple partial overrides before resolving against defaults.

declarations.name: layerThemes

parameters.name: encodedThemes
parameters.description: Variadic encoded theme strings.

declarations.description:
Layer multiple encoded theme strings into a single encoded result.

Each argument is a Base64-encoded JSON PartialTheme (as
produced by encodeTheme).  They are parsed, merged left
to right via mergePartials, and re-encoded.

Entries that are `null`, `undefined`, empty strings, or strings
that fail to decode/parse are silently skipped, making it safe
to pass optional or potentially-invalid theme overrides without
conditional logic.

declarations.name: VALIDITY_CHANGED_EVENT
declarations.default: "validity-changed"
declarations.description: Event name dispatched by `FormFieldController` on every validation pass.

A range slider for selecting numeric values. Built entirely from
custom elements with ARIA `role="slider"` — no native
`<input type="range">` — for consistent cross-browser styling and
intentional touch interaction.

On touch devices, only dragging the thumb changes the value.
Tapping the track is ignored so the user can scroll the page
without accidentally adjusting the slider. On desktop, clicking
anywhere on the track jumps to that value and begins a drag.

The slider accepts an accessible name from two sources, checked
in priority order:

1. Slot content — `<esp-slider>Volume</esp-slider>` renders a
   visible label and names the slider via `aria-labelledby`.
2. The `label` attribute — `<esp-slider label="Volume">` applies
   an invisible `aria-label` when no slot content is present.

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-slider min="0" max="10" step="0.5" value="5">
    Rating
  </esp-slider>
  <esp-slider disabled min="0" max="100" value="30"
    label="Disabled slider">
  </esp-slider>
</esp-box>
```

members.name: formCtrl
members.default: new FormFieldController({ host: this, internals: this.internals, getFormValue: () => this.value || null, // A range slider always has a normalized numeric value (clamped // to [min, max] and snapped to step), so there is no // representable "empty" state that could trigger valueMissing. // Validity is always satisfied. getValidity: () => null, onReset: () => { this.value = String(this._low); }, onRestore: (state: string) => { this.value = state; }, onDisabled: (isDisabled: boolean) => { this.disabled = isDisabled; }, })

members.name: max
members.description: Maximum slider value.

members.name: step
members.description: Step increment. Values snap to the nearest step.

members.name: value
members.description:
Current slider value as a string for form compatibility.
Always kept in sync with the rendered state: clamped to
`[min, max]` and snapped to `step` whenever the value, min,
max, or step properties change.

members.name: name

members.name: disabled
members.description: Controls whether the slider is disabled.

members.name: label
members.description:
Accessible label for the slider. Used as `aria-label` on the
thumb when no slot content is present. When the default slot
has content, `aria-labelledby` takes precedence and this
property is ignored.

members.name: _nextId
members.description: Module-level counter for generating unique label ids.

members.name: _labelId
members.description: Stable id for the label element, used by `aria-labelledby`.

members.name: _safeStep
members.description:
The effective step, guaranteed to be a finite positive number.
Falls back to `1` when the `step` property is zero, negative,
`NaN`, or `Infinity`.

members.name: _safeMin
members.description:
Effective minimum, guarded against invalid attribute/property
values so slider math, ARIA, and form values never expose NaN.

members.name: _safeMax
members.description:
Effective maximum, guarded against invalid attribute/property
values so slider math, ARIA, and form values never expose NaN.

members.name: _stepPrecision
members.description:
Number of decimal digits implied by the effective step. Used
to round away floating-point noise so the public value stays
clean (e.g. `"0.3"` instead of `"0.30000000000000004"`).

Handles scientific notation: `0.0000001` stringifies as
`"1e-7"` (7 digits), and `0.00000012` stringifies as
`"1.2e-7"` (8 digits = 1 mantissa fractional digit + 7
exponent).

members.name: _low
members.description: Effective lower bound, safe when `min > max`.

members.name: _high
members.description: Effective upper bound, safe when `min > max`.

members.name: _snapToStep
members.description:
Snap `raw` to the nearest step relative to `_low`, clamp
to `[_low, _high]`, and round to the step's decimal precision.

members.name: numericValue
members.description: The value parsed, clamped, and snapped to step.

members.name: percentage
members.description: Fill/thumb position as a percentage of the track width.

members.name: _normalizeValue
members.description:
Normalize `this.value` so it always reflects the
clamped-and-stepped numeric value, and push the result into
`ElementInternals` without firing `value-changed`.

Called from `willUpdate` whenever `value`, `min`, `max`, or
`step` change.

members.name: focus
members.description: Focus the slider thumb.

members.name: validate
members.description: Re-run constraint validation and dispatch `validity-changed`.

members.name: checkValidity
members.description: Check whether the current state is valid (delegates to ElementInternals).

members.name: _handleLostPointerCapture
members.description:
Handles `lostpointercapture`, which fires when the browser
revokes capture due to an OS gesture, modal interrupt, or
the pointer leaving the document. This prevents the slider
from getting stuck in the dragging state.

members.name: _syncSlotContent
members.description:
Inspect the default slot's assigned nodes and update
`_hasSlotContent`. Called from both `firstUpdated` (to catch
initial light-DOM content before `slotchange` fires) and the
`slotchange` handler (for dynamic changes).

members.name: formResetCallback
members.description: Called by the browser when the owning `<form>` is reset.

members.name: formStateRestoreCallback
members.description: Called by the browser to restore form state (bfcache, etc.).

members.name: formDisabledCallback
members.description: Called by the browser when a parent `<fieldset>` is enabled or disabled.

members.name: variantTokenOriginalValues
members.default: new Map< string, { generatedPriority: string; generatedValue: string; originalPriority: string; originalValue: string | null; } >()

members.name: scheme
members.description:
The scheme to use for the component. This property is used internally
to control the light/dark scheme so it matches the scheme of
[esp-root](/components/root). It should not be set manually, it
exists as an attribute for styling purposes.

members.description: The [color variant](/guides/color/variants) of the element.

members.description:

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

declarations.description:
A toggle switch for boolean on/off values. Uses a CSS-only
track-and-thumb design with a smooth sliding transition.
Functionally equivalent to a checkbox but visually communicates
an immediate state change rather than a deferred selection.

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-form-item label="Notifications">
    <esp-switch value="notifications">
      Enable notifications
    </esp-switch>
  </esp-form-item>
</esp-box>
```

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-switch checked value="dark-mode">
    Dark mode
  </esp-switch>
  <esp-switch disabled value="disabled-opt">
    Disabled option
  </esp-switch>
</esp-box>
```

### Text mode

Set `mode="text"` with `off-label` and `on-label` to render a
two-option text toggle instead of a track and thumb. This is
ideal for binary choices like a billing-period selector:

```html
<esp-box>
  <esp-switch
    mode="text"
    off-label="Monthly"
    on-label="Annual"
    value="annual"
  >Billing period</esp-switch>
</esp-box>
```

The slotted text is visually hidden in text mode and folded
into the control's accessible label together with the current
selected option. Screen readers will announce both the label
and the active choice while the `off-label` / `on-label`
text is presented visually.

members.default: new FormFieldController({ host: this, internals: this.internals, getFormValue: () => (this.checked ? this.value || "on" : null), getValidity: () => { if (this.required && !this.checked) { return { flags: { valueMissing: true }, message: this.requiredMessage || "Please toggle this switch to continue.", }; } return null; }, onReset: () => { this.checked = false; }, onRestore: (state: string) => { this.checked = state === "on" || state === this.value; }, onDisabled: (isDisabled: boolean) => { this.disabled = isDisabled; }, })

declarations.description:
A compact, keyboard-accessible status icon that reveals explanatory content in an `esp-popover` on hover, focus, or tap. ```html The image is still queued. It will retry automatically. This page is live and visible to visitors. ``` ### Chrome modes The `chrome` attribute controls the chip treatment around the icon. `pill` (the default) renders the filled circular background, `outline` keeps only the border, and `none` drops both — leaving just the variant-colored icon and its popover affordance. `chrome="none"` is the right choice when the indicator sits inside dense metadata rows where a full pill would feel heavy. ```html Pill chrome wraps the icon in a filled circle. Outline chrome keeps the border but drops the fill. None drops the chrome entirely — just the variant-colored icon. ``` ### Wide chip with a non-square SVG The built-in `icon` attribute pulls from the Espalier sprite, which is authored for **square** glyphs at the chip's default size. When you need a non-square mark — a file-type badge, a short token of text, a pill of dimensions — slot your own SVG into the `icon` slot and size the chip through the three CSS hooks: `--esp-status-indicator-width`, `--esp-status-indicator-height`, and `--esp-status-indicator-border-radius`. The slotted SVG fills the chip, so give it a matching `viewBox` and use `chrome="none"` if you don't want the chip's pill background fighting your art. ```html Original file type

Uploaded as a JPEG; responsive variants are generated on demand.

 ``` ### Rich popover content The default slot accepts any HTML, not just plain text. Small blocks of structured content — a heading, a list of values, a definition list — render inside the popover and are clamped by `--esp-status-indicator-popover-max-width` (default `32ch`). Raise the hook when the content needs more room. ```html Responsive layers ready
  • 320w — mobile
  • 640w — tablet
  • 1280w — desktop
  • 1920w — large desktop
 ```

A tabbed interface that groups multiple [esp-tab](/components/tabs) elements. The tab strip is generated automatically from each child's `label` attribute. Only one tab panel is visible at a time. ```html

Overview content goes here.

Detailed information lives here.

Configuration options.

 ``` ### Pre-selected tab Set `active` on a child `esp-tab` to make it the initially visible panel: ```html

Alpha panel.

Beta starts active.

 ``` ### Disabled tab Individual tabs can be disabled: ```html

You can select this tab.

This tab cannot be reached.

This tab works too.

 ``` ### Disabled group Set `disabled` on the group to disable all tabs at once: ```html

All tabs are disabled.

Cannot switch to this.

 ``` ### Color variants ```html

Complementary variant content.

More complementary content.

 ``` ### Listening for tab changes ```html

First panel.

Second panel.

 ```

members.name: textContrast

type.text:
Partial<
    Record<SemanticColorName, { bg: SemanticColorName; targetLc: number }>
  >

members.default: { text: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, dangerText: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, headings: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, headingsHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 60 }, link: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, linkHover: { bg: "background", targetLc: 75 }, actionText: { bg: "actionBackground", targetLc: 75 }, inputCaret: { bg: "layer2", targetLc: 60 }, inputSelection: { bg: "inputSelectionBg", targetLc: 60 }, }
members.description:
Text tokens that require APCA contrast enforcement.

Each entry maps a text-bearing semantic token to:
  - `bg`: the semantic token it sits on (reference color)
  - `targetLc`: the minimum |Lc| the text must achieve

members.name: statusVariantSources
members.description:
Variant color sources whose meaning is carried by a fixed hue rather
than the brand seed — the destructive / positive / warning status
families (ADR-004). For these variants the filled-action tokens are
pinned to the variant's own color so a theme cannot make them look
like an ordinary brand action. See task 098.

members.name: semanticActionTokens
members.description:
Semantic tokens that paint a control's filled action surface. For a
status variant these always derive from the variant's own color, so
the Taproot "Buttons" semantic group (which re-points
`actionBackground` / `actionText` to a brand source) cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. See
task 098.

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource
members.description:
Resolve the color source used to derive a semantic token for the
active variant.

Normally this is the theme's mapping source, so the variant performs a
full palette shift relative to its own color. The exception is the
meaning-bearing status variants (`danger`, `success`, `warning`): their
filled-action tokens (`actionBackground`, `actionText`) are pinned to
the variant's own color — the `primary` entry of the sub-variant
palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

members.name: fieldName
members.description:
A string value used for the id of the textarea.

```html
<esp-textarea field-name="comments"></esp-textarea>
```

members.name: value
members.description:
The value of the textarea.

```html
<esp-textarea value="Some text"></esp-textarea>
```

members.name: name
members.description:
The name used when the textarea participates in a `<form>`.

```html
<form>
  <esp-textarea name="comments" value="Hello"></esp-textarea>
</form>
```

members.name: required
members.description:
When true, the field must have a value before the form can be
submitted.

```html
<esp-textarea required></esp-textarea>
```

members.name: requiredMessage
members.description:
A custom message to display when the field is required but empty.
Defaults to `"Please fill out this field."` when not set.

```html
<esp-textarea required required-message="Comments are required!"></esp-textarea>
```

members.name: validationMessage
members.description:
A custom message to display when validation fails (e.g.
minlength not met). Overrides the default friendly message.

```html
<esp-textarea minlength="10"
  validation-message="Please write at least 10 characters.">
</esp-textarea>
```

members.name: disabled
members.description:
When true, the textarea is disabled and cannot be
interacted with.

```html
<esp-textarea disabled></esp-textarea>
```

members.name: placeholder
members.description:
Placeholder text shown when the textarea is empty.

```html
<esp-textarea placeholder="Enter your message..."></esp-textarea>
```

members.name: rows
members.description:
The visible number of text lines.

```html
<esp-textarea rows="6"></esp-textarea>
```

members.name: cols
members.description:
The visible width in average character widths.

```html
<esp-textarea cols="40"></esp-textarea>
```

members.name: minlength
members.description: Minimum number of characters.

members.name: maxlength
members.description: Maximum number of characters.

members.name: _syncInput
members.description:
Sync the form value and fire `value-changed`. If the field
is already showing an error, validate eagerly so the error
clears immediately when the user fixes it. Otherwise just
sync silently — errors will appear on blur.

members.name: connected
members.description:
Every connected toaster, in connection order. `esp-page`
auto-injects a toaster, so a page nesting several `esp-page`s has
several toasters. One shared bus subscription feeds one shared
toast queue, and a single **renderer** — re-chosen whenever the
roster or the queue changes, preferring a visible instance —
displays the whole queue. Keeping the queue shared (rather than
per-instance) means a routing change migrates the entire live
stack to the new renderer instead of fragmenting it into
overlapping per-toaster stacks.

members.name: queue
members.description: All live toasts, shared across every instance.

members.name: toasts
members.description:
The shared queue slice this instance currently displays: the full
queue for the chosen renderer, empty for everyone else.

members.name: resizeObserver
members.description:
Re-routes live toasts when this toaster is collapsed or expanded
with no accompanying queue or roster change — e.g. a persistent
toast whose renderer's panel goes `display: none`. Collapsing an
ancestor resizes the host to zero, which fires this observer.
(`visibility`/`opacity` flips produce no resize; those re-route on
the next queue or roster event.)

members.name: dismiss
members.description:
Remove a toast from the shared queue exactly once — the timer and
the manual destroy button can both race to dismiss it.

members.name: syncRenderer
members.description:
Choose the renderer — the first connected instance that reports
visible, or the first connected one if none do — and hand it the
whole shared queue; every other instance renders nothing. Run on
every queue, roster, or renderer-size change, so the stack follows
visibility and never splits across toasters.

palette, which is the variant color itself — so a theme that re-points
the Buttons semantic group to a brand source cannot rotate a
destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

members.name: text
members.description:
Plain-text content to display in the tooltip. For richer
HTML, use a `<template>` child element instead.

members.name: attach
members.description: Which side of the trigger the tooltip appears on.

members.name: align
members.description: How the tooltip is aligned relative to the trigger.

members.name: offset
members.description: Distance between the trigger and the tooltip.

members.name: showDelay
members.description: Delay in milliseconds before showing the tooltip.

members.name: hideDelay
members.description: Delay in milliseconds before hiding the tooltip.

members.name: triggerTabindex
members.description:
Tabindex applied to the tooltip trigger span.

Leave it unset to get the right default automatically: a tooltip
wrapping a focusable button (`<button>` or `<esp-button>`) takes its
wrapper span out of the tab order (`-1`) so it does not add a second,
redundant tab stop, while a tooltip wrapping non-interactive content
(an icon, an abbreviation, plain text) keeps the wrapper focusable
(`0`) so keyboard users can still reveal it on focus.

Set it explicitly to override that auto-detection — for example
`trigger-tabindex="0"` to force a focusable wrapper, or
`trigger-tabindex="-1"` to opt a non-button trigger out. An explicit
value always wins; a non-numeric or `NaN` value falls back to `0`.

When the wrapper leaves the tab order, keyboard focus lands on the
slotted control instead, so the tooltip's `aria-describedby` is
mirrored onto that control and assistive tech still announces the
description on focus.

members.name: triggerTabindexExplicit
members.description:
True once `trigger-tabindex` is set explicitly — whether via the attribute,
a JS property assignment, or a parent such as `esp-button-group` in toolbar
mode. An explicit value takes precedence over the button-child
auto-detection. Cleared when the attribute/property is removed (Lit sets
the property to `null`) so auto-detection takes over again.

members.name: effectiveTriggerTabindex
members.description:
The tabindex actually rendered on the wrapper span. An explicit
`trigger-tabindex` wins (the setter already normalized any non-finite
value to `0`); otherwise it is derived from the trigger — `-1` for a
focusable button child that does not need the wrapper as a tab stop, `0`
otherwise.

members.name: hasButtonTrigger
members.description: Whether the slotted trigger is a focusable button control.

members.name: handleSlotChange
members.description:
The slotted trigger drives both `effectiveTriggerTabindex` (via
`hasButtonTrigger`) and `aria-describedby` mirroring, so re-evaluate both
when the slotted content changes.

members.name: syncTriggerDescribedBy
members.description:
When the wrapper span is taken out of the tab order
(`triggerTabindex < 0`), keyboard focus lands on the slotted control
instead of the wrapper. Mirror the tooltip's `aria-describedby` onto
that control so assistive tech still announces the description; clear
it again if the wrapper is refocusable. Any `aria-describedby` the
consumer already set on the control is preserved.

The scheme to use for the component. This property is used internally
to control the light/dark scheme so it matches the scheme of
[esp-root](/components/root). It should not be set manually, it
exists as an attribute for styling purposes.

members.name: variant
members.description: The [color variant](/guides/color/variants) of the element.

members.name: traverseToClosest

parameters.description: The selector of the element to look for

members.description:
Traverse up the DOM tree to find the closest element that
matches the selector. This method is aware of shadow DOM

members.name: semanticActionTokens

members.name: effectiveVariantTokenSource

destructive / warning / positive action off its semantic hue. Ordinary
brand and primary variants keep full theme customization. See task 098.

declarations.description:
A declarative node for a static [`<esp-tree>`](/components/tree).

`esp-tree-item` is a lightweight, **declarative data carrier**. Nest
items to describe a static hierarchy; the parent `esp-tree` parses
these elements into its internal model and renders the whole tree
centrally (a single ARIA tree with one roving tab stop). The item
elements themselves are not rendered visually — this keeps the
keyboard and accessibility model identical whether a tree is built
from slotted items or from the data-driven `nodes` property.

Each item contributes one **web-path segment** to the selected node's
path. A node's submitted value is the `/`-joined path of its segments
(e.g. `weddings/2026`). Set the standard `id` attribute when a static
node needs a host-owned stable identity; otherwise the parent tree
derives one for the lifetime of the element instance.

```html
<esp-tree name="folder">
  <esp-tree-item segment="weddings" label="Weddings">
    <esp-tree-item segment="2025" label="2025"></esp-tree-item>
    <esp-tree-item segment="2026" label="2026"></esp-tree-item>
  </esp-tree-item>
  <esp-tree-item segment="portraits" label="Portraits"></esp-tree-item>
</esp-tree>
```

To express a non-selectable grouping node (a header that can be
expanded but not chosen), set `selectable="false"`. To mark a branch
that lazy-loads its children through the tree's `loadChildren`
callback, add the `has-children` attribute. Set `icon` to show an
Espalier sprite icon for the rendered node, and `editable` to expose
the tree's trailing edit affordance. The action flags (`deletable`,
`movable-up`, `movable-down`, `cuttable`, and `paste-child-target`)
expose the parent tree's built-in node action events. Assign arbitrary
host-owned payloads to the `data` property; there is no `data`
attribute because attribute values cannot reliably carry object
identity.

declarations.name: normalizeSegment
declarations.description:
Normalize free text to a conservative, offline-validatable web-path
segment: lowercase ASCII `[a-z0-9-]`, runs of any other character
collapsed to a single hyphen, with leading/trailing hyphens trimmed.

This is a deliberately conservative subset. The server's slugifier
remains the source of truth at save time; this only keeps users from
building an obviously invalid path offline.

A form-associated hierarchy primitive for displaying and selecting
from a slug-structured tree — a folder/path picker, a category
chooser, or any place a flat field should become an expandable tree.
For static declarative node definitions, see
[`<esp-tree-item>`](/components/tree/item/).

The name is apt: an espalier is a tree trained flat against a frame,
so the visual language — branches trained to vertical guide wires,
grafting a new shoot onto an existing limb — is the component's own
metaphor.

## Data-driven first, composition-capable

Bind a hierarchy to the `nodes` property for the primary, data-driven
API. For static trees, nest
[`<esp-tree-item>`](/components/tree/item) children instead — they are
parsed into the same model, so the keyboard and accessibility model is
identical either way. When both are supplied, `nodes` wins. Data-driven
nodes must provide stable, unique `id` values; the tree keys expansion,
lazy loading, focus, and host re-feeds by `id` so relabeling or moving a
node can change its path without changing its identity.

```html
<esp-form-item label="Folder">
  <esp-tree name="folder" required></esp-tree>
</esp-form-item>
<script>
  const tree = findByTagName("esp-tree")[0];
  tree.nodes = [
    { id: "weddings", segment: "weddings", label: "Weddings", children: [
      { id: "w2026", segment: "2026", label: "2026" },
    ] },
    { id: "portraits", segment: "portraits", label: "Portraits" },
  ];
</script>
```

Nodes may also carry opaque host data. The tree never reads into `data`;
it carries the value through select, edit, toggle, and graft events so a
host can round-trip its own model without a side lookup.

```html
<esp-tree id="navigation"></esp-tree>
<script>
  const tree = findById("navigation");
  tree.nodes = [
    {
      id: "nav-about",
      segment: "about",
      label: "About Us",
      data: { target: { kind: "page", pageId: "about-us" } },
      editable: true,
    },
  ];
  tree.addEventListener("esp-tree-select", (event) => {
    console.log(event.detail.node.id, event.detail.node.data);
  });
</script>
```

## Selection and form value

Single-select for v1. Clicking a selectable row selects it; selecting
a branch opens it, and clicking an already-selected branch toggles it
open or closed. Selecting a row outside the open selected branch
collapses that branch unless `keep-expanded` is set. Branches can also
expand and collapse through the leading icon button or with keyboard:
`ArrowRight` expands closed branches first, then focuses a row's
trailing action button when one is present; `ArrowLeft` collapses or
moves to the parent. `Enter` and `Space` select the focused row. The
submitted form value is the selected node's full path
(defaulting to root-inclusive web paths like `/weddings/2026`), or
`null` when nothing is selected. The root row is always present,
defaults to label and path `/`, and is selectable by default; set
`root-selectable="false"` when the root should be navigation-only.
Validity reflects the committed selection through the standard
Espalier form flow.

## Lazy loading

Mark a branch with `hasChildren` and set the `loadChildren` callback to
fetch its children on first expand. Expansion state is stable across
re-renders and reloads.

```html
<esp-tree id="lazy"></esp-tree>
<script>
  const tree = findById("lazy");
  tree.nodes = [{ id: "root", segment: "library", label: "Library", hasChildren: true }];
  tree.loadChildren = async () => {
    const res = await fetch("/assets/data/tree-library-children.json");
    return await res.json();
  };
</script>
```

## Grafting a new branch

Set `allow-graft` to expose inline `+` affordances at the level where
a new child would appear. Clicking one opens the built-in graft
dialog, which shows the parent and resulting path while the value is
normalized to a valid web-path segment. Enter commits, Escape
cancels. Keyboard users can press `Insert` on the focused row to add
beneath it. Newly grafted branches are ephemeral client state the
host reads back via events — the component persists nothing.

```html
<esp-tree id="graftable" allow-graft></esp-tree>
<script>
  const tree = findById("graftable");
  tree.nodes = [
    { id: "weddings", segment: "weddings", label: "Weddings", children: [
      { id: "w2026", segment: "2026", label: "2026" },
    ] },
    { id: "portraits", segment: "portraits", label: "Portraits" },
  ];
  tree.addEventListener("esp-tree-graft", (e) => {
    console.log("grafted", e.detail.path);
  });
</script>
```

As the input changes, the tree emits a live `esp-tree-graft-input`
event. A consumer can run an async check the offline component cannot
(most importantly a server-side duplicate not present in the loaded
children) and push the result back with `setGraftError(message)`; the
tree then shows it inline and blocks the commit until it is cleared.
For advanced add flows, listen for the cancelable
`esp-tree-graft-request` event and call `preventDefault()` to skip
the default dialog.

Replace the `buildPath` property to use non-web path semantics, and
replace `normalizeGraftSegment` to loosen, tighten, or completely
skip the default web-slug normalization used by the graft dialog.

## Node actions

Mark a node with `editable: true` to render an edit action on the
trailing side of the row. Clicking it, or pressing `F2` on the
focused row, emits `esp-tree-edit` with the node path and public node
data so the host can open its own editor. Optional flags
(`deletable`, `movableUp`, `movableDown`, `cuttable`, and
`pasteChildTarget`) expose the matching built-in editing actions for
host-owned tree workflows.

If a row has exactly one action, the tree renders one trailing
icon-only `esp-button` with an accessible label such as `Edit About`.
If a row has more than one action, the actions collapse into one
trailing `esp-action-menu` trigger so rows never accumulate multiple
visible action buttons. The tree only emits events — it does not
delete, move, cut, paste, mutate source nodes, or own clipboard state.

```html
<esp-tree id="navigation"></esp-tree>
<script>
  const tree = findById("navigation");
  tree.nodes = [
    {
      id: "about",
      segment: "about",
      label: "About",
      editable: true,
      deletable: true,
      movableDown: true,
      cuttable: true,
      children: [
        {
          id: "team",
          segment: "team",
          label: "Team",
          editable: true,
        },
      ],
    },
    {
      id: "services",
      segment: "services",
      label: "Services",
      pasteChildTarget: true,
    },
  ];
  tree.addEventListener("esp-tree-cut", (event) => {
    console.log("cut requested", event.detail.path);
  });
  tree.addEventListener("esp-tree-paste-child", (event) => {
    console.log("paste as child requested", event.detail.path);
  });
</script>
```

## Expand/collapse icon

The expand/collapse caret ships inline with the component, so unlike
node `icon`s it needs no icon sprite to be hosted. Override it by
setting the `expandIcon` property to your own (trusted) SVG markup; it
renders on every branch toggle and is rotated 90° when the branch is
open, so provide a right-pointing icon. For icon pairs that should not
rotate, set `collapseIcon` to the trusted SVG markup to use while the
branch is open.

```html
<esp-tree id="custom-caret"></esp-tree>
<script>
  const tree = findById("custom-caret");
  tree.nodes = [
    { id: "weddings", segment: "weddings", label: "Weddings", children: [
      { id: "w2026", segment: "2026", label: "2026" },
    ] },
  ];
  // A filled triangle caret instead of the default outline chevron.
  tree.expandIcon = `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24"
    fill="currentColor" stroke="none"><path d="M9 6l6 6l-6 6z" /></svg>`;
</script>
```

members.name: nodes
members.description:
The hierarchy to display, as the primary data-driven API. When
non-empty this takes precedence over slotted `esp-tree-item`
children.

members.name: value
members.description:
The selected node's full path (segments joined by `/`), or the
empty string when nothing is selected. This is also the form
submission value.

members.name: name
members.description: The name used when the tree participates in a `<form>`.

members.name: required
members.description: When true, a selection must be made before the form can submit.

members.name: requiredMessage
members.description:
A custom message shown when the tree is required but nothing is
selected. Defaults to `"Please select an option."`.

members.name: disabled
members.description: When true, the tree is disabled and cannot be interacted with.

members.name: allowGraft
members.description:
When true, an inline `+` affordance is shown on every node and at
the root for grafting a new child branch.

members.name: rootLabel
members.description: Label shown for the always-rendered root ancestor row.

members.name: rootPath
members.description: Path submitted when the root row is selected. Defaults to `/`.

members.name: rootSelectable
members.description:
Whether the root row can be selected. Defaults to true. Set
`root-selectable="false"` to make it navigation-only.

members.name: label
members.description:
An accessible name for the tree. Usually unnecessary inside an
`esp-form-item`, which provides the label; set it for standalone
use.

members.name: graftError
members.description:
A consumer-supplied error for the in-progress graft, shown inline
and blocking commit until cleared. Set it with `setGraftError`,
typically in response to an `esp-tree-graft-input` event after an
async check. Mirrored as the `graft-error` attribute.

members.name: loadChildren
members.description:
Async callback to lazy-load the children of a branch marked
`hasChildren`, on first expand.

members.name: buildPath
members.description:
Builds a child path from a parent path and segment. Defaults to a
web URL style builder (`/parent/child`) and can be replaced for
filesystem or application-specific path semantics.

members.name: normalizeGraftSegment
members.description:
Normalizes graft input into the segment passed to `buildPath`.
Defaults to `normalizeSegment`; replace it to allow a different
character set or no client-side normalization.

members.name: keepExpanded
members.description:
Keep already-expanded branches open when selecting a row outside of
the selected branch. By default, selecting outside an open selected
branch collapses that branch.

members.name: expandIcon
members.description:
Raw SVG markup for the expand/collapse caret. Defaults to an
inline Tabler-style caret, so the tree ships its own icon and the
host does not need to provide an icon sprite for it. Leave it empty
to use the built-in caret, or set it to trusted SVG markup to use a
different icon. It is rendered on every branch's toggle button and
rotated 90° when the branch is expanded unless `collapseIcon` is
set, so supply a right-pointing icon.

members.name: collapseIcon
members.description:
Optional raw SVG markup shown while the branch is open (expanded),
in place of rotating `expandIcon`. This allows non-rotatable glyph
pairs such as `+` and `-`. Leave unset to preserve the default
rotate-90° behavior.

members.name: _model
members.description: Resolved render model, rebuilt when the source data changes.

members.name: _slottedNodes
members.description: Nodes parsed from slotted `esp-tree-item` children.

members.name: _lazyLoaded
members.description: Lazy-loaded children, keyed by parent id, kept across rebuilds.

members.name: _grafted
members.description: Ephemeral grafted children, keyed by parent id.

members.name: _expanded
members.description: Expanded branch ids.

members.name: _loading
members.description: Node ids with an in-flight `loadChildren`, to guard double-loads.

members.name: _generation
members.description:
Bumped whenever the source data is replaced, to invalidate the
results of loads that were in flight against the old data.

members.name: _defaultValue
members.description: The value captured at first render, restored on form reset.

members.name: _pendingFocus
members.description: Whether the next render should move focus to the active row.

members.name: _pendingScrollId
members.description: Row or branch id that should be kept in view after render.

members.name: _pendingGraftFocus
members.description: Whether the next render should focus the graft input.

members.name: _activeId
members.description: The roving-tabindex focus target.

members.name: _selectedId
members.description: Selected node identity; `value` remains the selected node's path.

members.name: _graftParentId
members.description: The parent id whose graft input is open, or null when none.

members.name: _graftErrorInternal

members.name: formResetCallback
members.description: Called by the browser when the owning `<form>` is reset.

members.name: formStateRestoreCallback
members.description: Called by the browser to restore form state (bfcache, etc.).

members.name: formDisabledCallback
members.description: Called by the browser when a parent `<fieldset>` is en/disabled.

members.name: validate
members.description: Re-run constraint validation and dispatch `validity-changed`.

members.name: checkValidity
members.description: Check whether the current state is valid.

members.name: focus
members.description: Move focus to the active tree row.

members.name: setGraftError
members.description:
Push a consumer-supplied error for the in-progress graft (or clear
it with `null`). While set, the error is shown inline and the graft
commit is blocked. Call this in response to an `esp-tree-graft-input`
event after an async server check.

members.name: _reconcileState
members.description: Drop expansion/active state for ids that no longer exist.

members.name: _revealSelectedValue
members.description:
Expand the ancestors of the selected value so its row is visible. Walks
up the parent chain via the row index; a value whose row is absent (no
selection, or a lazy branch not yet loaded) is a no-op. Only opens
ancestors — it never collapses a branch or changes the selection, so it
composes with manual expand/collapse and `keepExpanded`.

members.name: _select
members.description: Select a node. No-op for non-selectable nodes or a no-op re-select.

members.name: _onRowClick
members.description:
Clicking a row selects that row when it is selectable. Branch
expansion opens on first selection, then toggles when the selected
branch itself is clicked again.

members.name: _onToggleClick
members.description: The leading icon action toggles branch expansion.

members.name: _edit
members.description: Invoke an editable node's host-owned edit flow.

members.name: variantTokenOriginalValues
